23	
24	- [ ] **[M] Three Kingdoms Banqi (三國暗棋) implementation** — Build BoardShape::ThreeKingdom mask + rules/three_kingdom.rs with 3-side capture rules. Architecture supports it (TurnOrder, Side, BoardShape variant exist); rules logic and home-zone layout need spec from canonical source. → [research](backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md)
25	- [ ] **[S] Wire DARK_CHAIN house rule** — Chain captures pass through face-down squares; auto-reveal as chain progresses. Requires changing ChainHop.captured to Option<Piece> and updating sanitize_for_observer for the network ABI. See docs/rules/banqi-house.md.
26	- [ ] **[S] Wire HORSE_DIAGONAL house rule** — Banqi horse moves like xiangqi horse (L-shape with 馬腿 leg block) when flag enabled. Reuse pattern from rules/xiangqi.rs::gen_horse.
27	- [ ] **[S] Wire CANNON_FAST_MOVE house rule** — Banqi cannon non-capturing slide goes any number of squares (capture still requires the one-piece jump). Reuse the ray walk from rules/banqi.rs::gen_chariot_rush.
28	- [ ] **[M] Threefold repetition draw detection** — Add Zobrist hashing on Board + (side_to_move) and a position-count map. Update on make_move/unmake_move. Triggers GameStatus::Drawn when count >= rules.draw_policy.repetition_threshold. See state/repetition.rs (currently a stub).
29	
30	## P2
31	
32	Worth doing, no rush.
33	
34	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: in-game rule editor (gear icon → modal)** — Picker-side form already encodes rules into the URL (`?strict=1`, `?house=chain,rush&seed=N`). Add a sidebar gear that opens a pre-filled modal reusing the picker components, navigates to the new query, and starts a fresh game. → [research](backlog/web-ingame-rules-modal.md)
35	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: WS auto-reconnect with backoff** — PR-1 surfaces "Disconnected — refresh" when the WS read pump closes. Add an exponential-backoff retry that reuses the URL+password and resets the `Hello` flow on success. → [research](backlog/web-ws-reconnect.md)
36	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: CJK/ASCII glyph toggle in UI** — `glyph::Style` is already plumbed; expose a sidebar toggle + persist in `localStorage`. Shares no logic with chess-tui's `--style` flag.
37	- [ ] **[M] Promote orient.rs / glyph.rs to a shared client crate** — Both `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs` and `clients/chess-web/src/orient.rs` (likewise `glyph.rs`) are byte-identical. Pull into `clients/chess-client-shared` once a third client appears or the copies first diverge. → [research](backlog/promote-client-shared.md)
38	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: Playwright E2E smoke** — Headless two-tab flow that creates a room, plays a few moves, resigns, and rematches. Cheaper than `wasm-bindgen-test` for full-app smoke. → [research](backlog/web-playwright.md)
39	- [ ] **[M] chess-net: mixed-variant rooms** — Today one server boots with one `--variant` and every auto-created room copies it. Move to per-room `RuleSet` (a chooser inside the `CreateRoom` form). Architecture supports it (`RoomState` already owns its `GameState`); blocks on UX decisions about a variant filter inside the lobby and what default to apply when joining via a URL with no variant hint.
40	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat moderation primitives** — Current chat is a friend-only space (50-line ring buffer, no rate limiting, no mute, no word filter). Add per-player rate limit (~1 msg/sec leaky bucket), client-side `ChatMute` toggle, and an opt-in `--chat-filter <list>` server flag for substring scrubbing. → [research](backlog/chess-net-chat-moderation.md)
41	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat system messages** — `ChatLine.from` is `Side` today so there's no in-channel narration when seats fill / leave / rematch resets. Widen the type to an enum with a `System` variant and emit join/leave/resign system lines. Wire-breaking → bumps protocol to v4. → [research](backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md)
42	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: spectator chat permission per room** — ADR-0006 picked players-only as the default; a streamer flow wants a per-room "spectators may chat" toggle (set on room creation, read by `process_chat`'s gate). Small protocol addition next to room password.
43	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: reconnect-as-same-seat** — Disconnecting frees the seat; the reconnect path currently rebooks at the next free side. Add a one-shot rejoin token (issued in `Hello`, presented as `?token=...` on reconnect) so a flaky link doesn't lose your color.
44	- [ ] **[S] chess-tui: chat scrollback (PgUp/PgDn)** — The chat pane shows the most recent N lines that fit the sidebar; older lines from the 50-line ring buffer are unreachable. Add scroll keys + a "↑ N more" indicator. Pure UI — no protocol change.
45	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: chat polish** — Emoji picker, markdown-lite (bold/italic/code), unread-message badge when the chat panel is scrolled up. Cheap UX wins; none touch the protocol.
46	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: lobby filter + sort** — `/` filter inside the lobby (by id substring or variant); sort by recency / seat count / locked-or-open. Defer until ≥10 concurrent rooms is a real workflow — for a handful of friends the unsorted live list works fine.
47	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: lobby push debouncing** — `notify_lobby` fires on every seat insertion / removal / status flip; back-to-back churn (rematch storms, reconnect retries) can produce a flurry of identical `Rooms` payloads. Coalesce within ≥250ms windows. Premature today; revisit if observable in the wild.
48	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: server-side admin / kick / signed token** — Close stuck rooms or force-disconnect a misbehaving client without restarting the whole server. Wants either an admin-only ws path (`/admin/ws?token=…`) or signed JSON tokens accepted by `/ws/<id>`. Folds into a future TLS / auth pass.
49	- [ ] **[S] WXF notation (相3進5 style)** — Chinese-language traditional xiangqi notation. Pattern lives in crates/chess-core/src/notation/wxf.rs (currently empty). ICCS already implemented; mirror the same encode/decode pair.
50	- [ ] **[M] Time controls (計時器)** — Per-side clocks: main time + increment, byo-yomi, or sudden death. Belongs in chess-net (server-authoritative tick) or as a thin state wrapper if local-only is enough. Adds GameStatus::Won { reason: Timeout } trigger — the WinReason variant already exists. Out of scope: client-side animation; servers tick authoritatively and broadcast remaining time in PlayerView.
51	- [ ] **[S] Takeback (悔棋) request/approve protocol** — Local hot-seat undo already works via state.unmake_move() and the chess-cli 'undo' command. This TODO is the multiplayer flow: requester sends Takeback, opponent approves/rejects, server unmakes the last N plies (typically 1 or 2) and broadcasts updated PlayerView. Lives in chess-net once that crate exists.
52	- [ ] **[S] Handicap mode (讓棋)** — Stronger player removes one or more of their own pieces from the initial setup before play begins (xiangqi tradition: 讓單馬 / 讓雙馬 / 讓單炮 / 讓單車 / 讓雙馬一炮 / 讓雙馬雙炮 / etc). Shape likely: Handicap field on RuleSet (so save/load + replay metadata preserve the choice) consumed by setup::build_xiangqi which skips placing the listed pieces. Xiangqi-only for first cut — banqi's random shuffle complicates the equivalent and can wait until someone asks.
53	
54	## P3
55	
56	Someday / nice-to-have.
57	
58	- [ ] **[L] chess-engine + chess-ai: search and evaluation** — Alpha-beta + iterative deepening + Zobrist transposition for xiangqi. Banqi needs ISMCTS (information-set MCTS) to handle hidden info — research-heavy and a separate problem from xiangqi search. → [research](backlog/chess-ai-search.md)
59	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: move animations** — CSS transitions on piece translate-translate as a `<g transform>` interpolation. Cheap visual polish; doesn't change protocol. Care needed: the SVG renders pieces by display row/col, so animation needs to track piece identity across renders (key-by-square is wrong for moves).
60	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: history scrubber + undo via UI** — Engine already has `unmake_move` and `Replay::play_to`. Need a sidebar timeline that replays positions visually without committing to engine state. Reuse `Replay::from_game` so server replays + client scrubbing share one path.
61	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: mobile layout** — Sidebar collapses below the board on narrow viewports; piece tap target enlarged. Touch input already works via SVG `on:click`; this is purely CSS + viewport meta.
62	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: PWA / offline cache** — Service worker caches `dist/` so local pass-and-play works offline. Online play needs the server, so PWA only helps the local-only flows.
63	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: i18n strings table** — All copy is currently inline English+CJK. Pull into a strings module keyed by locale, switch via `?lang=zh-Hant` or browser locale.
64	- [ ] **[S] chess-web + chess-tui: persisted post-game card with share link** — End-of-game overlay auto-fades after 3 s today; a "save / share this result" affordance would let players link to a replay (engine has `Replay::from_game` ready; need a URL scheme like `/replay/<base64>`). Pure follow-up to the FX overlay shipped 2026-05-07. Web-only first; chess-tui can mirror with a clipboard copy of the same URL.
65	- [ ] **[S] chess-tui + chess-web: picker redesign (rules/style preview panel)** — Current picker shows a hero block + variant cards; a richer flow would let users live-preview the board with selected rules + glyph style before starting. Out-of-scope of the 2026-05-07 polish PR (kept minimal). Worth doing once the rule-editing modal (`backlog/web-ingame-rules-modal.md`) lands so we can share components.
66	- [ ] **[S] Replay format (PGN-equivalent)** — Once notation is full (WXF + ICCS + banqi), the history Vec<MoveRecord> already contains everything. Add a serializer to text format + import. Unblocks observers / spectators / training data.
67	- [ ] **[S] insta snapshot tests for initial positions** — Lock in the visual rendering of fresh xiangqi/banqi positions. Catches regressions in setup.rs that pass type-checks but place pieces wrong. Plan flagged this; deferred from PR 1 because unit tests in setup.rs already cover key invariants.
68	
69	## P?
70	
71	Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.
72	
73	- [ ] **[?/L] Wish/SSH multiplayer alternative to chess-net** — Original analysis (docs/architecture.md) noted Go+wish would give 'ssh chess.you.dev' for free. Rust equivalent: russh (less polished). Spike: is this simpler than chess-net + chess-web for the 'play with friends' scenario, or just neat?
74	- [ ] **[?/L] Unity renderer via FFI** — Architecture deliberately keeps chess-core platform-agnostic so Unity is reachable. Spike: does building a chess-core C ABI + Unity importer get us anything beyond 'Web with art assets via Leptos + sprites'? Likely not, but documenting the option closes the loop.
75	- [ ] **[?/M] WebRTC peer-to-peer fallback for chess-web** — Lets two browsers play without chess-net hosting. Needs a signalling step (could reuse chess-net briefly for handshake). Big UX delta: NAT traversal failures are the user's problem, not the server's. Worth it only if hosting cost ever becomes a concern.
76	
77	## Done
78	
79	- ✅ [2026-05-07] [P2/S] Victory effects + 將軍/CHECK toggle + picker hero (TUI & Web, protocol v4) — End-of-game gets a confetti burst (40+ particles, ~3 s) and a centered ASCII / SVG VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW banner overlaid on the board. Net players see personalised VICTORY/DEFEAT keyed off their seat; spectators and Local pass-and-play see neutral "Red Wins / Black Wins". chess-tui ships `--no-confetti` / `--no-check-banner` CLI flags; chess-web ships matching localStorage toggles (`chess.fx.confetti`, `chess.fx.checkBanner`) wired to two checkboxes in the sidebar footer. The "將軍 / CHECK" badge is now toggleable in both clients (default ON); chess-tui Net mode finally shows it (used to be Local-only). Wire change: `PlayerView` gains `pub in_check: bool` (`#[serde(default)]`, no-leak — banqi always reads false), bumps protocol to v4 — wire-compatible with v3 clients via the `serde(default)`. Picker polish: TUI gets a 5-row ASCII title (with single-line fallback in narrow terminals); Web gets a hero header with 帥 glyph + bilingual title + tagline. New: `clients/chess-tui/src/{banner,confetti}.rs`, `clients/chess-web/src/{prefs,components/end_overlay,components/confetti}.rs`, `docs/adr/0007-spectator-check-flag.md`. ASCII art is hand-rolled (no `figlet-rs` dep); confetti is xorshift32 + ratatui buffer cells (TUI) and 48 CSS-keyframed `<div>`s (web). Spectator share/replay link + bigger picker redesign tracked separately in P3.
80	
81	- ✅ [2026-05-07] [P2/M] chess-net: spectator slots + in-room chat (protocol v3) — Third+ connection joining a room with `?role=spectator` upgrades to read-only spectator (`ServerMsg::Spectating` welcome instead of `Hello`); Move/Resign/Rematch/Chat from a spectator return `Error{"spectators cannot ..."}`. Players-only chat: `ClientMsg::Chat { text }` is rate-limit free for now but trimmed to ≤256 chars and stamped with `ts_ms` server-side; broadcast as `ServerMsg::Chat { line }` to seats + spectators and replayed to late joiners via `ServerMsg::ChatHistory` (50-line ring buffer, `VecDeque` per room). `RoomSummary` gains `spectators: u16` (`#[serde(default)]` for v2 back-compat); the lobby push includes the count and the chess-web lobby gains a Watch button while chess-tui gains a `w` lobby key. Spectator capacity defaults to 16 per room, `--max-spectators` / `CHESS_NET_MAX_SPECTATORS` to override. chess-tui press 't' to open a chat input editor (Enter sends, Esc cancels, players only). chess-web ships a sidebar `<ChatPanel>` with auto-scrolling log + disabled-for-spectators input. `make play-spectator` + `scripts/play-spectator.sh` boot a 2-player + 1-spectator tmux demo. ADR-0006 captures the spectator opt-in choice (`?role=spectator` rather than auto-fallback) for v2 back-compat. 8 new server-smoke tests + 5 new round-trip tests; v2 RoomSummary still decodes via `serde(default)`. Chat moderation, spectator chat permission per room, system messages (`ChatLine.from` enum widening to v4), reconnect-as-same-seat, and chess-web chat polish are separate P2 follow-ups.
82	
83	- ✅ [2026-05-07] [P2/M] chess-web: Leptos + WASM frontend — `clients/chess-web` ships as a single-page Leptos+Trunk SPA covering all three variants (xiangqi local + online; banqi local + online; three-kingdom renders the empty 4×8 stub with a "WIP" overlay per CLAUDE.md). SVG-only rendering — no art assets — code-as-presentation, mirroring chess-tui's intersection layout. Both modes share one `<Board>` component: local mode owns `GameState` and projects `PlayerView` after every move; online mode receives `PlayerView` via `gloo-net` WS to `chess-net`'s `/ws/<room>` and `/lobby` endpoints. `orient.rs` + `glyph.rs` are byte-copies of the chess-tui modules with cloned tests (drift caught by both crates' CI). chess-net gains a `static-serve` feature (default-on) and `--static-dir <path>` flag that mounts `tower_http::services::ServeDir` as the route fallback so a single binary serves the SPA + WS in production; chess-web depends on chess-net with `default-features = false` so axum/tokio do not leak into the WASM build. New `make play-web` / `make build-web` / `make stop-web` targets + `scripts/play-web.sh` mirror the existing tmux harnesses. Trunk dev-server proxies `/ws*` and `/lobby` to chess-net at :7878. Reconnect / mobile layout / move animations / Playwright E2E / promote-orient-glyph-to-shared-crate are separate follow-ups in P2/P3.
84	
85	- ✅ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-net: multi-room lobby + optional password — `crates/chess-net` server now keys rooms by string id (`Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<RoomState>>>>>`). New routes: `GET /ws/<id>` (auto-creates the room), `GET /lobby` (live `ServerMsg::Rooms` push on every state change), `GET /rooms` (JSON snapshot for `curl`). Optional `?password=<secret>` query param locked in by the first joiner; mismatched joiners get `Error{"bad password"}` before any `Hello` and are dropped. Default room `main` (served from `/` and `/ws`) is permanent so v1 clients keep working. `chess-tui` adds `Screen::HostPrompt`, `Screen::Lobby`, `Screen::CreateRoom` plus the `Connect to server…` picker entry; `--lobby <ws>` and `--password <pw>` flags. Lobby uses a second sync `tungstenite` worker — no tokio added to the TUI. New ADR-0005, `make play-lobby` launcher, 8 server-smoke tests, 3 protocol round-trips. Mixed-variant servers / spectators / lobby filter / debouncing / admin tokens / TLS are separate P2 follow-ups.
86	
87	- ✅ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-net MVP: single-room websocket server + chess-tui --connect + tmux launcher — `crates/chess-net` ships an axum-ws server (`chess-net-server`) hosting one game/room. Wire schema: tagged JSON enums `ServerMsg` (Hello/Update/Error) and `ClientMsg` (Move/Resign), `Move::Reveal` stays `revealed: None` end-to-end. First connection = Red, second = Black, third gets "room full". `chess-tui --connect ws://...` runs a sync `tungstenite` worker thread + `std::sync::mpsc` channels (no tokio in TUI). `make play-local` / `scripts/play-local.sh` opens a tmux session with two clients in window 0 and the server in window 1. 8 new tests (6 protocol round-trip + 2 end-to-end smoke). Lobby / reconnect / time controls / takeback still deferred (kept as separate TODO items).
88	
89	- ✅ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-tui: ratatui frontend — interactive TUI for xiangqi + banqi. Vim cursor (hjkl) + arrows + mouse, Enter/Space select-or-commit, `u` undo, `f` flip (banqi). Two render styles: CJK glyphs (帥仕相俥傌炮兵 / 將士象車馬砲卒, default with color) and ASCII (--style ascii). Banqi displays in traditional 8×4 layout (transposed from 4×8 model). Variant picker on bare invocation; --as black flips xiangqi orientation for net-readiness. Engine stays presentation-free — orient.rs + glyph.rs live in the client.
90	
91	- ✅ [2026-05-06] [P1/S] End-condition unit tests (checkmate / stalemate / banqi no-moves / no-progress) — tests/end_conditions.rs verifies refresh_status produces Won{Checkmate} / Won{Stalemate} / Won{OnlyOneSideHasPieces} from .pos fixtures. Pattern reusable for endgame puzzles.
92	
93	Recently shipped. When implementing an active item, in the same commit run:
94	
95	```
96	scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<one-line shipped summary>"
97	```
98	
99	This moves the entry here using the dated `Done` syntax and re-validates.

```text
160	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
161	    // Spectators see-only — no click handlers on the board for them.

164	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
165	        if is_spectator {
166	            return;
167	        }
168	        let v = view.get();
169	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
170	            return;
171	        }
172	        let cur = selected.get();
173	        if cur == Some(sq) {
174	            selected.set(None);
175	            return;
176	        }
177	        if let Some(from) = cur {
178	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
179	                handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
180	                selected.set(None);
181	                return;
182	            }
183	        }
184	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
185	        match cell {
186	            VisibleCell::Revealed(p) if p.piece.side == v.side_to_move => {
187	                selected.set(Some(sq));
188	            }
189	            VisibleCell::Hidden => {
190	                if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
191	                    handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
192	                    selected.set(None);
193	                }
194	            }
195	            _ => selected.set(None),
196	        }
197	    });
198

201	            shape=shape
202	            observer=obs
203	            view=view
204	            selected=selected
205	            on_click=on_click
```

```text
1	//! Banqi house-rule scenario tests.
2	//! Constructs hand-crafted positions to exercise CHAIN_CAPTURE and CHARIOT_RUSH.
3	
4	use chess_core::board::Board;
5	use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
6	use chess_core::moves::Move;
7	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
8	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
9	use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
10	use smallvec::smallvec;
11	
12	fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
13	    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
14	    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
15	    for sq in squares {
16	        state.board.set(sq, None);
17	    }
18	    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
19	    state
20	}
21	
22	fn place(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
23	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
24	}
25	
26	#[test]
27	fn chain_capture_three_in_a_row() {
28	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
29	    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
30	    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
31	    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
32	    let s3 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
33	    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
34	    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
35	    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
36	    place(&mut state.board, s3, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
37	
38	    let chains: Vec<Move> = state
39	        .legal_moves()
40	        .into_iter()
41	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. }))
42	        .collect();
43	
44	    // Should have chains of length 2 and 3.
45	    let lengths: Vec<usize> = chains
46	        .iter()
47	        .filter_map(|m| match m {
48	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => Some(path.len()),
49	            _ => None,
50	        })
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`

```text
1	//! Wire protocol between `chess-net-server` and clients.
2	//!
3	//! Frames are JSON text frames, one message per frame. Tagged enums via
4	//! `#[serde(tag = "type")]` keep the schema obvious in `wscat`/`jq`.
5	//!
6	//! All payload types come straight from `chess-core` and already derive
7	//! `Serialize/Deserialize`, including the proptest-validated no-leak
8	//! projection in `PlayerView` (hidden banqi pieces stay opaque on the wire).
9	
10	use chess_core::moves::Move;
11	use chess_core::piece::Side;
12	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
14	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
15	
16	/// Bumped whenever the wire schema changes incompatibly.
17	///
18	/// v1 → v2: added `ServerMsg::Rooms` and `ClientMsg::ListRooms` for the
19	/// multi-room lobby. Old (v1) clients still work against a v2 server in
20	/// the default room "main" — the new message variants are additive.
21	///
22	/// v2 → v3: added `ServerMsg::{Spectating, ChatHistory, Chat}` and
23	/// `ClientMsg::Chat` for read-only spectators + in-room chat. v2 clients
24	/// keep working as players (they never request spectator role; the new
25	/// chat variants are additive). `RoomSummary` gains a `spectators` field
26	/// with `#[serde(default)]` so v2 lobby clients deserializing a v3 server
27	/// snapshot ignore the new column.
28	///
29	/// v3 → v4: added `PlayerView.in_check: bool` so clients can render a
30	/// "將軍 / CHECK" warning without having to recompute it from the board.
31	/// Wire-compatible — `#[serde(default)]` on the new field means a v3
32	/// client deserializing a v4 message reads `in_check = false` and a v4
33	/// client deserializing a v3 message gets the same default. The bump is
34	/// for documentation, not enforcement; the handshake still uses lenient
35	/// equality. See ADR-0007.
36	pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u16 = 4;
37	
38	/// Server → client.
39	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
40	#[serde(tag = "type")]
41	pub enum ServerMsg {
42	    /// Sent once per client right after side assignment. Carries the variant
43	    /// (so the client knows what to render) plus the initial `PlayerView`.
44	    Hello { protocol: u16, observer: Side, rules: RuleSet, view: PlayerView },
45	    /// Sent to every seated client after each committed move.
46	    Update { view: PlayerView },
47	    /// Illegal move, room full, malformed message, opponent disconnect, etc.
48	    /// Non-fatal by default — clients should display and keep the connection.
49	    Error { message: String },
50	    /// Pushed on the lobby socket only — initial snapshot on connect, then
51	    /// again whenever any room's state changes (seat insertion / removal /
52	    /// game finish / GC). Game sockets never receive this variant.
53	    Rooms { rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> },
54	    /// Spectator-side counterpart to `Hello` — no `observer` because
55	    /// spectators are not seated. Carries the same `rules` + initial `view`
56	    /// (projected from `Side::RED`'s perspective so banqi hidden tiles stay
57	    /// hidden). Sent immediately after a `?role=spectator` connection
58	    /// upgrades successfully.
59	    Spectating { protocol: u16, rules: RuleSet, view: PlayerView },
60	    /// Pushed once right after `Hello` / `Spectating` with the room's chat
61	    /// ring buffer (≤50 lines). Empty `lines` for a fresh room.
62	    ChatHistory { lines: Vec<ChatLine> },
63	    /// Pushed live to every recipient (seats + spectators) on each new chat
64	    /// line. `from` is currently always a player's `Side`; system messages
65	    /// (player joined / left) are deferred — see
66	    /// `backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md`.
67	    Chat { line: ChatLine },
68	}
69	
70	/// Client → server.
71	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
72	#[serde(tag = "type")]
73	pub enum ClientMsg {
74	    /// Submit a move. For `Move::Reveal`, send `revealed: None` — the server
75	    /// fills in the piece identity locally; the wire form stays `None`.
76	    Move { mv: Move },
77	    /// Resign — ends the game with `WinReason::Resignation` for the opponent.
78	    Resign,
79	    /// Request a rematch. Only valid after the current game has ended
80	    /// (Won / Drawn). Server resets the game when both sides have requested
81	    /// and re-sends `Hello` to each. Same observer assignment, same rules,
82	    /// fresh `GameState`.
83	    Rematch,
84	    /// Lobby-only: forces a fresh `Rooms` snapshot to the requester. The
85	    /// server pushes `Rooms` automatically on state changes; this is the
86	    /// manual-refresh button (`r` in the TUI lobby).
87	    ListRooms,
88	    /// Send a chat line. Server enforces players-only (spectators get
89	    /// `Error{"spectators cannot chat"}`), trims to ≤256 chars after stripping
90	    /// control chars, stamps `ts_ms` server-side, and broadcasts the result
91	    /// to every seat + spectator as `ServerMsg::Chat`.
92	    Chat { text: String },
93	}
94	
95	/// One chat line as it appears on the wire and in the per-room ring buffer.
96	/// `ts_ms` is unix milliseconds set server-side so clients don't have to
97	/// agree on a clock.
98	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
99	pub struct ChatLine {
100	    pub from: Side,
101	    pub text: String,
102	    pub ts_ms: u64,
103	}
104	
105	/// One row in the lobby browser. Mirrors what's interesting to a player
106	/// deciding whether to join: variant for setting expectations, seat count
107	/// for "is this room joinable?", `has_password` for the lock icon, and
108	/// `status` so finished rooms stand out from active games.
109	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
110	pub struct RoomSummary {
111	    pub id: String,
112	    pub variant: String,
113	    pub seats: u8,
114	    /// Watching-only clients connected via `?role=spectator`. v2 lobby
115	    /// snapshots omit this field entirely; the `serde(default)` keeps v2
116	    /// clients deserializing a v3 `RoomSummary` cleanly (they just see 0).
117	    #[serde(default)]
118	    pub spectators: u16,
119	    pub has_password: bool,
120	    pub status: RoomStatus,
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-net/src/server.rs`

```text
860	fn process_move(g: &mut RoomState, seat: Side, mv: Move, room_id: &str) {
861	    if g.state.side_to_move != seat {
862	        send_to(g, seat, ServerMsg::Error { message: "not your turn".into() });
863	        return;
864	    }
865	    match g.state.make_move(&mv) {
866	        Ok(()) => {
867	            g.state.refresh_status();
868	            eprintln!("[server][{}] {:?} -> {:?}", room_id, seat, mv);
869	            broadcast_update(g);
870	        }
871	        Err(e) => {
872	            send_to(g, seat, ServerMsg::Error { message: format!("illegal move: {e}") });
873	        }
874	    }
875	}
876	
877	fn send_to(g: &RoomState, seat: Side, msg: ServerMsg) {
878	    if let Some((_, tx)) = g.seats.iter().find(|(s, _)| *s == seat) {
879	        let _ = tx.send(msg);
880	    }
881	}
882	
883	fn broadcast_update(g: &RoomState) {
884	    for (side, tx) in &g.seats {
885	        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, *side);
886	        let _ = tx.send(ServerMsg::Update { view });
887	    }
888	    if !g.spectators.is_empty() {
889	        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, Side::RED);
890	        for tx in &g.spectators {
891	            let _ = tx.send(ServerMsg::Update { view: view.clone() });
892	        }
893	    }
894	}
895	
896	/// Fan a single message out to every connection (seats + spectators).
897	/// Used for `Chat` where every recipient should see the same payload —
898	/// `Update` uses [`broadcast_update`] instead because seats need
899	/// per-side projections.
900	fn broadcast_to_all(g: &RoomState, msg: &ServerMsg) {
901	    for (_, tx) in &g.seats {
902	        let _ = tx.send(msg.clone());
903	    }
904	    for tx in &g.spectators {
905	        let _ = tx.send(msg.clone());
906	    }
907	}
908
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! ICCS notation: file `a..i` × rank `0..9` → `h2e2` style.
2	//!
3	//! Encoder is straightforward; decoder takes a state because string-form
4	//! moves are ambiguous between Step/Capture/CannonJump (the geometry is
5	//! identical from outside) — we resolve by intersecting with `legal_moves`.
6	
7	use crate::board::Board;
8	use crate::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
9	use crate::error::CoreError;
10	use crate::moves::Move;
11	use crate::state::GameState;
12	use crate::view::PlayerView;
13	
14	/// Encode a single square as `<file><rank>` e.g. `h2`.
15	pub fn encode_square(board: &Board, sq: Square) -> String {
16	    let (f, r) = board.file_rank(sq);
17	    format!("{}{}", file_char(f.0), r.0)
18	}
19	
20	fn file_char(f: u8) -> char {
21	    (b'a' + f) as char
22	}
23	
24	/// Parse a `<file><rank>` square string (e.g. `h2`) against `board`'s
25	/// dimensions. Public so live-preview hooks in clients can reuse it without
26	/// duplicating bounds checks.
27	pub fn parse_square_str(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Option<Square> {
28	    parse_square(board, s).ok()
29	}
30	
31	fn parse_square(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError> {
32	    let mut chars = s.chars();
33	    let f = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
34	    let r = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
35	    if chars.next().is_some() {
36	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
37	    }
38	    if !f.is_ascii_lowercase() {
39	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
40	    }
41	    let file_idx = (f as u8) - b'a';
42	    let rank_idx = r.to_digit(10).ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))? as u8;
43	    if file_idx >= board.width() || rank_idx >= board.height() {
44	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(format!("{s} out of board bounds")));
45	    }
46	    Ok(board.sq(File(file_idx), Rank(rank_idx)))
47	}
48	
49	/// Encode a move as ICCS. `Reveal` becomes `flip <sq>`. `ChainCapture`
50	/// chains squares with `x` separators.
51	pub fn encode_move(board: &Board, m: &Move) -> String {
52	    match m {
53	        Move::Reveal { at, .. } => format!("flip {}", encode_square(board, *at)),
54	        Move::Step { from, to } => {
55	            format!("{}{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
56	        }
57	        Move::Capture { from, to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { from, to, .. } => {
58	            format!("{}x{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
59	        }
60	        Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
61	            let mut s = encode_square(board, *from);
62	            for hop in path {
63	                s.push('x');
64	                s.push_str(&encode_square(board, hop.to));
65	            }
66	            s
67	        }
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	/// Decode a move from ICCS, resolving Step/Capture ambiguity by matching
72	/// against the current legal-move list. Forms accepted:
73	/// - `h2e2`               — step
74	/// - `h2xh9`              — capture (single)
75	/// - `a3xb3xc3`           — chain capture (any number of x-separated squares)
76	/// - `flip a3` / `flip a3` — reveal
77	pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
78	    let legal = state.legal_moves();
79	    decode_move_with(&state.board, &legal, input)
80	}
81	
82	/// Same decoder, but resolves against a [`PlayerView`] — useful from
83	/// network clients that only hold the projected view, not the authoritative
84	/// `GameState`. Builds an empty board of the view's shape for square parsing
85	/// (notation only consults width/height) and matches against `view.legal_moves`.
86	pub fn decode_move_from_view(view: &PlayerView, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
87	    let board = Board::new(view.shape);
88	    decode_move_with(&board, &view.legal_moves, input)
89	}
90	
91	fn decode_move_with(board: &Board, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
92	    let trimmed = input.trim();
93	    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("flip ") {
94	        let at = parse_square(board, rest.trim())?;
95	        return Ok(Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None });
96	    }
97	
98	    // Split into squares: handle both 'h2e2' (no separator) and 'h2xe2'.
99	    let parts: Vec<Square> = if trimmed.contains('x') {
100	        trimmed.split('x').map(|p| parse_square(board, p.trim())).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?
101	    } else if trimmed.len() == 4 {
102	        vec![parse_square(board, &trimmed[..2])?, parse_square(board, &trimmed[2..])?]
103	    } else {
104	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
105	    };
106	
107	    if parts.len() < 2 {
108	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
109	    }
110	
111	    let from = parts[0];
112	
113	    if parts.len() == 2 {
114	        // Step / Capture / CannonJump — find unique match by (from, to).
115	        let to = parts[1];
116	        let candidates: Vec<&Move> = legal
117	            .iter()
118	            .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(to))
119	            .collect();
120	        match candidates.as_slice() {
121	            [one] => Ok((*one).clone()),
122	            [] => Err(CoreError::Illegal("no legal move matches notation")),
123	            _multi => {
124	                // Multiple matches (e.g. Step vs ChainCapture with len 2). Prefer
125	                // the simplest: Step > Capture > CannonJump > ChainCapture.
126	                let pick = candidates
127	                    .iter()
128	                    .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. }))
129	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { .. })))
130	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::CannonJump { .. })))
131	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })));
132	                pick.map(|m| (*m).clone()).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("ambiguous"))
133	            }
134	        }
135	    } else {
136	        // ChainCapture — find legal move whose path matches.
137	        let chain_squares = &parts[1..];
138	        let candidate = legal.iter().find(|m| match m {
139	            Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
140	                *f == from
141	                    && path.len() == chain_squares.len()
142	                    && path.iter().zip(chain_squares.iter()).all(|(h, sq)| h.to == *sq)
143	            }
144	            _ => false,
145	        });
146	        candidate.cloned().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no chain capture matches notation"))
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	#[cfg(test)]
151	mod tests {
152	    use super::*;
153	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
154	
155	    #[test]
156	    fn round_trip_simple_step() {
157	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
158	        // Pick a legal Step and round-trip it.
159	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
160	        let s = encode_move(&state.board, &m);
161	        let decoded = decode_move(&state, &s).unwrap();
162	        assert_eq!(m, decoded);
163	    }
164	
165	    #[test]
166	    fn parse_h2e2_in_xiangqi() {
167	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
168	        // h2 is a Red cannon. h2e2 moves it 6 squares west — the standard 炮二平五 (well, 炮 to e2).
169	        let m = decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap();
170	        if let Move::Step { from, to } = m {
171	            let (f1, r1) = state.board.file_rank(from);
172	            let (f2, r2) = state.board.file_rank(to);
173	            assert_eq!((f1.0, r1.0, f2.0, r2.0), (7, 2, 4, 2));
174	        } else {
175	            panic!("expected Step, got {m:?}");
176	        }
177	    }
178	
179	    #[test]
180	    fn flip_decodes_to_reveal() {
181	        let state = GameState::new(crate::rules::RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
182	            crate::rules::HouseRules::empty(),
183	            3,
184	        ));
185	        let m = decode_move(&state, "flip a0").unwrap();
186	        assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
187	    }
188	
189	    #[test]
190	    fn bad_notation_rejected() {
191	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
192	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "").is_err());
193	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "zz").is_err());
194	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "z9z9").is_err());
195	    }
196	
197	    #[test]
198	    fn decode_from_view_matches_state() {
199	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
200	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
201	        assert_eq!(
202	            decode_move_from_view(&view, "h2e2").unwrap(),
203	            decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap()
204	        );
205	    }
206	
207	    #[test]
208	    fn decode_from_view_flip_in_banqi() {
209	        let state = GameState::new(crate::rules::RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
210	            crate::rules::HouseRules::empty(),
211	            3,
212	        ));
213	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
214	        let m = decode_move_from_view(&view, "flip a0").unwrap();
215	        assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
216	    }
217	}
218
```

 `./crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs`

```text
82	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
83	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
84	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
85	        }
86	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
87	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
88	
89	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
90	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
91	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
92	
93	        self.history.push(MoveRecord { mover, the_move: normalized.clone(), no_progress_before });
94	
95	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
96	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
97	        } else {
98	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
99	        }
100	
101	        self.turn_order.advance();
102	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
103	
104	        Ok(())
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Undo the last move.
108	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
109	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
110	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
111	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
112	        // Rewind turn order
113	        if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
114	            self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
115	        } else {
116	            self.turn_order.current -= 1;
117	        }
118	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
119	        // Game status reset to Ongoing — caller may recompute.
120	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
121	        Ok(())
122	    }
123	
124	    fn normalize_for_apply(&self, m: &Move) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
125	        match m {
126	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None } => {
127	                let pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
128	                if pos.revealed {
129	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
130	                }
131	                Ok(Move::Reveal { at: *at, revealed: Some(pos.piece) })
132	            }
133	            _ => Ok(m.clone()),
134	        }
135	    }
136	
137	    fn apply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
138	        match m {
139	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
140	                let mut pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
141	                if pos.revealed {
142	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
143	                }
144	                pos.revealed = true;
145	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
146	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment.
147	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
148	                    if let Some(p) = revealed {
149	                        self.side_assignment =
150	                            Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
151	                    }
152	                }
153	            }
154	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
155	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Step: empty from"))?;
156	                if self.board.get(*to).is_some() {
157	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Step: occupied to"));
158	                }
159	                self.board.set(*from, None);
160	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
161	            }
162	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured: _ } => {
163	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Capture: empty from"))?;
164	                self.board.set(*from, None);
165	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
166	            }
167	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured: _ } => {
168	                let p =
169	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("CannonJump: empty from"))?;
170	                self.board.set(*from, None);
171	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
172	            }
173	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
174	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty from"))?;
175	                if path.is_empty() {
176	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty path"));
177	                }
178	                self.board.set(*from, None);
179	                for hop in &path[..path.len() - 1] {
180	                    self.board.set(hop.to, None);
181	                }
182	                self.board.set(path.last().unwrap().to, Some(p));
183	            }
184	        }
185	        Ok(())
186	    }
187	
188	    fn unapply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
189	        match m {
190	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => {
191	                let mut pos =
192	                    self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Reveal: empty"))?;
193	                pos.revealed = false;
194	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
195	                // First-flip might have set side_assignment; if this was the first move, clear it.
196	                if self.history.is_empty() {
197	                    self.side_assignment = None;
198	                }
199	            }
200	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
201	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Step: empty to"))?;
202	                self.board.set(*to, None);
203	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
204	            }
205	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
206	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Capture: empty to"))?;
207	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
208	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
209	            }
210	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
211	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Jump: empty to"))?;
212	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
213	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
214	            }
215	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
216	                let last_to = path.last().unwrap().to;
217	                let p =
218	                    self.board.get(last_to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Chain: empty last"))?;
219	                self.board.set(last_to, None);
220	                // Restore intermediate captures (in any order).
221	                for hop in path {
222	                    self.board.set(hop.to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(hop.captured)));
223	                }
224	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
225	            }
226	        }
227	        Ok(())
228	    }
229	
230	    /// Whether `side`'s general is currently under attack by the opponent.
231	    /// Xiangqi-specific; banqi has no general-in-check concept.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-07 08:47:46Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a summary report of my findings:

## Investigation Report: Banqi Side Assignment & Turn Order

### 1. **Where Banqi's Initial `side_to_move` is Set**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/setup.rs`  
**Lines**: 75–98 (`build_banqi` function)

```rust
fn build_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
    // ... shuffles 32 pieces face-down ...
    GameState {
        rules,
        board,
        side_to_move: Side::RED,              // ← FIXED: always RED at init
        turn_order: TurnOrder::two_player(),  // ← Seats: [RED, BLACK]
        history: Vec::new(),
        status: GameStatus::Ongoing,
        side_assignment: None,                // ← Locked after first Reveal
        no_progress_plies: 0,
    }
}
```

**Current behavior**: `side_to_move` is hardcoded to `Side::RED` at initialization. The `turn_order` is a fixed two-player round-robin (`[RED, BLACK]` at indices 0, 1).

---

### 2. **"Side Undecided" / "First Reveal Binds Side Assignment" Concept**

**YES — This is implemented, but with a key asymmetry:**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs`  
**Lines**: 139–152 (in `apply_inner`), and 307–317 (the binding function)

```rust
Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
    // ... normalize and reveal piece ...
    if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
        if let Some(p) = revealed {
            self.side_assignment = 
                Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
        }
    }
}

fn banqi_side_assignment(flipper: Side, revealed_side: Side) -> SideAssignment {
    let mut mapping = SmallVec::new();
    if flipper == Side::RED {
        mapping.push(revealed_side);           // RED controls revealed_side
        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite()); // BLACK controls the OTHER side
    } else {
        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite()); // RED controls the OTHER side
        mapping.push(revealed_side);            // BLACK controls revealed_side
    }
    SideAssignment { mapping }
}
```

**Semantics**: When the first piece is flipped:
- If `Side::RED` flips a **Red piece** → RED plays Red, BLACK plays Black
- If `Side::RED` flips a **Black piece** → RED plays Black, BLACK plays Red (the flipper gets the revealed color; the opponent gets the opposite)

**Note**: The `SideAssignment` is `Option<_>` and starts `None`. Once set on the first flip, it never changes. Legal move generation respects it — you can only move/capture your assigned color.

---

### 3. **How `side_to_move` Advances After Each Move**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs`  
**Lines**: 101–102 (in `make_move`)

```rust
self.turn_order.advance();
self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
```

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/turn_order.rs`  
**Lines**: 36–38 (in `advance`)

```rust
pub fn advance(&mut self) {
    self.current = (self.current + 1) % self.seats.len() as u8;
}
```

**For banqi**: The turn order is purely round-robin through `[RED, BLACK]`. **Every move (including the first Reveal) flips to the next player.** The first `Move::Reveal` does NOT interact specially with side-assignment beyond setting it — turn still advances normally.

---

### 4. **Player → Seat Assignment in chess-net (network layer)**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-net/src/server.rs`  
**Lines**: 94–103 (in `RoomState::next_seat`)

```rust
fn next_seat(&self) -> Option<Side> {
    match self.seats.len() {
        0 => Some(Side::RED),
        1 => {
            let taken = self.seats[0].0;
            Some(if taken == Side::RED { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED })
        }
        _ => None,
    }
}
```

**Seating flow** (lines 408, 423):
- First joiner: assigned `Side::RED`
- Second joiner: assigned `Side::BLACK`
- Third+ joiner: `Error{"room full"}` (or spectator if `?role=spectator`)

**Independence**: Network seating is **completely orthogonal** to engine side-assignment. The server seat determines who gets to move according to `GameState.side_to_move` (which follows `TurnOrder`), but does NOT determine piece color until the first flip locks it in the engine.

---

### 5. **Tests Covering Initial Side / Turn Order in Banqi**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_basic.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn first_flip_locks_side_assignment() {
    let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 5));
    assert!(state.side_assignment.is_none());
    let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().next().unwrap();
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    assert!(state.side_assignment.is_some(), "side assignment must lock after first flip");
}

#[test]
fn fresh_banqi_offers_32_reveals() {
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
    let moves = state.legal_moves();
    assert_eq!(moves.len(), 32);
    assert!(moves.iter().all(|m| matches!(m, Move::Reveal { .. })));
}
```

**Other fixtures**: `crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_house_chain.rs` manually sets `side_assignment` in test helpers but does not test the initial binding flow.

---

### 6. **Rules Documentation**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/docs/rules/banqi.md`  
**Lines**: 12–16

```
## Setup

1. Shuffle all 32 pieces (16 red + 16 black) face-down across the 4×8 grid.
2. The starting player flips any piece on their turn — the **color** they reveal 
   determines their faction for the rest of the game (`SideAssignment` is locked here).
3. From this point onward, players control their assigned color.
```

**Status**: "skeleton — base banqi rules" (header). The documentation **explicitly states** the traditional rule (first flip determines faction), and the code **implements it correctly**.

**Backlog**: `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` covers the 3-faction variant but does not touch side-assignment (focuses on board layout and capture rules for 3-player play).

---

### Summary

The **current implementation is correct** per traditional banqi rules:
- Initial `side_to_move` is fixed to RED (first mover).
- `side_assignment` starts `None` and is **locked on the first Reveal** based on the flipper's seat and the revealed piece's color.
- The flipper takes control of the revealed color; the opponent gets the opposite.
- Turn order advances normally (RED → BLACK → RED …) regardless of when side-assignment binds.
- Network seating (server assigning RED/BLACK seats) is independent of engine color binding.

No "TODO" comments or partial implementations exist. The backlog and TODO.md contain no related work-in-progress.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-07 08:48:10Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have the full picture. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Investigation Report: Banqi UX Bug - Input Flow Analysis

### 1. TUI Input Handling (`clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`)

**Key finding:** Two separate code paths for reveal vs. capture, but **reveal is hardcoded as `Move::Reveal`** regardless of whether a piece is selected.

- **Line 1061** (`Action::FlipKey`): When user presses `f`, always builds `Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None }`, even if `g.selected` contains a piece to capture with.
- **Lines 1077–1129** (`handle_select_or_commit`): The click/Enter flow:
  - Lines 1092–1106: No piece selected → try to select from `sq`. If it's `VisibleCell::Hidden`, suggest "Press 'f' to flip" (line 1102).
  - Lines 1112–1127: Piece selected at `from` → search legal moves for `m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq)`. **This lookup will fail if `sq` is a hidden tile** because `Move::Reveal` returns `None` from `to_square()` (moves.rs line 67). So the UI falls through to "Illegal move" (line 1123).

**The bug:** Line 1118's `find()` can never match a `Move::Reveal` with a selected piece. Hidden tiles are only "legal" if no piece is selected — then clicking one at line 190 attempts `find_move(&v, sq, sq)` which succeeds because `Reveal` matches when `from == to` (state.rs line 58).

---

### 2. Web Client Input Handling (`clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs`)

**Similar architecture, same dead-end pattern:**

- **Lines 164–197** (`on_click` callback):
  - Lines 177–182: Has piece selected → call `find_move(&v, from, sq)`. Like TUI, **this fails for hidden `sq`** because `Move::Reveal` doesn't match the endpoint logic.
  - Lines 189–193: No piece selected → click hidden tile → `find_move(&v, sq, sq)` succeeds (state.rs line 58: `Reveal` matches when both endpoints are the same square).

**Helper function** (`state.rs` lines 48–60 `find_move` + `matches_endpoints`): Returns `Option<Move>` by checking if `m.origin_square() == from && to_square() == to`. For `Reveal`, `to_square()` returns `None`, so `matches_endpoints` only succeeds if `from == to == at`.

---

### 3. Move Enum and Server Protocol

**`crates/chess-core/src/moves.rs` (lines 19–38):**
- `Move::Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> }` — single variant for banqi flips.
- `Move::Capture { from, to, captured }` — separate variant for captures.
- **No variant for "reveal-then-capture"** — these are strictly two moves.

**`crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs` (line 74–76):**
```rust
/// Submit a move. For `Move::Reveal`, send `revealed: None` — the server
/// fills in the piece identity locally; the wire form stays `None`.
Move { mv: Move },
```

**`crates/chess-net/src/server.rs` (lines 860–875 `process_move`):**
- Checks `side_to_move`, calls `g.state.make_move(&mv)`.
- On `Err(e)` → sends "illegal move" to client.
- No special handling for "reveal under piece selection" — just delegates to core game logic.

---

### 4. Notation Parser (`crates/chess-core/src/notation/iccs.rs`)

**Encode (lines 49–69):**
- `Move::Reveal { at, .. }` → `"flip <sq>"` (e.g., `flip a0`)
- `Move::Capture { from, to, .. }` → `"<from>x<to>"` (e.g., `h3xh4`)

**Decode (lines 91–147):**
- Line 93: Checks for `"flip "` prefix → returns `Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None }`
- Lines 99–109: Non-flip moves split on `x` or parse 4-char strings
- **No syntax exists for "select piece A then reveal-and-capture hidden B"** — the notation parser only generates `Move::Reveal` or `Move::Capture`, never a combined action.

---

### Summary: Where the Protocol Breaks

1. **Legal-move generation** (`banqi.rs` lines 45–56): `gen_reveals()` unconditionally emits `Move::Reveal` for all face-down tiles, regardless of whether a piece is selected.
2. **TUI/web endpoint matching** (app.rs lines 1118, play.rs line 178): Both search legal moves by `from/to` square pair. `Move::Reveal` has no `to` endpoint, so it never matches a "piece selected → click hidden tile" action.
3. **Protocol invariant**: Client sends `Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None }`, server accepts/rejects via `make_move()`. No atomic "reveal-and-capture" move variant exists.

**To support the UX**, the system would need:
- A 4th move variant (e.g., `RevealAndCapture { at: Square, ... }`) or
- A 2-move transaction model, or
- A rule change: allow revealing hidden tiles only when no piece is selected (current de facto behavior).

The code currently enforces option 3 silently by rejecting the attempt as "Illegal move."

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 08:49:41Z)**_

Good — three solid reports. Key findings to verify before I ask you anything:

- **Dark capture (暗吃)**: confirmed — `banqi.rs:45–56` emits one `Move::Reveal` per hidden tile and `Move::Capture` (line 75) explicitly blocks face-down targets. TUI/web both look up moves by `(from, to)` pair, but `Move::Reveal::to_square()` returns `None`, so "select piece + click hidden" never matches. There is no atomic reveal+capture move type today.
- **First-flip-determines-color**: this is **already implemented** — `state/mod.rs:307` (`banqi_side_assignment`) locks `side_assignment` on the first Reveal based on flipper-seat × revealed-color. There's even a passing test (`banqi_basic.rs:first_flip_locks_side_assignment`). So the rule is correct in the engine — the bug, if any, is likely UI labeling. I want to confirm with you.
- **Horse diagonal & Chariot rush**: `HORSE_DIAGONAL` is fully no-op. Let me read the existing rush code to confirm what changes to it the user is asking for.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-core/src/rules/banqi.rs`

```text
1	//! Banqi move generation.
2	//!
3	//! Base rules (always emitted):
4	//! - `Reveal` for every face-down piece.
5	//! - 1-step orthogonal `Step` / `Capture` for face-up own pieces.
6	//! - Cannon `CannonJump` over exactly one screen (face-up or face-down).
7	//!
8	//! House-rule extensions (this PR):
9	//! - `CHAIN_CAPTURE`: emit `ChainCapture` for face-up consecutive captures
10	//!   along the same direction (length ≥ 2).
11	//! - `CHARIOT_RUSH`: chariot uses xiangqi-style multi-square ray for both
12	//!   slides and captures.
13	//!
14	//! Deferred to PR 2 (toggles ship as accepted-but-no-effect):
15	//! - `DARK_CHAIN`: chain through face-down squares (requires Option<Piece>
16	//!   in ChainHop and the server-authoritative reveal flow).
17	//! - `HORSE_DIAGONAL`: horse moves like xiangqi.
18	//! - `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`: cannon non-capturing slide.
19	
20	use smallvec::SmallVec;
21	
22	use crate::board::Board;
23	use crate::coord::{Direction, Square};
24	use crate::moves::{ChainHop, Move, MoveList};
25	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
26	use crate::rules::HouseRules;
27	use crate::state::GameState;
28	
29	pub fn generate(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
30	    let house = state.rules.house;
31	    gen_reveals(state, out);
32	
33	    let me = state.side_to_move;
34	    let board = &state.board;
35	
36	    for sq in board.squares() {
37	        let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) else { continue };
38	        if !pos.revealed || pos.piece.side != me {
39	            continue;
40	        }
41	        gen_for_face_up_piece(state, sq, pos.piece, house, out);
42	    }
43	}
44	
45	fn gen_reveals(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
46	    // Banqi rule: ANY face-down piece can be flipped on a player's turn,
47	    // regardless of which side controls the seat. The first flip locks
48	    // the side assignment (handled in state::make_move).
49	    for sq in state.board.squares() {
50	        if let Some(pos) = state.board.get(sq) {
51	            if !pos.revealed {
52	                out.push(Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None });
53	            }
54	        }
55	    }
56	}
57	
58	fn gen_for_face_up_piece(
59	    state: &GameState,
60	    from: Square,
61	    piece: Piece,
62	    house: HouseRules,
63	    out: &mut MoveList,
64	) {
65	    let board = &state.board;
66	
67	    // 1-step orthogonal (and the 連吃 chains it seeds).
68	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
69	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
70	        match board.get(to) {
71	            None => {
72	                // Empty: 1-step slide.
73	                out.push(Move::Step { from, to });
74	            }
75	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
76	                // Face-down piece blocks (can't capture without flipping).
77	                // CANNON jumps handled separately below.
78	            }
79	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == piece.side => {
80	                // Own piece: blocked.
81	            }
82	            Some(target) => {
83	                if can_capture(piece.kind, target.piece.kind) {
84	                    out.push(Move::Capture { from, to, captured: target.piece });
85	                    if house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
86	                        gen_chain_extensions(board, piece, from, to, dir, target.piece, out);
87	                    }
88	                }
89	            }
90	        }
91	    }
92	
93	    // Chariot rush replaces the chariot's 1-step capture with a ray.
94	    // We've already emitted the 1-step Step/Capture above; the ray emits
95	    // additional sliding moves. The 1-step capture is still in the list
96	    // (a strict subset of CHARIOT_RUSH's possibilities).
97	    if piece.kind == PieceKind::Chariot && house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH) {
98	        gen_chariot_rush(board, from, piece.side, out);
99	    }
100	
101	    // Cannon: jump-over-screen captures (always, this is base banqi).
102	    if piece.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
103	        gen_cannon_jumps(board, from, piece.side, out);
104	    }
105	}
106	
107	fn gen_chariot_rush(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
108	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
109	        let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
110	        // Steps 1..N are non-capturing slides. Step 1 was already emitted
111	        // by the base rule, but it's harmless to push duplicates — `make_move`
112	        // is idempotent w.r.t. matching moves and the legal-moves consumer
113	        // dedups via SmallVec ordering. Actually let's avoid duplicates:
114	        for sq in walked.iter().skip(1) {
115	            out.push(Move::Step { from, to: *sq });
116	        }
117	        if let Some(target_sq) = blocker {
118	            if let Some(pos) = board.get(target_sq) {
119	                if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != side {
120	                    // Multi-square capture (1-step capture already emitted by base rule).
121	                    if !walked.is_empty() {
122	                        out.push(Move::Capture { from, to: target_sq, captured: pos.piece });
123	                    }
124	                }
125	            }
126	        }
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	fn gen_cannon_jumps(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
131	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
132	        // Find the screen (first occupied square in this direction).
133	        let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
134	        let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
135	
136	        // Continue past the screen looking for a target.
137	        let mut cursor = screen_sq;
138	        while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
139	            match board.get(next) {
140	                None => {
141	                    cursor = next;
142	                }
143	                Some(pos) => {
144	                    // Cannon captures any face-up enemy regardless of rank.
145	                    if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != side {
146	                        out.push(Move::CannonJump {
147	                            from,
148	                            to: next,
149	                            screen: screen_sq,
150	                            captured: pos.piece,
151	                        });
152	                    }
153	                    break;
154	                }
155	            }
156	        }
157	    }
158	}
159	
160	/// Extend a 1-step capture into chains of length 2, 3, … along the same
161	/// direction. Each emitted ChainCapture has `from = seed_from` and a path
162	/// starting with the seed hop, plus one or more additional captures.
163	fn gen_chain_extensions(
164	    board: &Board,
165	    moving: Piece,
166	    seed_from: Square,
167	    seed_to: Square,
168	    dir: Direction,
169	    seed_captured: Piece,
170	    out: &mut MoveList,
171	) {
172	    let mut path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> = SmallVec::new();
173	    path.push(ChainHop { to: seed_to, captured: seed_captured });
174	
175	    extend_recursive(board, moving, seed_from, dir, seed_to, &mut path, out);
176	}
177	
178	fn extend_recursive(
179	    board: &Board,
180	    moving: Piece,
181	    origin: Square,
182	    dir: Direction,
183	    cursor: Square,
184	    path: &mut SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]>,
185	    out: &mut MoveList,
186	) {
187	    let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) else { return };
188	    let Some(target) = board.get(next) else { return };
189	    if !target.revealed {
190	        return; // face-down; without DARK_CHAIN we stop
191	    }
192	    if target.piece.side == moving.side {
193	        return; // own piece blocks
194	    }
195	    if !can_capture(moving.kind, target.piece.kind) {
196	        return; // outranked
197	    }
198	    path.push(ChainHop { to: next, captured: target.piece });
199	    out.push(Move::ChainCapture { from: origin, path: path.clone() });
200	    extend_recursive(board, moving, origin, dir, next, path, out);
201	    path.pop();
202	}
203	
204	// ---- Capture rank logic ------------------------------------------------------
205	
206	/// Whether `attacker` may capture `defender` under standard banqi rank rules.
207	/// Cannons are not handled here — they capture via `gen_cannon_jumps`.
208	pub fn can_capture(attacker: PieceKind, defender: PieceKind) -> bool {
209	    use PieceKind::*;
210	    match (attacker, defender) {
211	        // Cannon's only legal capture is the jump; not via this path.
212	        (Cannon, _) => false,
213	        // Soldier-beats-General special case.
214	        (Soldier, General) => true,
215	        (General, Soldier) => false,
216	        // Otherwise: outrank the defender (≥).
217	        (a, d) => a.banqi_rank() >= d.banqi_rank(),
218	    }
219	}
220	
221	#[cfg(test)]
222	mod tests {
223	    use super::*;
224	    use crate::piece::PieceOnSquare;
225	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
226	
227	    fn empty_banqi() -> GameState {
228	        // Build a banqi state and clear all pieces, for hand-crafted positions.
229	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
230	        for sq in state.board.squares().collect::<Vec<_>>() {
231	            state.board.set(sq, None);
232	        }
233	        state
234	    }
235	
236	    fn place(state: &mut GameState, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind, revealed: bool) {
237	        let p = Piece::new(side, kind);
238	        state.board.set(
239	            sq,
240	            Some(if revealed { PieceOnSquare::revealed(p) } else { PieceOnSquare::hidden(p) }),
241	        );
242	    }
243	
244	    #[test]
245	    fn fresh_banqi_only_emits_reveals() {
246	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
247	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
248	        assert_eq!(moves.len(), 32);
249	        assert!(moves.iter().all(|m| matches!(m, Move::Reveal { .. })));
250	    }
251	
252	    #[test]
253	    fn rank_capture_table() {
254	        // High rank captures low.
255	        assert!(can_capture(PieceKind::General, PieceKind::Advisor));
256	        assert!(can_capture(PieceKind::Chariot, PieceKind::Horse));
257	        // Equal rank can also capture.
258	        assert!(can_capture(PieceKind::Horse, PieceKind::Horse));
259	        // Low cannot capture high.
260	        assert!(!can_capture(PieceKind::Soldier, PieceKind::Advisor));
261	        // Soldier-beats-General; reverse is false.
262	        assert!(can_capture(PieceKind::Soldier, PieceKind::General));
263	        assert!(!can_capture(PieceKind::General, PieceKind::Soldier));
264	        // Cannon goes through the jump path, not this one.
265	        assert!(!can_capture(PieceKind::Cannon, PieceKind::Soldier));
266	    }
267	
268	    #[test]
269	    fn solo_chariot_one_step_orthogonal() {
270	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
271	        let me_sq = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(1));
272	        place(&mut state, me_sq, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot, true);
273	        // Make sure side_assignment lets RED move.
274	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
275	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
276	        });
277	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
278	        // 4 orthogonal moves, all to empty squares.
279	        let steps: Vec<_> = moves.iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).collect();
280	        assert_eq!(steps.len(), 4);
281	    }
282	
283	    #[test]
284	    fn cannon_jumps_over_screen() {
285	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
286	        let cannon_sq = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(0), crate::coord::Rank(0));
287	        let screen_sq = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(0), crate::coord::Rank(2));
288	        let target_sq = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(0), crate::coord::Rank(4));
289	        place(&mut state, cannon_sq, Side::RED, PieceKind::Cannon, true);
290	        place(&mut state, screen_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
291	        place(&mut state, target_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General, true);
292	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
293	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
294	        });
295	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
296	        let jumps: Vec<_> = moves.iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::CannonJump { .. })).collect();
297	        assert!(!jumps.is_empty(), "cannon should produce at least one jump capture");
298	    }
299	
300	    #[test]
301	    fn chain_capture_emits_when_enabled() {
302	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
303	        // Set HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE on.
304	        state.rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0);
305	        // Red horse at b1, two black soldiers at b2 and b3 — capturable since
306	        // horse(2) >= soldier(0).
307	        let h = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(1));
308	        let s1 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(2));
309	        let s2 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(3));
310	        place(&mut state, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse, true);
311	        place(&mut state, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
312	        place(&mut state, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
313	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
314	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
315	        });
316	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
317	        let chains: Vec<_> =
318	            moves.iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })).collect();
319	        assert!(!chains.is_empty(), "chain capture should emit");
320	        // The 2-hop chain is among them.
321	        let two_hop =
322	            chains.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } if path.len() == 2));
323	        assert!(two_hop, "expected a 2-hop chain capture");
324	    }
325	
326	    #[test]
327	    fn chain_capture_disabled_means_only_single_capture() {
328	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
329	        state.rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0);
330	        let h = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(1));
331	        let s1 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(2));
332	        let s2 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(3));
333	        place(&mut state, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse, true);
334	        place(&mut state, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
335	        place(&mut state, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
336	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
337	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
338	        });
339	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
340	        let chains: Vec<_> =
341	            moves.iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })).collect();
342	        assert!(chains.is_empty(), "no chains without CHAIN_CAPTURE");
343	    }
344	
345	    #[test]
346	    fn chain_capture_make_unmake_round_trip() {
347	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
348	        state.rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0);
349	        let h = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(1));
350	        let s1 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(2));
351	        let s2 = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(1), crate::coord::Rank(3));
352	        place(&mut state, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse, true);
353	        place(&mut state, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
354	        place(&mut state, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier, true);
355	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
356	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
357	        });
358	
359	        let snapshot = state.clone();
360	        let chain = state
361	            .legal_moves()
362	            .into_iter()
363	            .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } if path.len() == 2))
364	            .expect("chain capture should exist");
365	
366	        // Verify origin is correct.
367	        if let Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } = &chain {
368	            assert_eq!(*from, h, "chain origin must be the horse's starting square");
369	        }
370	
371	        state.make_move(&chain).unwrap();
372	        // Horse should be at s2; s1 and h should be empty.
373	        assert!(state.board.get(h).is_none());
374	        assert!(state.board.get(s1).is_none());
375	        assert!(state.board.get(s2).is_some());
376	
377	        state.unmake_move().unwrap();
378	        assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board, "board must restore after chain undo");
379	    }
380	
381	    #[test]
382	    fn chariot_rush_emits_long_slides() {
383	        let mut state = empty_banqi();
384	        state.rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, 0);
385	        let c = state.board.sq(crate::coord::File(0), crate::coord::Rank(0));
386	        place(&mut state, c, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot, true);
387	        state.side_assignment = Some(crate::state::SideAssignment {
388	            mapping: smallvec::smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK],
389	        });
390	        let moves = state.legal_moves();
391	        // Without rush: chariot at corner has 2 moves (N, E). With rush:
392	        // up to 3+7 = 10 moves along the long axis.
393	        assert!(moves.len() >= 5, "chariot rush should produce more slides");
394	    }
395	}
396
```

```text
1	//! Banqi house rules.
2	//!
3	//! Five independent toggles + three named presets. See `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`.
4	
5	use bitflags::bitflags;
6	
7	bitflags! {
8	    /// Toggleable banqi house rules. Combine with `|`.
9	    #[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
10	    #[derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
11	    pub struct HouseRules: u32 {
12	        /// 連吃 — chain captures along the same line.
13	        const CHAIN_CAPTURE     = 1 << 0;
14	        /// 暗連 — chain through face-down squares (implies CHAIN_CAPTURE).
15	        const DARK_CHAIN        = 1 << 1;
16	        /// 車衝 — chariot moves multi-square in a line.
17	        const CHARIOT_RUSH      = 1 << 2;
18	        /// 馬斜 — horse moves like xiangqi (L with leg).
19	        const HORSE_DIAGONAL    = 1 << 3;
20	        /// 炮快移 — cannon non-capturing move slides any distance.
21	        const CANNON_FAST_MOVE  = 1 << 4;
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	/// Classic banqi, no house rules.
26	pub const PRESET_PURIST: HouseRules = HouseRules::empty();
27	
28	/// Common Taiwanese house ruleset: chain captures + chariot rush.
29	pub const PRESET_TAIWAN: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE.union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
30	
31	/// Aggressive ruleset enabling chain (with dark), chariot rush, horse diagonal.
32	pub const PRESET_AGGRESSIVE: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE
33	    .union(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN)
34	    .union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH)
35	    .union(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL);
36	
37	/// `DARK_CHAIN` implies `CHAIN_CAPTURE`. Returns the canonical form.
38	pub fn normalize(mut rules: HouseRules) -> HouseRules {
39	    if rules.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN) {
40	        rules.insert(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE);
41	    }
42	    rules
43	}
44	
45	#[cfg(test)]
46	mod tests {
47	    use super::*;
48	
49	    #[test]
50	    fn presets_are_distinct() {
51	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN);
52	        assert_ne!(PRESET_TAIWAN, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
53	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
54	    }
55	
56	    #[test]
57	    fn taiwan_preset_has_chain_and_rush() {
58	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
59	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
60	        assert!(!PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN));
61	    }
62	
63	    #[test]
64	    fn dark_chain_normalizes_to_include_chain() {
65	        let r = normalize(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN);
66	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
67	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN));
68	    }
69	}
70
```
</details></tool-use>
