<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`

```text
1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients — it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
13	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
14	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
15	
16	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub enum VisibleCell {
18	    Empty,
19	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
20	    Hidden,
21	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
22	}
23	
24	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
25	pub struct PlayerView {
26	    pub observer: Side,
27	    pub shape: BoardShape,
28	    pub width: u8,
29	    pub height: u8,
30	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
31	    pub side_to_move: Side,
32	    pub status: GameStatus,
33	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
34	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
35	    /// Whether the observer's own general is currently under attack.
36	    /// Always `false` for banqi/three-kingdom (no in-check concept) and
37	    /// for spectator-style observers without a real seat. Added in
38	    /// protocol v4; older clients see the default via `serde(default)`.
39	    #[serde(default)]
40	    pub in_check: bool,
41	}
42	
43	impl PlayerView {
44	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
45	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
46	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
47	        let board = &state.board;
48	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
49	            .map(|i| {
50	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
51	                match board.get(sq) {
52	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
53	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
54	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
55	                }
56	            })
57	            .collect();
58	
59	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
60	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
61	        } else {
62	            MoveList::new()
63	        };
64	
65	        // Only the observer's own general counts. Banqi/3K have no general,
66	        // so `is_in_check` returns false naturally — no variant gate needed.
67	        let in_check = state.is_in_check(observer);
68	
69	        Self {
70	            observer,
71	            shape: board.shape(),
72	            width: board.width(),
73	            height: board.height(),
74	            cells,
75	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
76	            status: state.status,
77	            legal_moves,
78	            in_check,
79	        }
80	    }
81	}
82	
83	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` moves before they reach the network.
84	/// Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the engine resolves
85	/// the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
86	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
87	    moves
88	        .into_iter()
89	        .map(|m| match m {
90	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
91	            other => other,
92	        })
93	        .collect()
94	}
95	
96	#[cfg(test)]
97	mod tests {
98	    use super::*;
99	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
100	
101	    #[test]
102	    fn fresh_banqi_view_has_all_hidden_for_any_observer() {
103	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 7));
104	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
105	        assert_eq!(view.cells.len(), 32);
106	        assert!(view.cells.iter().all(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Hidden)));
107	    }
108	
109	    #[test]
110	    fn xiangqi_view_shows_revealed_pieces() {
111	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
112	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
113	        let revealed: Vec<_> =
114	            view.cells.iter().filter(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))).collect();
115	        assert_eq!(revealed.len(), 32);
116	    }
117	
118	    #[test]
119	    fn opponent_view_has_empty_legal_moves() {
120	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
121	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
122	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
123	        assert!(!red_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "side-to-move sees moves");
124	        assert!(black_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "opponent doesn't");
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn reveal_moves_have_no_identity_in_view() {
129	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
130	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
131	        for m in &view.legal_moves {
132	            if let Move::Reveal { revealed, .. } = m {
133	                assert!(revealed.is_none(), "Reveal payload must be stripped in view");
134	            }
135	        }
136	    }
137	
138	    #[test]
139	    fn fresh_xiangqi_view_has_neither_side_in_check() {
140	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
141	        let red = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
142	        let black = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
143	        assert!(!red.in_check, "starting xiangqi: red not in check");
144	        assert!(!black.in_check, "starting xiangqi: black not in check");
145	    }
146	
147	    #[test]
148	    fn xiangqi_in_check_view_flags_observer() {
149	        // Three-chariot mating net (same as tests/fixtures/xiangqi/three-chariot-mate.pos):
150	        // Red rooks on d8/e8/f8, Black general on e9, Red general on e0.
151	        // Black is in check; red is not.
152	        let pos = "variant: xiangqi\nside_to_move: black\n\nboard:\n  . . . . k . . . .\n  . . . R R R . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . K . . . .\n";
153	        let state = GameState::from_pos_text(pos).expect("parse pos");
154	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
155	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
156	        assert!(black_view.in_check, "black observer must see in_check = true");
157	        assert!(!red_view.in_check, "red observer must see in_check = false");
158	    }
159	
160	    #[test]
161	    fn banqi_view_never_flags_in_check() {
162	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 13));
163	        for side in [Side::RED, Side::BLACK] {
164	            let view = PlayerView::project(&state, side);
165	            assert!(!view.in_check, "banqi has no general; in_check must be false");
166	        }
167	    }
168	
169	    #[test]
170	    fn no_hidden_identity_in_serialized_view() {
171	        // The smoke-level version of the proptest in tests/view_projection.rs.
172	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 42));
173	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
174	        let json = serde_json::to_string(&view).unwrap();
175	        // None of the piece-kind names should appear in any cell — they're all hidden.
176	        // (Empty cells trivially never contain a kind; we just confirm the doc-level
177	        // invariant.)
178	        for kind_name in ["General", "Advisor", "Elephant", "Chariot", "Horse", "Cannon", "Soldier"]
179	        {
180	            assert!(
181	                !json.contains(kind_name),
182	                "fresh banqi view must not leak any piece kind ({kind_name})"
183	            );
184	        }
185	    }
186	}
187

```text
30	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
31	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
32	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
33	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
34	    pub strict: bool,
35	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CHAIN` auto-implies `CHAIN_CAPTURE`
36	    /// after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
37	    pub house: HouseRules,
38	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
39	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
40	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
41	}
42	
43	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
44	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
45	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
46	///
47	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
48	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
49	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
50	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
51	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
52	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
53	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
54	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
55	    LocalRulesParams { strict, house: house::normalize(house), seed }
56	}
57	
58	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
59	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
60	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
61	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
62	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
63	    match variant {
64	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
65	            if params.strict {
66	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
67	            }
68	        }
69	        Variant::Banqi => {
70	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
71	            if !csv.is_empty() {
72	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
73	            }
74	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
75	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
76	            }
77	        }
78	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
79	    }
80	    parts.join("&")
81	}
82	
83	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
84	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
85	/// non-empty.
86	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
87	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
88	    if q.is_empty() {
89	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
90	    } else {
91	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
96	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
97	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
98	    match variant {
99	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
100	            if params.strict {
101	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
102	            } else {
103	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
104	            }
105	        }
106	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
107	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
108	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
109	        },
110	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
111	    }
112	}
113	
114	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
115	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
116	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
117	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
118	            out.insert(flag);
119	        }
120	    }
121	    out
122	}
123	
124	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
125	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
126	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
127	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
128	            parts.push(tok);
129	        }
130	    }
131	    parts.join(",")
132	}
133	
134	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
135	    HOUSE_TOKENS.iter().find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok)).map(|(f, _)| *f)
136	}
137	
138	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
139	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
140	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
141	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
142	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
143	        _ => None,
144	    }
145	}
146	
147	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
148	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
149	    (HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN, "dark"),
150	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
151	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
152	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
153	];
154	
155	#[cfg(test)]
156	mod tests {
157	    use super::*;
158	
159	    fn from_pairs<'a>(pairs: &'a [(&'a str, &'a str)]) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String> + 'a {
160	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
161	    }
162	
163	    #[test]
164	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
165	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
166	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
167	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
168	    }
169	
170	    #[test]
171	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
172	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
173	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());
174	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("XIANGQI").is_none());
175	    }
176	
177	    #[test]
178	    fn slug_round_trip() {
179	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
180	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
181	        }
182	    }
183	
184	    #[test]
185	    fn xiangqi_default_is_casual() {
186	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
187	        assert!(!p.strict);
188	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
189	        assert!(r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
190	    }
191	
192	    #[test]
193	    fn xiangqi_strict_query_yields_strict_ruleset() {
194	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("strict", "1")]));
195	        assert!(p.strict);
196	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
197	        assert!(!r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
198	    }
199	
200	    #[test]
201	    fn banqi_house_csv_round_trips() {
202	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "chain,rush"), ("seed", "42")]));
203	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
204	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
205	        assert_eq!(p.seed, Some(42));
206	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Banqi, &p);
207	        assert!(q.contains("house=chain,rush"));
208	        assert!(q.contains("seed=42"));
209	    }
210	
211	    #[test]
212	    fn banqi_dark_chain_normalizes_to_include_chain() {
213	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark")]));
214	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN));
215	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
216	    }
217	
218	    #[test]
219	    fn banqi_preset_taiwan_picks_chain_and_rush() {
220	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan")]));
221	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
222	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
223	    }
224	
225	    #[test]
226	    fn banqi_house_overrides_preset_when_both_set() {
227	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan"), ("house", "")]));
228	        // Empty house= still wins → no flags.
229	        assert!(p.house.is_empty());

68	#[component]
69	fn BanqiCard() -> impl IntoView {
70	    let chain = create_rw_signal(false);
71	    let dark = create_rw_signal(false);
72	    let rush = create_rw_signal(false);
73	    let seed_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
74	
75	    let house = move || {
76	        let mut h = HouseRules::empty();
77	        if chain.get() {
78	            h.insert(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE);
79	        }
80	        if dark.get() {
81	            h.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN);
82	        }
83	        if rush.get() {
84	            h.insert(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
85	        }
86	        h
87	    };
88	    let apply_preset = move |preset: HouseRules| {
89	        chain.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
90	        dark.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN));
91	        rush.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
92	    };
93	    let href = move || {
94	        let seed = seed_text.with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok());
95	        let params = LocalRulesParams { strict: false, house: house(), seed };
96	        build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &params)
97	    };
98	
99	    view! {
100	        <div class="variant-card variant-card--form">
101	            <h2>"Banqi 暗棋"</h2>
102	            <p>"Hidden-piece variant on a 4×8 board. Whoever flips first plays that color."</p>
103	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
104	                <legend>"Preset"</legend>
105	                <div class="preset-row">
106	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
107	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_PURIST)>"Purist"</button>
108	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
109	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_TAIWAN)>"Taiwan"</button>
110	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
111	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE)>"Aggressive"</button>
112	                </div>
113	            </fieldset>
114	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
115	                <legend>"House rules"</legend>
116	                <label class="check-row">
117	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || chain.get()
118	                        on:change=move |ev| chain.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
119	                    <span>"連吃 — chain captures along a line"</span>
120	                </label>
121	                <label class="check-row">
122	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || dark.get()
123	                        on:change=move |ev| dark.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
124	                    <span>"暗連 — chain through face-down squares (implies 連吃)"</span>
125	                </label>
126	                <label class="check-row">
127	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || rush.get()
128	                        on:change=move |ev| rush.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
129	                    <span>"車衝 — chariot rays the full board on a capture"</span>
130	                </label>
131	                <p class="hint">
132	                    "馬斜 / 炮快移 are accepted by the engine but not yet wired into move-gen "
133	                    "(see "<code>"TODO.md"</code>")."
134	                </p>
135	            </fieldset>
136	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
137	                <legend>"Seed (optional)"</legend>
138	                <input
139	                    type="text"
140	                    inputmode="numeric"
141	                    placeholder="leave blank for random"
142	                    class="text-input"
143	                    prop:value=move || seed_text.get()
144	                    on:input=move |ev| seed_text.set(event_target_value(&ev))
145	                />
146	                <p class="hint">"Same seed = same shuffle, useful for puzzles or rematches."</p>
147	            </fieldset>
148	            <A href=href class="btn btn-primary card-cta">"Start Banqi →"</A>
149	        </div>
150	    }
151	}

388	fn parse_house_list(s: &str) -> Result<HouseRules, CoreError> {
389	    let mut h = HouseRules::empty();
390	    if s.is_empty() {
391	        return Ok(h);
392	    }
393	    for tok in s.split(',') {
394	        h |= match tok.trim() {
395	            "chain" | "chain-capture" => HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE,
396	            "dark-chain" | "dark" => HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN,
397	            "rush" | "chariot-rush" => HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
398	            "horse-diagonal" | "diag" => HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL,
399	            "cannon-fast" | "fast-cannon" => HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE,
400	            other => return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(format!("unknown house rule: {other}"))),
401	        };
402	    }
403	    Ok(crate::rules::house::normalize(h))
404	}

 `./crates/chess-core/src/notation/iccs.rs`

```text
1	//! ICCS notation: file `a..i` × rank `0..9` → `h2e2` style.
2	//!
3	//! Encoder is straightforward; decoder takes a state because string-form
4	//! moves are ambiguous between Step/Capture/CannonJump (the geometry is
5	//! identical from outside) — we resolve by intersecting with `legal_moves`.
6	
7	use crate::board::Board;
8	use crate::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
9	use crate::error::CoreError;
10	use crate::moves::Move;
11	use crate::state::GameState;
12	use crate::view::PlayerView;
13	
14	/// Encode a single square as `<file><rank>` e.g. `h2`.
15	pub fn encode_square(board: &Board, sq: Square) -> String {
16	    let (f, r) = board.file_rank(sq);
17	    format!("{}{}", file_char(f.0), r.0)
18	}
19	
20	fn file_char(f: u8) -> char {
21	    (b'a' + f) as char
22	}
23	
24	/// Parse a `<file><rank>` square string (e.g. `h2`) against `board`'s
25	/// dimensions. Public so live-preview hooks in clients can reuse it without
26	/// duplicating bounds checks.
27	pub fn parse_square_str(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Option<Square> {
28	    parse_square(board, s).ok()
29	}
30	
31	fn parse_square(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError> {
32	    let mut chars = s.chars();
33	    let f = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
34	    let r = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
35	    if chars.next().is_some() {
36	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
37	    }
38	    if !f.is_ascii_lowercase() {
39	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
40	    }
41	    let file_idx = (f as u8) - b'a';
42	    let rank_idx = r.to_digit(10).ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))? as u8;
43	    if file_idx >= board.width() || rank_idx >= board.height() {
44	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(format!("{s} out of board bounds")));
45	    }
46	    Ok(board.sq(File(file_idx), Rank(rank_idx)))
47	}
48	
49	/// Encode a move as ICCS. `Reveal` becomes `flip <sq>`. `ChainCapture`
50	/// chains squares with `x` separators.
51	pub fn encode_move(board: &Board, m: &Move) -> String {
52	    match m {
53	        Move::Reveal { at, .. } => format!("flip {}", encode_square(board, *at)),
54	        Move::Step { from, to } => {
55	            format!("{}{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
56	        }
57	        Move::Capture { from, to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { from, to, .. } => {
58	            format!("{}x{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
59	        }
60	        Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
61	            let mut s = encode_square(board, *from);
62	            for hop in path {
63	                s.push('x');
64	                s.push_str(&encode_square(board, hop.to));
65	            }
66	            s
67	        }
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	/// Decode a move from ICCS, resolving Step/Capture ambiguity by matching
72	/// against the current legal-move list. Forms accepted:
73	/// - `h2e2`               — step
74	/// - `h2xh9`              — capture (single)
75	/// - `a3xb3xc3`           — chain capture (any number of x-separated squares)
76	/// - `flip a3` / `flip a3` — reveal
77	pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
78	    let legal = state.legal_moves();
79	    decode_move_with(&state.board, &legal, input)
80	}
81	
82	/// Same decoder, but resolves against a [`PlayerView`] — useful from
83	/// network clients that only hold the projected view, not the authoritative
84	/// `GameState`. Builds an empty board of the view's shape for square parsing
85	/// (notation only consults width/height) and matches against `view.legal_moves`.
86	pub fn decode_move_from_view(view: &PlayerView, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
87	    let board = Board::new(view.shape);
88	    decode_move_with(&board, &view.legal_moves, input)
89	}
90	
91	fn decode_move_with(board: &Board, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
92	    let trimmed = input.trim();
93	    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("flip ") {
94	        let at = parse_square(board, rest.trim())?;
95	        return Ok(Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None });
96	    }
97	
98	    // Split into squares: handle both 'h2e2' (no separator) and 'h2xe2'.
99	    let parts: Vec<Square> = if trimmed.contains('x') {
100	        trimmed.split('x').map(|p| parse_square(board, p.trim())).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?
101	    } else if trimmed.len() == 4 {
102	        vec![parse_square(board, &trimmed[..2])?, parse_square(board, &trimmed[2..])?]
103	    } else {
104	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
105	    };
106	
107	    if parts.len() < 2 {
108	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
109	    }
110	
111	    let from = parts[0];
112	
113	    if parts.len() == 2 {
114	        // Step / Capture / CannonJump — find unique match by (from, to).
115	        let to = parts[1];
116	        let candidates: Vec<&Move> = legal
117	            .iter()
118	            .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(to))
119	            .collect();
120	        match candidates.as_slice() {
121	            [one] => Ok((*one).clone()),
122	            [] => Err(CoreError::Illegal("no legal move matches notation")),
123	            _multi => {
124	                // Multiple matches (e.g. Step vs ChainCapture with len 2). Prefer
125	                // the simplest: Step > Capture > CannonJump > ChainCapture.
126	                let pick = candidates
127	                    .iter()
128	                    .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. }))
129	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { .. })))
130	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::CannonJump { .. })))
131	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })));
132	                pick.map(|m| (*m).clone()).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("ambiguous"))
133	            }
134	        }
135	    } else {
136	        // ChainCapture — find legal move whose path matches.
137	        let chain_squares = &parts[1..];
138	        let candidate = legal.iter().find(|m| match m {
139	            Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
140	                *f == from
141	                    && path.len() == chain_squares.len()
142	                    && path.iter().zip(chain_squares.iter()).all(|(h, sq)| h.to == *sq)
143	            }
144	            _ => false,
145	        });
146	        candidate.cloned().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no chain capture matches notation"))
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	#[cfg(test)]
151	mod tests {
152	    use super::*;
153	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
154	
155	    #[test]
156	    fn round_trip_simple_step() {
157	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
158	        // Pick a legal Step and round-trip it.
159	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
160	        let s = encode_move(&state.board, &m);
161	        let decoded = decode_move(&state, &s).unwrap();
162	        assert_eq!(m, decoded);
163	    }
164	
165	    #[test]
166	    fn parse_h2e2_in_xiangqi() {
167	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
168	        // h2 is a Red cannon. h2e2 moves it 6 squares west — the standard 炮二平五 (well, 炮 to e2).
169	        let m = decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap();
170	        if let Move::Step { from, to } = m {
171	            let (f1, r1) = state.board.file_rank(from);
172	            let (f2, r2) = state.board.file_rank(to);
173	            assert_eq!((f1.0, r1.0, f2.0, r2.0), (7, 2, 4, 2));
174	        } else {
175	            panic!("expected Step, got {m:?}");
176	        }
177	    }
178	
179	    #[test]
180	    fn flip_decodes_to_reveal() {
181	        let state = GameState::new(crate::rules::RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
182	            crate::rules::HouseRules::empty(),
183	            3,
184	        ));
185	        let m = decode_move(&state, "flip a0").unwrap();
186	        assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
187	    }
188	
189	    #[test]
190	    fn bad_notation_rejected() {
191	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
192	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "").is_err());
193	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "zz").is_err());
194	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "z9z9").is_err());
195	    }
196	
197	    #[test]
198	    fn decode_from_view_matches_state() {
199	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
200	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
201	        assert_eq!(
202	            decode_move_from_view(&view, "h2e2").unwrap(),
203	            decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap()
204	        );
205	    }
206	
207	    #[test]
208	    fn decode_from_view_flip_in_banqi() {
209	        let state = GameState::new(crate::rules::RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
210	            crate::rules::HouseRules::empty(),
211	            3,
212	        ));
213	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
214	        let m = decode_move_from_view(&view, "flip a0").unwrap();
215	        assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
216	    }
217	}
218

```rs
//! After the first flip locks `side_assignment`, banqi move-gen must emit
//! moves for the piece-color the seat actually controls — not the seat name.

use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::Side;
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_core::state::GameState;

/// Find a banqi seed where RED's first reveal happens to expose a Black
/// piece. We deterministically scan seeds; with 32 cells / 2 colors the
/// first available reveal is Black with high frequency.
fn seed_where_first_reveal_is_black() -> u64 {
    for seed in 0u64..256 {
        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), seed));
        // Pick the FIRST face-down piece by iteration order and check its
        // hidden color (we peek at the board rather than calling make_move
        // so we can pick a deterministic seed without mutating state).
        let first_sq = state.board.squares().find(|sq| {
            state.board.get(*sq).map(|p| !p.revealed).unwrap_or(false)
        });
        let Some(sq) = first_sq else { continue };
        let pos = state.board.get(sq).unwrap();
        if pos.piece.side == Side::BLACK {
            // Validate that we can reveal this square as RED's first move.
            let m = Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None };
            if state.make_move(&m).is_ok() {
                return seed;
            }
        }
    }
    panic!("no seed found where RED's first reveal is Black");
}

#[test]
fn red_seat_revealing_black_plays_black_color() {
    let seed = seed_where_first_reveal_is_black();
    let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), seed));
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::RED, "banqi always starts with RED seat");

    // Locate the first face-down square (matches the seed-search above).
    let first_sq = state
        .board
        .squares()
        .find(|sq| state.board.get(*sq).map(|p| !p.revealed).unwrap_or(false))
        .unwrap();
    let revealed_color = state.board.get(first_sq).unwrap().piece.side;
    assert_eq!(revealed_color, Side::BLACK, "seed selected for first-flip-Black");

    // RED reveals a Black piece — RED-seat now plays Black.
    state.make_move(&Move::Reveal { at: first_sq, revealed: None }).unwrap();

    let mapping = state
        .side_assignment
        .as_ref()
        .expect("side_assignment must lock after first flip")
        .mapping
        .clone();
    assert_eq!(mapping[Side::RED.0 as usize], Side::BLACK, "RED-seat plays Black");
    assert_eq!(mapping[Side::BLACK.0 as usize], Side::RED, "BLACK-seat plays Red");

    // Now turn advances to BLACK-seat (who plays Red color). `current_color()`
    // must reflect Red, not Black.
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn advances to BLACK seat");
    assert_eq!(
        state.current_color(),
        Side::RED,
        "BLACK-seat plays Red color after first flip"
    );

    // Move-gen must not offer moves for Black pieces; only Red pieces +
    // remaining reveals. (The seat is BLACK, but the color it plays is RED.)
    let moves = state.legal_moves();
    let any_non_reveal_for_black = moves.iter().any(|m| {
        if let Move::Reveal { .. } = m {
            return false;
        }
        // Look up the piece at the move origin and confirm it's RED-colored
        // (i.e. the seat's controlled color).
        let origin = m.origin_square();
        match state.board.get(origin) {
            Some(p) if p.revealed => p.piece.side == Side::RED,
            _ => false,
        }
    });
    let any_non_reveal_for_red = moves.iter().any(|m| {
        if let Move::Reveal { .. } = m {
            return false;
        }
        let origin = m.origin_square();
        match state.board.get(origin) {
            Some(p) if p.revealed => p.piece.side == Side::BLACK,
            _ => false,
        }
    });
    // BLACK-seat plays RED color → only Red-piece moves should appear.
    // (At this point only one piece is revealed — the Black one we flipped.
    // BLACK can't move it. So only Reveal moves are legal. The presence/absence
    // checks below validate the *direction* of the filter even when no movable
    // piece exists yet.)
    assert!(
        !any_non_reveal_for_red,
        "BLACK-seat (playing Red) must not see moves on Black-colored pieces"
    );
    let _ = any_non_reveal_for_black; // either path is fine — no Red is revealed yet
}

#[test]
fn before_first_flip_current_color_falls_back_to_seat() {
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    assert!(state.side_assignment.is_none());
    assert_eq!(state.current_color(), state.side_to_move);
}

```

```text
1	//! Banqi house-rule scenario tests.
2	//! Constructs hand-crafted positions to exercise CHAIN_CAPTURE and CHARIOT_RUSH.
3	
4	use chess_core::board::Board;
5	use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
6	use chess_core::moves::Move;
7	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
8	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
9	use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
10	use smallvec::smallvec;
11	
12	fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
13	    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
14	    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
15	    for sq in squares {
16	        state.board.set(sq, None);
17	    }
18	    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
19	    state
20	}
21	
22	fn place(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
23	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
24	}
25	
26	#[test]
27	fn chain_capture_three_in_a_row() {
28	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
29	    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
30	    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
31	    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
32	    let s3 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
33	    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
34	    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
35	    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
36	    place(&mut state.board, s3, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
37	
38	    let chains: Vec<Move> = state
39	        .legal_moves()
40	        .into_iter()
41	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. }))
42	        .collect();
43	
44	    // Should have chains of length 2 and 3.
45	    let lengths: Vec<usize> = chains
46	        .iter()
47	        .filter_map(|m| match m {
48	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => Some(path.len()),
49	            _ => None,
50	        })
51	        .collect();
52	    assert!(lengths.contains(&2), "expected length-2 chain");
53	    assert!(lengths.contains(&3), "expected length-3 chain");
54	}
55	
56	#[test]
57	fn chain_capture_blocked_by_outranked_target() {
58	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
59	    // Red soldier at a0, black soldier at a1 (capturable, equal rank),
60	    // black general at a2 (NOT capturable... wait, soldier beats general).
61	    // Use elephant instead — soldier(0) cannot capture elephant(4).
62	    let s = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
63	    let t1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
64	    let t2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
65	    place(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
66	    place(&mut state.board, t1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
67	    place(&mut state.board, t2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);
68	
69	    let chains: Vec<Move> = state
70	        .legal_moves()
71	        .into_iter()
72	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. }))
73	        .collect();
74	    // The chain stops at the elephant — soldier can't take it.
75	    assert!(chains.is_empty(), "chain should not extend past outranked target; got {chains:?}");
76	}
77	
78	#[test]
79	fn chariot_rush_emits_long_slides_along_file() {
80	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, 0));
81	    let c = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
82	    place(&mut state.board, c, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
83	    let moves = state.legal_moves();
84	    // Without a blocker, chariot can reach any of 7 squares N + 3 squares E
85	    // = 10 destinations (1-step also counted). Expect at least 8 Step moves.
86	    let steps = moves.iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).count();
87	    assert!(steps >= 8, "chariot rush should produce many slides; got {steps}");
88	}
89	
90	#[test]
91	fn chain_capture_round_trip_via_make_unmake() {
92	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
93	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
94	    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
95	    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
96	    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
97	    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
98	    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
99	
100	    let snapshot = state.clone();
101	    let chain = state
102	        .legal_moves()
103	        .into_iter()
104	        .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } if path.len() == 2))
105	        .expect("chain should be available");
106	
107	    state.make_move(&chain).unwrap();
108	    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
109	    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
110	    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, snapshot.side_to_move);
111	}
112
```
</details></tool-use>

```rs
//! Banqi `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE) move-gen + apply/unapply tests.
//! Covers both the probe variant (default) and the trade variant
//! (DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE).

use chess_core::board::Board;
use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
use smallvec::smallvec;

fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
    for sq in squares {
        state.board.set(sq, None);
    }
    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
    state
}

fn place_revealed(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

fn place_hidden(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_emitted_when_flag_on_target_face_down() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let dark: Vec<Move> = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { .. }))
        .collect();
    assert!(!dark.is_empty(), "expected at least one DarkCapture move");
    assert!(dark.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == target)));
}

#[test]
fn no_dark_capture_when_flag_off() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let dark = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { .. }))
        .count();
    assert_eq!(dark, 0, "no DarkCapture moves without the flag");
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_succeeds_when_attacker_outranks() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: h, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    // Horse at target, h is empty.
    assert!(state.board.get(h).is_none(), "attacker moved");
    let landed = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
    assert!(landed.revealed);
    assert_eq!(landed.piece.kind, PieceKind::Horse);
    assert_eq!(landed.piece.side, Side::RED);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_probe_keeps_attacker_when_outranked() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    // Probe: attacker stays put; target is now revealed.
    let attacker = state.board.get(s).unwrap();
    assert!(attacker.revealed);
    assert_eq!(attacker.piece.kind, PieceKind::Soldier);
    let revealed_target = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
    assert!(revealed_target.revealed, "target must be revealed after probe");
    assert_eq!(revealed_target.piece.kind, PieceKind::Elephant);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_trade_kills_attacker_when_outranked() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
        HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
        0,
    ));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    // Trade: attacker dies; target stays revealed in place.
    assert!(state.board.get(s).is_none(), "trade variant: attacker must be removed");
    let target_pos = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
    assert!(target_pos.revealed);
    assert_eq!(target_pos.piece.kind, PieceKind::Elephant);
    assert_eq!(target_pos.piece.side, Side::BLACK);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_make_unmake_round_trip_capture_path() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    let snapshot = state.clone();

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: h, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, snapshot.side_to_move);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_make_unmake_round_trip_probe_path() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);
    let snapshot = state.clone();

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_make_unmake_round_trip_trade_path() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
        HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
        0,
    ));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);
    let snapshot = state.clone();

    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
}

#[test]
fn chariot_rush_emits_dark_capture_against_hidden_blocker_with_gap() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
        HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE,
        0,
    ));
    let c = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, c, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let dark = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } if *from == c && *to == target));
    assert!(dark.is_some(), "車衝暗吃 should be emitted across an empty gap");
}

```rs
//! 馬斜 (HORSE_DIAGONAL): horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal
//! captures ignore rank.

use chess_core::board::Board;
use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
use smallvec::smallvec;

fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
    for sq in squares {
        state.board.set(sq, None);
    }
    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
    state
}

fn place_revealed(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

#[test]
fn horse_emits_diagonal_steps_only_with_flag() {
    // Without HORSE_DIAGONAL: horse at (1,1) sees only 4 orthogonal Steps.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    let steps_no_flag = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, .. } if *from == h))
        .count();
    assert_eq!(steps_no_flag, 4);

    // With HORSE_DIAGONAL: 4 ortho + 4 diag = 8 Steps.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    let steps_with_flag = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, .. } if *from == h))
        .count();
    assert_eq!(steps_with_flag, 8, "horse with diagonal should have 8 step moves");
}

#[test]
fn horse_diagonal_captures_higher_rank() {
    // Horse rank = 2, General rank = 6. Without rank-ignore, horse cannot
    // capture General. With HORSE_DIAGONAL on a diagonal target, it can.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let g_sq = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, g_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General);

    let captures: Vec<_> = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter_map(|m| match m {
            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } if from == h && to == g_sq => {
                Some(captured)
            }
            _ => None,
        })
        .collect();
    assert!(
        !captures.is_empty(),
        "馬斜 should let horse capture diagonally adjacent general (rank ignored)"
    );
    assert_eq!(captures[0].kind, PieceKind::General);
}

#[test]
fn horse_orthogonal_captures_still_follow_rank_rules() {
    // Without HORSE_DIAGONAL, horse vs General orthogonal: horse(2) < general(6),
    // so no capture. Confirm we didn't accidentally rank-ignore orthogonal too.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let g_sq = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, g_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General);

    let captures = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == g_sq))
        .count();
    assert_eq!(captures, 0, "orthogonal horse-vs-general should still be rank-blocked");
}

#[test]
fn horse_diagonal_blocked_by_own_piece() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let mate = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, mate, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let move_to_mate = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, to } | Move::Capture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == mate))
        .count();
    assert_eq!(move_to_mate, 0, "own piece blocks diagonal");
}

#[test]
fn horse_diagonal_dark_capture_emitted_with_both_flags() {
    let rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
        HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE,
        0,
    );
    let mut state = empty_banqi(rules);
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    state.board.set(
        target,
        Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))),
    );

    let dark = state
        .legal_moves()
        .into_iter()
        .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == target));
    assert!(dark.is_some(), "diagonal hidden target should produce DarkCapture");
}

1076	
1077	    fn handle_select_or_commit(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
1078	        if !matches!(g.state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
1079	            g.last_msg =
1080	                Some("Game over. Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.".into());
1081	            return;
1082	        }
1083	        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
1084	        let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(g.cursor.0, g.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
1085	            g.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
1086	            return;
1087	        };
1088	
1089	        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, g.state.side_to_move);
1090	
1091	        match g.selected {
1092	            None => {
1093	                // Try to select a piece. Allowed if there's a legal move from this square.
1094	                let any = view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
1095	                if any {
1096	                    g.selected = Some(sq);
1097	                    g.last_msg = None;
1098	                } else {
1099	                    // Maybe it's a banqi hidden cell — suggest 'f' to flip.
1100	                    match g.state.board.get(sq) {
1101	                        Some(p) if !p.revealed => {
1102	                            g.last_msg = Some("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into());
1103	                        }
1104	                        _ => g.last_msg = Some("No legal move from that square.".into()),
1105	                    }
1106	                }
1107	            }
1108	            Some(from) if from == sq => {
1109	                g.selected = None;
1110	                g.last_msg = None;
1111	            }
1112	            Some(from) => {
1113	                // Find a legal move from `from` to `sq`. Prefer Capture / CannonJump
1114	                // over a chain ending at sq (for banqi); take the first match.
1115	                let candidate = view
1116	                    .legal_moves
1117	                    .iter()
1118	                    .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
1119	                    .cloned();
1120	                match candidate {
1121	                    Some(m) => Self::apply_move(g, m),
1122	                    None => {
1123	                        g.last_msg = Some("Illegal move.".into());
1124	                        g.selected = None;
1125	                    }
1126	                }
1127	            }
1128	        }
1129	    }
1130	
1131	    fn apply_move(g: &mut GameView, m: Move) {
1132	        match g.state.make_move(&m) {
1133	            Ok(()) => {
1134	                g.state.refresh_status();
1135	                g.selected = None;
1136	                g.last_msg = Some(format!(
1137	                    "Played: {}",
1138	                    chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_move(&g.state.board, &m)
1139	                ));
1140	            }
1141	            Err(e) => {
1142	                g.last_msg = Some(format!("Engine rejected move: {e}"));
1143	                g.selected = None;
1144	            }
1145	        }
1146	    }
1147	
1148	    fn dispatch_net(&mut self, action: Action) {
1149	        let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen else {
1150	            return;
1151	        };
1152	        // Pre-welcome: no view yet, nothing to dispatch on.
1153	        let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
1154	            return;
1155	        };
1156	        let shape = view.shape;
1157	        let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1158	        let role = n.role.unwrap_or(NetRole::Player(Side::RED));
1159	        let observer = role.observer();
1160	        match action {
1161	            Action::CursorUp if n.cursor.0 > 0 => {
1162	                n.cursor.0 -= 1;
1163	            }
1164	            Action::CursorDown if n.cursor.0 + 1 < rows => {
1165	                n.cursor.0 += 1;
1166	            }
1167	            Action::CursorLeft if n.cursor.1 > 0 => {
1168	                n.cursor.1 -= 1;
1169	            }
1170	            Action::CursorRight if n.cursor.1 + 1 < cols => {
1171	                n.cursor.1 += 1;
1172	            }
1173	            Action::Cancel => {
1174	                n.selected = None;
1175	                n.last_msg = None;
1176	            }
1177	            Action::SelectOrCommit => {
1178	                if role.is_spectator() {
1179	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
1180	                    return;
1181	                }
1182	                let outcome = compute_select_outcome(n, observer);
1183	                apply_select_outcome(n, outcome);
1184	            }
1185	            Action::Flip => {
1186	                if role.is_spectator() {
1187	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
1188	                    return;
1189	                }
1190	                if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
1191	                    n.last_msg = Some("Game over.".into());
1192	                    return;
1193	                }
1194	                if view.side_to_move != observer {
1195	                    n.last_msg = Some("Not your turn.".into());
1196	                    return;
1197	                }
1198	                let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape)

83	pub struct GameView {
84	    pub state: GameState,
85	    pub cursor: (u8, u8),
86	    pub selected: Option<Square>,
87	    pub last_msg: Option<String>,
88	    /// `Some(state)` while the user is typing a coordinate move (`:` or `m`).
89	    /// Mutually exclusive with `chat_input` in Net mode.
90	    pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
91	}
92	
93	/// Per-prompt state for coord-input mode (`:` instant or `m` live preview).
94	/// Lives on both `GameView` and `NetView`; mirrored shape so the dispatchers
95	/// can share helpers.
96	pub struct CoordInputState {
97	    pub kind: CoordKind,
98	    pub buf: String,
99	    /// Live mode only: `(cursor, selected)` snapshot taken on entry, restored
100	    /// on Esc. Always `None` for `Instant` (instant mode never touches them).
101	    pub snapshot: Option<((u8, u8), Option<Square>)>,
102	}
103	
104	/// Role assigned by the server: a seated player (with a side) or a
105	/// read-only spectator. `None` on `NetView` until the welcome arrives.
106	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
107	pub enum NetRole {
108	    Player(Side),
109	    Spectator,
110	}
111	
112	impl NetRole {
113	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
114	        matches!(self, NetRole::Player(_))
115	    }
116	
117	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
118	        matches!(self, NetRole::Spectator)
119	    }
120	
121	    /// Side from whose POV the board is rendered. Spectators view from
122	    /// Red's perspective (matches what chess-net's broadcast_update
123	    /// projects for spectator updates).
124	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
125	        match self {
126	            NetRole::Player(s) => s,
127	            NetRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
128	        }
129	    }
130	}
131	
132	pub struct NetView {
133	    pub client: NetClient,
134	    pub url: String,
135	    pub last_view: Option<PlayerView>,
136	    pub rules: Option<RuleSet>,
137	    /// Server-assigned role. `None` until `Hello` / `Spectating` arrives.
138	    pub role: Option<NetRole>,
139	    pub cursor: (u8, u8),
140	    pub selected: Option<Square>,
141	    pub last_msg: Option<String>,
142	    /// True between Connected and Disconnected events. Used by the sidebar.
143	    pub connected: bool,
144	    /// In-room chat ring buffer (cap [`CHAT_HISTORY_CAP`]). Mirrors the
145	    /// server's per-room buffer so the sidebar can replay history.
146	    pub chat: VecDeque<ChatLine>,
147	    /// `Some(buf)` while the user is typing a chat line (entered via 't').
148	    /// Players only — the chat-start action is a no-op for spectators.
149	    pub chat_input: Option<String>,
150	    /// `Some(state)` while the user is typing a coord move (`:` or `m`).
151	    /// Mutually exclusive with `chat_input`.
152	    pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
153	}
154	
155	/// Free-text "ws://host:port" prompt entered before the lobby.
156	pub struct HostPromptView {

158	    pub error: Option<String>,
159	}
160	
161	/// Live room browser. Reads from a separate `NetClient` connected to the
162	/// server's `/lobby` endpoint; joining a room spawns a fresh `NetClient` to
163	/// `/ws/<id>?password=…` and transitions to `Screen::Net`.
164	pub struct LobbyView {
165	    pub client: NetClient,
166	    /// Original `ws://host:port` (no path). The lobby ws is `host/lobby`;
167	    /// joining builds `host/ws/<id>` from the same prefix.

169	    pub rooms: Vec<RoomSummary>,
170	    pub cursor: usize,
171	    pub last_msg: Option<String>,
172	    pub connected: bool,
173	    /// When `Some`, the user picked a password-locked room and we're

175	    pub pending_join: Option<PendingJoin>,
176	}
177	
178	pub struct PendingJoin {
179	    pub room_id: String,
180	    pub password_buf: String,
181	    /// `true` when the user is joining as a spectator (watch flow); the

246	    /// resets the status without dropping the screen.

248	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/src/state.rs`

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable —
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::moves::Move;
6	use chess_core::piece::Side;
7	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
8	
9	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
10	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
11	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
12	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
13	pub enum ClientRole {
14	    Player(Side),
15	    Spectator,
16	}
17	
18	impl ClientRole {
19	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
20	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
24	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
25	    }
26	
27	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
28	        // Spectators render from RED's POV — matches what chess-net's
29	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
30	        match self {
31	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
32	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
33	        }
34	    }
35	}
36	
37	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
38	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
39	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
40	    if buf.len() > max {
41	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
42	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
47	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
48	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
49	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
50	}
51	
52	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
53	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
54	        return false;
55	    }
56	    match mv.to_square() {
57	        Some(t) => t == to,
58	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
59	    }
60	}
61	
62	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render dots).
63	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
64	    view.legal_moves
65	        .iter()
66	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
67	        .map(|m| match m.to_square() {
68	            Some(t) => t,
69	            None => match m {
70	                Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
71	                _ => unreachable!("Move::to_square() returned None for a non-Reveal move"),
72	            },
73	        })
74	        .collect()
75	}
76	
77	#[cfg(test)]
78	mod tests {
79	    use super::*;
80	    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
81	    use chess_core::state::GameState;
82	
83	    #[test]
84	    fn find_move_locates_xiangqi_step() {
85	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
86	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
87	        // Red soldier at file 0 rank 3 should have a step forward to rank 4.
88	        let from = Square(3 * 9); // rank 3, file 0
89	        let to = Square(4 * 9); // rank 4, file 0
90	        let mv = find_move(&view, from, to);
91	        assert!(matches!(mv, Some(Move::Step { .. })), "expected step from soldier, got {:?}", mv);
92	    }
93	
94	    #[test]
95	    fn legal_targets_for_chariot_in_corner() {
96	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
97	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
98	        // Red chariot at (file 0, rank 0) — Square(0). Should have several
99	        // legal destinations along its file (rank moves blocked by horse).
100	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(0));
101	        assert!(!targets.is_empty(), "chariot at corner must have legal moves");
102	    }
103	
104	    #[test]
105	    fn legal_targets_empty_for_non_piece_square() {
106	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
107	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
108	        // Square(5*9 + 4) — a river-ish empty square. Empty piece → no targets.
109	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(5 * 9 + 4));
110	        assert!(targets.is_empty());
111	    }
112	
113	    #[test]
114	    fn truncate_front_drops_oldest_when_over_cap() {
115	        let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
116	        truncate_front(&mut v, 3);
117	        assert_eq!(v, vec![3, 4, 5]);
118	    }
119	
120	    #[test]
121	    fn truncate_front_noop_when_under_cap() {
122	        let mut v = vec![1, 2];
123	        truncate_front(&mut v, 5);
124	        assert_eq!(v, vec![1, 2]);
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn client_role_observer_defaults_red_for_spectator() {
129	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK).observer(), Side::BLACK);
130	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Spectator.observer(), Side::RED);
131	        assert!(ClientRole::Spectator.is_spectator());
132	        assert!(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED).is_player());
133	    }
134	}
135
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 09:22:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./TODO.md`

```text
15	     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
16	     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
17	     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
18	     machine readability. -->
19	
20	## P1
21	
22	Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.
23	
24	- [ ] **[M] Three Kingdoms Banqi (三國暗棋) implementation** — Build BoardShape::ThreeKingdom mask + rules/three_kingdom.rs with 3-side capture rules. Architecture supports it (TurnOrder, Side, BoardShape variant exist); rules logic and home-zone layout need spec from canonical source. → [research](backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md)
25	- [ ] **[S] Wire DARK_CHAIN house rule** — Chain captures pass through face-down squares; auto-reveal as chain progresses. Requires changing ChainHop.captured to Option<Piece> and updating sanitize_for_observer for the network ABI. See docs/rules/banqi-house.md.
26	- [ ] **[S] Wire HORSE_DIAGONAL house rule** — Banqi horse moves like xiangqi horse (L-shape with 馬腿 leg block) when flag enabled. Reuse pattern from rules/xiangqi.rs::gen_horse.
27	- [ ] **[S] Wire CANNON_FAST_MOVE house rule** — Banqi cannon non-capturing slide goes any number of squares (capture still requires the one-piece jump). Reuse the ray walk from rules/banqi.rs::gen_chariot_rush.
28	- [ ] **[M] Threefold repetition draw detection** — Add Zobrist hashing on Board + (side_to_move) and a position-count map. Update on make_move/unmake_move. Triggers GameStatus::Drawn when count >= rules.draw_policy.repetition_threshold. See state/repetition.rs (currently a stub).
29	
30	## P2
31	
32	Worth doing, no rush.
33	
34	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: in-game rule editor (gear icon → modal)** — Picker-side form already encodes rules into the URL (`?strict=1`, `?house=chain,rush&seed=N`). Add a sidebar gear that opens a pre-filled modal reusing the picker components, navigates to the new query, and starts a fresh game. → [research](backlog/web-ingame-rules-modal.md)
35	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: WS auto-reconnect with backoff** — PR-1 surfaces "Disconnected — refresh" when the WS read pump closes. Add an exponential-backoff retry that reuses the URL+password and resets the `Hello` flow on success. → [research](backlog/web-ws-reconnect.md)
36	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: CJK/ASCII glyph toggle in UI** — `glyph::Style` is already plumbed; expose a sidebar toggle + persist in `localStorage`. Shares no logic with chess-tui's `--style` flag.
37	- [ ] **[M] Promote orient.rs / glyph.rs to a shared client crate** — Both `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs` and `clients/chess-web/src/orient.rs` (likewise `glyph.rs`) are byte-identical. Pull into `clients/chess-client-shared` once a third client appears or the copies first diverge. → [research](backlog/promote-client-shared.md)
38	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: Playwright E2E smoke** — Headless two-tab flow that creates a room, plays a few moves, resigns, and rematches. Cheaper than `wasm-bindgen-test` for full-app smoke. → [research](backlog/web-playwright.md)
39	- [ ] **[M] chess-net: mixed-variant rooms** — Today one server boots with one `--variant` and every auto-created room copies it. Move to per-room `RuleSet` (a chooser inside the `CreateRoom` form). Architecture supports it (`RoomState` already owns its `GameState`); blocks on UX decisions about a variant filter inside the lobby and what default to apply when joining via a URL with no variant hint.
40	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat moderation primitives** — Current chat is a friend-only space (50-line ring buffer, no rate limiting, no mute, no word filter). Add per-player rate limit (~1 msg/sec leaky bucket), client-side `ChatMute` toggle, and an opt-in `--chat-filter <list>` server flag for substring scrubbing. → [research](backlog/chess-net-chat-moderation.md)
41	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat system messages** — `ChatLine.from` is `Side` today so there's no in-channel narration when seats fill / leave / rematch resets. Widen the type to an enum with a `System` variant and emit join/leave/resign system lines. Wire-breaking → bumps protocol to v4. → [research](backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md)
42	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: spectator chat permission per room** — ADR-0006 picked players-only as the default; a streamer flow wants a per-room "spectators may chat" toggle (set on room creation, read by `process_chat`'s gate). Small protocol addition next to room password.
43	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: reconnect-as-same-seat** — Disconnecting frees the seat; the reconnect path currently rebooks at the next free side. Add a one-shot rejoin token (issued in `Hello`, presented as `?token=...` on reconnect) so a flaky link doesn't lose your color.
44	- [ ] **[S] chess-tui: chat scrollback (PgUp/PgDn)** — The chat pane shows the most recent N lines that fit the sidebar; older lines from the 50-line ring buffer are unreachable. Add scroll keys + a "↑ N more" indicator. Pure UI — no protocol change.
45	- [ ] **[S] chess-web: chat polish** — Emoji picker, markdown-lite (bold/italic/code), unread-message badge when the chat panel is scrolled up. Cheap UX wins; none touch the protocol.
46	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: lobby filter + sort** — `/` filter inside the lobby (by id substring or variant); sort by recency / seat count / locked-or-open. Defer until ≥10 concurrent rooms is a real workflow — for a handful of friends the unsorted live list works fine.
47	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: lobby push debouncing** — `notify_lobby` fires on every seat insertion / removal / status flip; back-to-back churn (rematch storms, reconnect retries) can produce a flurry of identical `Rooms` payloads. Coalesce within ≥250ms windows. Premature today; revisit if observable in the wild.
48	- [ ] **[S] chess-net: server-side admin / kick / signed token** — Close stuck rooms or force-disconnect a misbehaving client without restarting the whole server. Wants either an admin-only ws path (`/admin/ws?token=…`) or signed JSON tokens accepted by `/ws/<id>`. Folds into a future TLS / auth pass.
49	- [ ] **[S] WXF notation (相3進5 style)** — Chinese-language traditional xiangqi notation. Pattern lives in crates/chess-core/src/notation/wxf.rs (currently empty). ICCS already implemented; mirror the same encode/decode pair.
```
</details></tool-use>

Don't assume code that consumes `HouseRules` handles every flag — grep `rules/banqi.rs::generate` to confirm. The deferred ones are P1 in `TODO.md`.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 09:22:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/rules/banqi-house.md`

```text
1	# Banqi House Rules (家規)
2	
3	> **Status**: skeleton — formal spec to be filled in alongside implementation.
4	
5	Five independent toggles. Combine freely. Three named presets bundle common combos.
6	
7	## Toggles
8	
9	### `CHAIN_CAPTURE` — 連吃
10	
11	After a capture, if the capturing piece can immediately capture another enemy piece (in the same line of motion, by its normal capture rules), it **may** continue. The chain ends when:
12	
13	- the next square is empty (cannot capture),
14	- the next square holds a face-down piece (unless `DARK_CHAIN` is also enabled),
15	- the next square holds a friendly piece, or
16	- the player chooses to stop.
17	
18	The whole chain is one logical move (one history entry, one network message).
19	
20	### `DARK_CHAIN` — 暗連
21	
22	Modifies `CHAIN_CAPTURE`. The chain may continue **through face-down squares**. When the chain enters a face-down square, that piece is revealed atomically as part of the move; if the revealed piece is friendly or unbeatable, the chain stops there.
23	
24	Implies `CHAIN_CAPTURE`.
25	
26	### `CHARIOT_RUSH` — 車衝
27	
28	Chariot moves any number of empty squares in a line (xiangqi-style ray), and captures the first enemy piece on that line. Replaces base banqi's 1-step chariot move.
29	
30	### `HORSE_DIAGONAL` — 馬斜
31	
32	Horse moves like a xiangqi horse: L-shape with leg (馬腿) blocking. Replaces base banqi's 1-step horse move.
33	
34	### `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` — 炮快移
35	
36	Cannon may move any number of empty squares in a line for non-capturing moves (capture still requires the one-piece jump). Speeds up cannon repositioning without changing capture geometry.
37	
38	## Presets
39	
40	```rust
41	HouseRules::PRESET_PURIST     // empty: classic banqi
42	HouseRules::PRESET_TAIWAN     // CHAIN_CAPTURE | CHARIOT_RUSH
43	HouseRules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE // CHAIN_CAPTURE | DARK_CHAIN | CHARIOT_RUSH | HORSE_DIAGONAL
44	```
45	
46	## Composition Example
47	
48	```rust
49	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, HouseRules};
50	
51	let rules = RuleSet::banqi(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE | HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
52	let mut state = chess_core::state::GameState::new(rules);
53	```
54	
55	Each flag controls one branch in `rules/banqi.rs::generate`. No traits, no boxing.
56	
57	## References
58	
59	- <https://darkchess.funtown.com.tw/rules2.html>
60
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 09:23:58Z)**_

All 248 tests pass, fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, wasm32 builds. Done.

## Summary of what shipped

**Engine (`chess-core`)**
- `Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker }` — new variant; server fills both Option fields at apply-time. Outcome (capture / probe / trade) decided from rank vs `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` flag.
- Renamed `HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN` → `DARK_CAPTURE` (same bit so old snapshots load), added `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` for the kill-on-fail variant.
- `GameState::current_color()` — consults `side_assignment.mapping` so banqi move-gen filters by the actual piece-color the seat plays after the first flip. Fixes the bug where Red-seat-revealing-Black still played Red pieces.
- `HORSE_DIAGONAL` wired: 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures ignore rank.
- `gen_chariot_rush` also emits `DarkCapture` against hidden blockers (車衝暗吃).

**Notation / snapshot / CLI / URL**
- ICCS encodes `DarkCapture` as `<from>x?<to>` and decodes the same form.
- Snapshot emits `dark` + `dark-trade`; parse accepts `dark-chain` alias.
- `chess-tui`, `chess-cli`, `chess-net-server`, `chess-web` URL parser all accept `dark-trade`, `horse`, plus the legacy `dark-chain` alias.
- `chess-web` picker now has checkboxes for 暗吃 / 暗吃·搏命 / 馬斜.

**Tests** — 16 new tests:
- `banqi_first_flip_color.rs` (2): proves `current_color()` remaps after first-flip.
- `banqi_dark_capture.rs` (9): probe/trade/capture outcomes, make/unmake round-trips, 車衝暗吃.
- `banqi_horse_diagonal.rs` (5): diagonal moves emitted only with flag, rank-ignored captures, ortho still rank-bound, friendly blocker, dark-capture diagonal.

**Docs** — `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `TODO.md` updated.

**Deferred to Phase 2** (added to `TODO.md`):
- Chains-with-dark-hops (true 暗連 — `Move::ChainCapture` extended through hidden tiles).
- Chain-builder UI (incremental click-by-click chain construction). Today's UX: clicking the final hop commits the whole chain; clicking intermediate hops commits a shorter chain ending there. The dark-capture fix and the rest of the asks all work end-to-end as-is.

---
