_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:26:06Z)**_

Web bundle rebuilt. Reload `/local/banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse&seed=...` to verify.

## Summary of the chain-mode overhaul + the just-fixed regression

**What was wrong before**
- My first chain-fix did atomic `Move::ChainCapture` + UI chain_builder — it only worked for chains in a *single direction*, so 相 east-capturing 砲 then wanting to capture 卒 *south* still ended the turn.
- After the engine-driven chain-mode rewrite, a second bug surfaced: `Move::DarkCapture` resolving to **Probe** (revealed piece is your own colour) or **Trade** (your attacker dies) was wrongly setting `chain_lock` onto whatever piece happened to be at `to` after the reveal — that's the just-revealed friend, not the attacker. So the engine let the user move the freshly-flipped 帥 / 仕 in chain mode.

**What's fixed now**
- Engine state: `state.chain_lock: Option<Square>` + `Move::EndChain { at }`. After any chain-eligible capture the turn does NOT auto-advance; `legal_moves` filters to "captures from the locked square + EndChain"; the player ends the chain by clicking the locked piece (Esc in TUI).
- `compute_chain_lock_after` now consults `dark_capture_outcome` for `Move::DarkCapture` and only treats it as chain-eligible when it actually resolved to `Capture` (attacker landed at `to`). Probe and Trade outcomes correctly skip chain mode and advance the turn.
- New regression tests covering both bugs (`banqi_chain_mode.rs` — 11 tests, all passing): probe-against-own-piece, trade-with-attacker-death, perpendicular capture in chain (the original 相→砲→卒 scenario), make/unmake round-trips for both `Capture` and `EndChain`.

**UI changes**
- Removed the now-redundant UI-side `ChainBuilder` from chess-web (`local.rs`, `play.rs`, `state.rs`) and chess-tui (`GameView` / `NetView` / `SelectOutcome`). The single source of truth is `view.chain_lock` from the projected `PlayerView`.
- chess-web shows a "連吃 — 繼續吃 or [End chain]" banner under the board when `chain_lock` is set. The locked piece renders with a dashed orange ring (the highlight you saw in the latest screenshot).
- chess-tui: in chain mode pressing Enter on the locked square sends `Move::EndChain`; pressing Enter elsewhere either captures (if legal from the locked square) or shows a guidance message. Esc now ends the chain when chain mode is active (instead of clearing a phantom UI builder).
- Fixed the chess-web piece-selection check (`p.piece.side == v.side_to_move` → `legal_moves.iter().any(...)`) so banqi after a first-flip-to-opposite-colour properly lets the seat select its own pieces.

**Verification:** all 248 workspace tests pass, fmt & clippy `-D warnings` clean, wasm32 build clean, `trunk build --release` clean.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:30:39Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:30:41Z)**_

Right — that's the seat name leaking through to the user-facing label. After Player 1 (RED seat) reveals Black, `side_assignment` locks RED→Black and BLACK→Red. Turn advances to the BLACK *seat*, who plays the Red *colour*. The sidebar shows the seat name ("Black 黑") when it should show the piece-colour-to-move ("Red 紅").

Fix: expose `current_color` on `PlayerView` (the seat-mapped piece colour) and have all "Turn:" labels use that.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:34:42Z)**_

Bundle rebuilt. Refresh the page to see the corrected label.

## What changed

- `PlayerView` gains a `current_color: Side` field (protocol v5, `serde(default = "default_red_side")` so v4 deserialisers still work). The server fills it from `state.current_color()` — i.e. the seat-mapped piece colour after a banqi first-flip locks `side_assignment`.
- All "Turn:" / "to move" labels now read `current_color`, not `side_to_move`:
  - `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` (local pass-and-play)
  - `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` (online sidebar)
  - `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` (both local and net sidebars, two call sites)
- Seat-routing checks (`v.observer != v.side_to_move`, "is it MY turn?") still use `side_to_move` (the seat) — those need to compare seats, not piece colours.

## Expected behaviour now

Starting state: **Turn: Red 紅** (RED seat = Player 1 about to flip).

Player 1 flips a Black 車 → `side_assignment` locks RED→Black, BLACK→Red → turn advances to BLACK seat (= Player 2) → `current_color = Red` → sidebar reads **Turn: Red 紅**. Player 2's turn, playing the Red pieces — exactly what you described.

For xiangqi (no `side_assignment`) and banqi pre-first-flip, `current_color == side_to_move`, so the label is identical to before. All 251 workspace tests still pass.

---

```bash
git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(banqi): dark-capture, horse-diagonal, engine-driven 連吃 chain mode (protocol v5)

- 暗吃 (DARK_CAPTURE, renamed from DARK_CHAIN; bit unchanged) atomically
  reveals + captures a face-down piece. Three outcomes resolved at apply
  time: Capture (rank ok), Probe (rank fail, attacker stays), or Trade
  (rank fail + DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag → attacker dies). New
  Move::DarkCapture variant; chariot-rush also emits it across a gap.
- 馬斜 (HORSE_DIAGONAL) wired: 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal
  captures ignore rank.
- side_assignment is finally consulted by move-gen via
  GameState::current_color() — banqi now correctly remaps the
  seat-to-piece-colour after the first flip (was previously vestigial).
- Engine-driven 連吃 chain mode: state.chain_lock + Move::EndChain. After
  any chain-eligible capture in banqi+CHAIN_CAPTURE the turn does NOT
  auto-advance; legal_moves filters to "captures from locked square +
  EndChain" and the player ends the chain explicitly. Probe / Trade
  outcomes of DarkCapture correctly skip the lock (the attacker didn't
  land at `to`).
- PlayerView gains chain_lock + current_color (serde-default for v4
  back-compat). All "Turn:" labels in chess-tui + chess-web now display
  current_color (the piece-colour to move) instead of side_to_move (the
  seat name) so banqi's seat/colour divergence reads naturally to the
  player.
- chess-web: SVG board renders the chain-locked piece with a dashed
  orange ring; a "連吃 — 繼續吃 or End chain" banner sits below the board
  while chain mode is active. Picker exposes 暗吃 / 暗吃·搏命 / 馬斜
  checkboxes; URL tokens dark / dark-trade / horse (+ dark-chain alias).
- chess-tui: Esc / Enter on the locked piece sends Move::EndChain;
  sidebar uses current_color.
- ICCS notation: DarkCapture → `<from>x?<to>`, EndChain → `end <sq>`.
- 16 new engine tests (banqi_first_flip_color, banqi_dark_capture,
  banqi_horse_diagonal, banqi_chain_mode) cover make/unmake round-trips
  for every outcome including the perpendicular-capture-in-chain
  scenario the user reported (相 east → 砲 → 卒 south).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested fixes for three banqi (暗棋) UX/rule bugs in a Rust workspace at `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess`:
   - **暗吃 (Dark Capture)**: Selecting a piece + clicking hidden tile should atomically reveal-and-capture, not just flip
   - **First-flip-determines-color**: First player flips a piece → that color is theirs; opponent goes next as opposite color
   - **馬斜 (Horse Diagonal)**: Wire up the existing house rule flag to add diagonal one-step captures (any piece, rank-ignored)
   
   Additionally clarified through Q&A: dark capture has both Probe (attacker stays on rank-fail) and Trade (attacker dies on rank-fail) variants. Chain captures should be single-step with player-driven continuation.
   
   Subsequent user feedback expanded scope: chain captures must work step-by-step in any direction (not just same line), the engine must not auto-advance turn after chain-eligible captures, dark-capture probe/trade must not activate chain mode, and the "Turn:" label must show piece-color not seat name.
   
   Final request: "git commit changes"

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust + WASM Chinese chess engine (chess-core)
   - Leptos + Trunk SVG-based web client (chess-web)
   - ratatui-based terminal client (chess-tui)
   - WebSocket multi-room server (chess-net) with protocol v5 (was v4)
   - `side_assignment.mapping` for banqi seat→piece-color remapping
   - `Move` enum with new `DarkCapture` and `EndChain` variants
   - `state.chain_lock: Option<Square>` for engine-driven 連吃
   - `dark_capture_outcome` enum: Capture/Probe/Trade
   - `serde(default)` for protocol back-compat
   - bitflags `HouseRules`: CHAIN_CAPTURE, DARK_CAPTURE, DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, CHARIOT_RUSH, HORSE_DIAGONAL, CANNON_FAST_MOVE
   - ICCS notation extensions (`x?` for DarkCapture, `end <sq>` for EndChain)
   - PlayerView with `chain_lock` and `current_color` fields

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/moves.rs`**: Added `Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> }` and `Move::EndChain { at: Square }`. Updated `origin_square()`, `to_square()`, `resets_no_progress()` for new variants.

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/rules/house.rs`**: Renamed `DARK_CHAIN` → `DARK_CAPTURE` (bit 1 unchanged for back-compat), added `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` (bit 5). Updated `normalize()`, `PRESET_AGGRESSIVE`.

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs`**: Added `chain_lock` field to `GameState`, added `current_color()`, `compute_chain_lock_after()`, `has_capture_from()`, `dark_capture_outcome()`, `DarkCaptureOutcome` enum. Modified `make_move` to skip turn-advance when chain_lock activates. Modified `unmake_move` to derive turn-advanced from chain_lock state. Modified `legal_moves` to filter when chain_lock set.
   
   Critical fix in `compute_chain_lock_after`:
   ```rust
   Move::DarkCapture { to, revealed, attacker, .. } => {
       let attacker_piece = (*attacker)?;
       let revealed_piece = (*revealed)?;
       match dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house) {
           DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => *to,
           DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe | DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => return None,
       }
   }
   ```

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/rules/banqi.rs`**: Modified `generate()` to use `state.current_color()`. Added `gen_horse_diagonal()`. Updated `gen_for_face_up_piece` to emit `Move::DarkCapture` for hidden orthogonal targets. Added `gen_chariot_rush(... house: HouseRules ...)` which now also emits DarkCapture against hidden blockers with gap.

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`**: Added `chain_lock: Option<Square>` and `current_color: Side` fields to `PlayerView` with serde defaults for back-compat. Updated `sanitize_for_observer` to strip DarkCapture identity.

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/state/history.rs`**: Added `chain_lock_before: Option<Square>` to `MoveRecord` with serde default.

   - **`crates/chess-core/src/notation/iccs.rs`**: DarkCapture encodes as `<from>x?<to>`, EndChain as `end <sq>`.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/state.rs`**: Added `end_chain_move()` helper. Removed earlier ChainBuilder helpers.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`**: Removed `chain_hops` prop, added `chain_lock_marker()` rendering dashed orange ring around locked piece.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`**: Updated turn_text/turn_class to use `v.current_color` instead of `v.side_to_move`.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs`**: Removed UI chain_builder. Added engine-chain-mode click handler:
   ```rust
   if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
       if sq == locked {
           if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) { ... }
           return;
       }
       if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) { ... }
       return;
   }
   ```
   Fixed selection check to use `v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq)` instead of `p.piece.side == v.side_to_move`.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs`**: Same chain_lock-driven flow as LocalPage. Updated turn_label to use `v.current_color`.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs`**: Added checkboxes for 暗吃 / 暗吃·搏命 / 馬斜.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`**: HOUSE_TOKENS includes "dark", "dark-trade", with HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES for legacy "dark-chain".

   - **`clients/chess-web/style.css`**: Added `.chain-lock` (dashed orange ring) and `.chain-banner` styles.

   - **`clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`**: Removed `ChainBuilder` struct/field. Updated `handle_select_or_commit`, `compute_select_outcome`, `apply_select_outcome` to use engine `view.chain_lock`. Esc / Enter on locked piece sends `Move::EndChain`. Updated CLI parser for new tokens.

   - **`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`**: Updated sidebar to use `view.current_color` for "Side to move:" / "Your turn:" labels (lines 910, 1054).

   - **Test files (4 new)**:
     - `banqi_first_flip_color.rs` (2 tests)
     - `banqi_dark_capture.rs` (9 tests including chariot-rush dark capture)
     - `banqi_horse_diagonal.rs` (5 tests)
     - `banqi_chain_mode.rs` (11 tests including perpendicular_capture_in_chain_keeps_lock, dark_capture_probe_against_own_piece_does_not_activate_chain_lock, dark_capture_trade_against_stronger_enemy_does_not_activate_chain_lock)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`smallvec` not in chess-web deps**: Test failed importing smallvec macro. Fixed by adding `[dev-dependencies] smallvec = { workspace = true }` to chess-web Cargo.toml.
   - **clippy `manual_contains` lint**: `builder.hops.iter().any(|s| *s == sq)` → `builder.hops.contains(&sq)`.
   - **clippy `if_same_then_else` lint**: in `has_capture_from` chariot rush branch — refactored to extract `walked.is_empty()` check first.
   - **User feedback - chain captures don't work in web**: My initial chain-builder UI only supported same-line chains. User said clicking captures committed single Capture immediately. Fixed by adding ChainBuilder state... but this was wrong approach.
   - **User feedback - 成功吃到了 輪次卻換到對面了**: User wanted engine to NOT auto-advance turn after chain-eligible captures. Replaced UI chain_builder with engine `chain_lock` + `Move::EndChain` mechanism.
   - **User feedback - 暗吃到自己卻還是留在連吃狀態**: `compute_chain_lock_after` was using `to` as landing for all DarkCapture outcomes, but Probe leaves attacker at `from`, Trade kills attacker. Fixed by consulting `dark_capture_outcome` enum.
   - **User feedback - Turn label shows Black after Black flip**: UI showed seat name when player wanted piece-color. Added `current_color` to PlayerView.
   - **`git add` failed in chess-web cwd**: Used `git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess` for absolute repo path.
   - **`.claude/plans/` and `.specstory/` accidentally staged**: A pre-commit hook auto-staged them after the commit. Unstaged with `git restore --staged`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Side assignment was vestigial**: Discovered `side_assignment.mapping` was set on first flip but never consulted by move-gen. Fixed by adding `current_color()` helper and routing all banqi piece-color filters through it.
   - **Chain mode design iteration**: First implemented atomic ChainCapture + UI chain-builder (wrong - only worked for same-line). Then switched to engine-driven `chain_lock` + `Move::EndChain` (correct - works for any direction).
   - **DarkCapture chain interaction**: Probe/Trade outcomes don't move the attacker, so chain_lock must skip them. Initially missed this, caused the "暗吃到自己" bug.
   - **Seat vs piece-color labeling**: Distinguished between `side_to_move` (seat, for routing) and `current_color` (piece-color, for display).

6. All user messages:
   - Initial bug report: "目前關於半棋/小盤/暗棋中的「暗連(應該改成暗吃 手動搭配連吃組成暗連)」 似乎UX上目前有點小bug 比如我點了我的旗子 要去吃未翻開的旗子 理論上應該是直接翻開判定能不能吃 如果可以吃則直接吃掉然後繼續 現在UI會變成翻開 此外目前仍然是紅黑輪換的規則 實際上應該有規則是 第一個先手的人翻開什麼顏色則扮演什麼方 比如一開始翻到黑色 則下一個輪到紅方 以此類推 然後順便在這輪把「馬斜」也做上"
   - Q1 answer: "暗吃就是一定翻開(不能通過預先知道能不能吃決定) 然後有兩種版本 一種是不能吃就會停住(你的探測) 一種是不能吃如果還是對方比自己大的該子會直接陣亡"
   - Q2 answer: "斜走一步,吃任意子 (Recommended)"
   - Q3 answer: "按吃跟連吃都是單步操作 關鍵在於切換黑紅方的輪次 主要是連吃狀態如果上一步是成功吃 則仍然可以動吃的那顆子(不過也可以放棄連吃 就只吃一步) 另外要注意 車衝 如果是吃而非移動 連吃下一步一定只能吃 不能接普通移動 (其他的邏輯也是如此)"
   - Q4 answer: "引擎保留原子連吃,UI走逐步點選 (Recommended)"
   - "測試了一下 Web的連吃好像沒有生效？ 你有test過了嗎？" with screenshot
   - "現在似乎還是怪怪的 banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse 的前提下 成功吃到了 輪次卻換到對面了 顯然不合理" with screenshots showing 相→砲 capture
   - Interrupted message: "現在看起來還是有很多bug 暗吃到自己卻還是留在連吃狀態 且還能動剛翻開的新棋子" with screenshots
   - "現在輪換順序還是有問題 第一個人翻到黑 此時應該馬上變成紅的回合 起始狀態是 Player 1: (unknown) Player 2: (unknown) 然後 Player 1先手翻到黑 -> 指定爲黑色玩家 而馬上換Player 2的回合 而因爲它自動被指定爲紅 所以現在應該要是紅色回合"
   - "git commit changes"

```text
Excellent. Now I have the complete picture. Let me provide my findings:

## Captured Pieces Recording — Analysis Report

### **1. MoveRecord / History** (`src/state/history.rs:9-20`)
**Current state:** NO dedicated capture-list field. `MoveRecord` only tracks:
- `mover: Side` (which side moved)
- `the_move: Move` (the move itself—see below)
- `no_progress_before: u16` (ply counter snapshot)
- `chain_lock_before: Option<Square>` (v5: chain-mode lock before move)

**Captured pieces are embedded inside the `Move` enum variant**, not in the record wrapper. This means capture info is available but requires iterating `state.history` and inspecting each `the_move`.

### **2. GameState** (`src/state/mod.rs:56-74`)
NO graveyard/cemetery field exists. The `GameState` struct contains:
- `board: Board`
- `history: Vec<MoveRecord>`
- `side_to_move`, `turn_order`, `status`, etc.
- `no_progress_plies: u16`
- `chain_lock: Option<Square>` (v5)

Captured pieces are ONLY preserved inside `MoveRecord.the_move` variants. There's no separate `captures: Vec<Piece>` or similar.

### **3. Move Variants** (`src/moves.rs:19-60`)
**Capture-carrying variants:**
- **`Move::Capture { from, to, captured: Piece }`** (line 29): Single capture records the `Piece`.
- **`Move::ChainCapture { from, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> }`** (line 34): Multi-hop chain. Each hop carries:
  ```rust
  pub struct ChainHop {
      pub to: Square,
      pub captured: Piece,
  }
  ```
  (lines 57–60) So every intermediate capture is preserved per-hop.
- **`Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen, captured: Piece }`** (line 37): Cannon screen-jump records the captured piece.
- **`Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> }`** (line 45): Banqi dark-capture stores both the revealed target AND the attacker. **Outcome (Capture/Trade/Probe) is recomputed at undo time** via `dark_capture_outcome()` (lines 563–579); the outcome isn't stored — see `state/mod.rs:330-368` for apply, lines 415–443 for unapply.

### **4. PlayerView Projection** (`src/view.rs:17-66`)
NO captured-pieces field exposed. `PlayerView` contains:
- `cells: Vec<VisibleCell>` (board state)
- `legal_moves`, `status`, `side_to_move`, `chain_lock`, `current_color`

**ADR-0004 compliance:** Captured pieces are safe to expose in UI—they were revealed at capture-time, so no hidden-identity leak. The view just doesn't currently expose them.

### **5. Piece / PieceKind** (`src/piece.rs:31-88`)
- `PieceKind` enum (7 kinds: General, Advisor, Elephant, Chariot, Horse, Cannon, Soldier)
- `Piece` struct: `{ side: Side, kind: PieceKind }` (line 78)
- `PieceOnSquare`: wraps `Piece` + `revealed: bool` (line 93)

**Glyph rendering:** Both `clients/chess-web/src/glyph.rs:15-20` and `clients/chess-tui/src/glyph.rs:16-20` export:
```rust
pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
```
So you already have piece→glyph mapping. Safe to reuse for "dead pieces" list.

---

## Recommendation: Architecture Choice

**Option (a) — Compute on-the-fly from history:**
- ✅ Zero protocol impact; protocol v5 already shipped
- ✅ No `GameState` mutation; cleaner
- ❌ Every UI render loops `state.history`, iterates captures
- ✅ Correct for replay (you traverse the full history anyway)

**Option (b) — Add `captures: Vec<CaptureLogEntry>` to GameState:**
- ❌ **Protocol bump required** (v5→v6) even though the field is "computed"
- ✅ O(1) access for UI sidebar render
- ⚠️ Must maintain during `make_move` / `unmake_move` (lines 104–170); be careful with undo

**For a banqi sidebar with potentially 16 captured pieces, compute-on-the-fly is fast enough.** Chain captures (up to 4 hops in the `SmallVec`) are already atomic in history, so you iterate move records and flatten them:

```
for record in state.history:
  match record.the_move {
    Capture { captured, .. } => captures.push(captured),
    ChainCapture { path, .. } => captures.extend(path.iter().map(|h| h.captured)),
    CannonJump { captured, .. } => captures.push(captured),
    DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {
      // Only add 'revealed' if outcome was Capture (recompute outcome)
      // Probe adds nothing, Trade adds only revealed
    }
    _ => {}
  }
```

**Verdict:** Stay with **(a)** unless sidebar performance becomes a bottleneck. No protocol change needed.
```

```text
1	//! Move enum.
2	//!
3	//! Flat enum (not a trait) so serde, equality, and pattern matching work
4	//! without ceremony. The `Reveal` variant carries `Option<Piece>` because
5	//! the network ABI distinguishes pre-flip (`None` from client) from
6	//! post-flip (`Some` from authoritative server). See ADR-0004.
7	
8	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
9	use smallvec::SmallVec;
10	
11	use crate::coord::Square;
12	use crate::piece::Piece;
13	
14	/// One logical move from a player's perspective.
15	///
16	/// `ChainCapture` is a single move even though it encodes a sequence —
17	/// undo pops once, history records once, the network ships one message.
18	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
19	pub enum Move {
20	    /// Banqi: flip the face-down piece at `at`. The identity is `None`
21	    /// when the client requests the flip and `Some(_)` once the
22	    /// authoritative engine has resolved it.
23	    Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> },
24	
25	    /// Plain non-capturing slide.
26	    Step { from: Square, to: Square },
27	
28	    /// Capture (single hop). `captured` is recorded so undo can restore.
29	    Capture { from: Square, to: Square, captured: Piece },
30	
31	    /// Chain capture (banqi house rule 連吃 / 暗連). `path` is the sequence
32	    /// of hops, each of which is a capture. Last hop's `to` is the final
33	    /// landing square. Length ≥ 1.
34	    ChainCapture { from: Square, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> },
35	
36	    /// Cannon capture-by-jump. `screen` is the piece jumped over.
37	    CannonJump { from: Square, to: Square, screen: Square, captured: Piece },
38	
39	    /// Banqi house rule `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE): atomically reveal a
40	    /// face-down piece and resolve a capture against it.
41	    /// `revealed` and `attacker` are `None` on the wire (client → server)
42	    /// and `Some(_)` once the authoritative engine has filled them in.
43	    /// Outcome (capture / probe / trade) is decided at apply-time from
44	    /// banqi rank rules + `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` flag.
45	    DarkCapture { from: Square, to: Square, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> },
46	
47	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode terminator. After a chain-eligible capture,
48	    /// `state.chain_lock` is set and the turn does NOT auto-advance —
49	    /// the player keeps moving the same piece via further captures.
50	    /// `Move::EndChain { at }` is the explicit "I'm done" pass: it clears
51	    /// `chain_lock` and advances the turn. `at` is the locked piece's
52	    /// square (carried so undo can restore).
53	    EndChain { at: Square },
54	}
55	
56	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
57	pub struct ChainHop {
58	    pub to: Square,
59	    pub captured: Piece,
60	}
61	
62	/// Move-list type alias — sized to avoid heap allocation in typical positions.
63	pub type MoveList = SmallVec<[Move; 32]>;
64	
65	impl Move {
66	    /// Origin square of the move (where the moving piece started).
67	    /// `Reveal` returns the flip square. `EndChain` returns the locked
68	    /// piece's square (the piece doesn't move).
69	    #[inline]
70	    pub fn origin_square(&self) -> Square {
71	        match self {
72	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
73	            Move::EndChain { at } => *at,
74	            Move::Step { from, .. }
75	            | Move::Capture { from, .. }
76	            | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. }
77	            | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
78	            | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. } => *from,
79	        }
80	    }
81	
82	    /// Destination square (where the moving piece ended). `Reveal` and
83	    /// `EndChain` have none (the piece doesn't move).
84	    #[inline]
85	    pub fn to_square(&self) -> Option<Square> {
86	        match self {
87	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => None,
88	            Move::Step { to, .. }
89	            | Move::Capture { to, .. }
90	            | Move::CannonJump { to, .. }
91	            | Move::DarkCapture { to, .. } => Some(*to),
92	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to),
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	    /// Whether this move resets the no-progress counter (capture or reveal).
97	    /// `EndChain` is a turn pass — it does NOT reset no_progress.
98	    #[inline]
99	    pub fn resets_no_progress(&self) -> bool {
100	        matches!(
101	            self,
102	            Move::Reveal { .. }
103	                | Move::Capture { .. }
104	                | Move::ChainCapture { .. }
105	                | Move::CannonJump { .. }
106	                | Move::DarkCapture { .. }
107	        )
108	    }
109	}
110
```
</details></tool-use>

1	//! Pieces and sides.
2	
3	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
4	
5	/// Player side. Two-player games use 0 and 1; 三國暗棋 uses 0, 1, 2.
6	///
7	/// Wraps a small int rather than a fixed enum so 3-player isn't a special case.
8	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
9	#[repr(transparent)]
10	pub struct Side(pub u8);
11	
12	impl Side {
13	    pub const RED: Side = Side(0);
14	    pub const BLACK: Side = Side(1);
15	    /// Third faction in 三國暗棋. Maps to 蜀/吳/魏 externally as the variant chooses.
16	    pub const GREEN: Side = Side(2);
17	
18	    #[inline]
19	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u8 {
20	        self.0
21	    }
22	
23	    /// 2-player only: the opposing side.
24	    #[inline]
25	    pub const fn opposite(self) -> Side {
26	        Side(self.0 ^ 1)
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
31	pub enum PieceKind {
32	    /// 將 / 帥
33	    General,
34	    /// 士 / 仕
35	    Advisor,
36	    /// 象 / 相
37	    Elephant,
38	    /// 車 / 俥
39	    Chariot,
40	    /// 馬 / 傌
41	    Horse,
42	    /// 炮 / 砲
43	    Cannon,
44	    /// 卒 / 兵
45	    Soldier,
46	}
47	
48	impl PieceKind {
49	    /// Banqi capture rank. Higher beats lower (with the cannon and
50	    /// soldier-vs-general exceptions handled in the move generator,
51	    /// not by this number).
52	    #[inline]
53	    pub const fn banqi_rank(self) -> u8 {
54	        match self {
55	            PieceKind::General => 6,
56	            PieceKind::Advisor => 5,
57	            PieceKind::Elephant => 4,
58	            PieceKind::Chariot => 3,
59	            PieceKind::Horse => 2,
60	            PieceKind::Cannon => 1,
61	            PieceKind::Soldier => 0,
62	        }
63	    }
64	
65	    /// Iteration helper.
66	    pub const ALL: [PieceKind; 7] = [
67	        PieceKind::General,
68	        PieceKind::Advisor,
69	        PieceKind::Elephant,
70	        PieceKind::Chariot,
71	        PieceKind::Horse,
72	        PieceKind::Cannon,
73	        PieceKind::Soldier,
74	    ];
75	}
76	
77	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
78	pub struct Piece {
79	    pub side: Side,
80	    pub kind: PieceKind,
81	}
82	
83	impl Piece {
84	    #[inline]
85	    pub const fn new(side: Side, kind: PieceKind) -> Self {
86	        Self { side, kind }
87	    }
88	}
89	
90	/// What sits on a square. Banqi pieces start `revealed: false`;
91	/// xiangqi pieces are always `revealed: true`.
92	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
93	pub struct PieceOnSquare {
94	    pub piece: Piece,
95	    pub revealed: bool,
96	}
97	
98	impl PieceOnSquare {
99	    #[inline]
100	    pub const fn revealed(piece: Piece) -> Self {
101	        Self { piece, revealed: true }
102	    }
103	
104	    #[inline]
105	    pub const fn hidden(piece: Piece) -> Self {
106	        Self { piece, revealed: false }
107	    }
108	}
109	
110	#[cfg(test)]
111	mod tests {
112	    use super::*;
113	
114	    #[test]
115	    fn side_opposite_is_involution() {
116	        assert_eq!(Side::RED.opposite(), Side::BLACK);
117	        assert_eq!(Side::BLACK.opposite(), Side::RED);
118	        assert_eq!(Side::RED.opposite().opposite(), Side::RED);
119	    }
120	
121	    #[test]
122	    fn banqi_rank_strictly_decreasing_general_to_soldier() {
123	        // General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot > Horse > Cannon > Soldier
124	        let order = [
125	            PieceKind::General,
126	            PieceKind::Advisor,
127	            PieceKind::Elephant,
128	            PieceKind::Chariot,
129	            PieceKind::Horse,
130	            PieceKind::Cannon,
131	            PieceKind::Soldier,
132	        ];
133	        for w in order.windows(2) {
134	            assert!(w[0].banqi_rank() > w[1].banqi_rank(), "{:?} should outrank {:?}", w[0], w[1],);
135	        }
136	    }
137	
138	    #[test]
139	    fn piece_kind_all_covers_seven_distinct() {
140	        let mut sorted: Vec<_> = PieceKind::ALL.iter().collect();
141	        sorted.sort_by_key(|p| format!("{:?}", p));
142	        sorted.dedup();
143	        assert_eq!(sorted.len(), 7);
144	    }
145	}

1	//! Game state: board + turn + history + status.
2	
3	pub mod history;
4	pub mod repetition;
5	pub mod turn_order;
6	
7	pub use history::MoveRecord;
8	pub use turn_order::TurnOrder;
9	
10	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
11	use smallvec::SmallVec;
12	
13	use crate::board::Board;
14	use crate::error::CoreError;
15	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
16	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceOnSquare, Side};
17	use crate::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
18	
19	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
20	pub enum GameStatus {
21	    Ongoing,
22	    Won { winner: Side, reason: WinReason },
23	    Drawn { reason: DrawReason },
24	}
25	
26	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
27	pub enum WinReason {
28	    Checkmate,
29	    Stalemate,
30	    Resignation,
31	    OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
32	    Timeout,
33	    /// Casual xiangqi (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`): the loser's general was
34	    /// physically captured rather than ending in checkmate. In standard
35	    /// rules this state is unreachable because the legality filter rejects
36	    /// any move that would leave the general capturable.
37	    GeneralCaptured,
38	}
39	
40	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
41	pub enum DrawReason {
42	    NoProgress,
43	    Repetition,
44	    Agreed,
45	    InsufficientMaterial,
46	}
47	
48	/// Side ↔ piece-color assignment for banqi (set after the first flip).
49	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
50	pub struct SideAssignment {
51	    /// `mapping[side.0 as usize]` = the piece-color that seat controls.
52	    pub mapping: SmallVec<[Side; 3]>,
53	}
54	
55	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
56	pub struct GameState {
57	    pub rules: RuleSet,
58	    pub board: Board,
59	    pub side_to_move: Side,
60	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
61	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
62	    pub status: GameStatus,
63	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
64	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
65	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
66	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock. `Some(sq)` means the moving piece at
67	    /// `sq` just made a chain-eligible capture and may continue capturing
68	    /// — the turn does NOT auto-advance until the player issues
69	    /// `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` (or makes another chain-eligible
70	    /// capture which keeps the lock active). `#[serde(default)]` so
71	    /// pre-chain-mode snapshots load.
72	    #[serde(default)]
73	    pub chain_lock: Option<crate::coord::Square>,
74	}
75	
76	impl GameState {
77	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
78	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
79	    }
80	
81	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
82	    ///
83	    /// When `chain_lock` is set (banqi 連吃 mid-chain), the legal-move
84	    /// list is filtered to capture-only moves originating from the
85	    /// locked square, plus `Move::EndChain` as the explicit terminator.
86	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
87	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
88	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
89	        if let Some(lock) = self.chain_lock {
90	            out.retain(|m| match m {
91	                Move::Capture { from, .. }
92	                | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
93	                | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. }
94	                | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } => *from == lock,
95	                _ => false,
96	            });
97	            out.push(Move::EndChain { at: lock });
98	        }
99	        out
100	    }
101	
102	    /// Apply a move. Caller is responsible for legality (use `legal_moves`).
103	    /// Returns the move pushed to history (with `Reveal` payloads filled in).
104	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
105	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
106	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
107	        }
108	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
109	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
110	        let chain_lock_before = self.chain_lock;
111	
112	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
113	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
114	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
115	
116	        self.history.push(MoveRecord {
117	            mover,
118	            the_move: normalized.clone(),
119	            no_progress_before,
120	            chain_lock_before,
121	        });
122	
123	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
124	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
125	        } else {
126	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
127	        }
128	
129	        // Decide whether the moving piece enters / continues chain mode.
130	        // Banqi-only, gated on CHAIN_CAPTURE flag, only after a real
131	        // capture that landed an attacker on a square where it can
132	        // capture again. EndChain explicitly clears the lock.
133	        self.chain_lock = self.compute_chain_lock_after(&normalized);
134	
135	        // Turn advances UNLESS we just entered / are continuing chain
136	        // mode (chain_lock now Some).
137	        if self.chain_lock.is_none() {
138	            self.turn_order.advance();
139	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
140	        }
141	
142	        Ok(())
143	    }
144	
145	    /// Undo the last move.
146	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
147	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
148	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
149	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
150	
151	        // If chain_lock is currently Some, the move did NOT advance the
152	        // turn (the player was either entering or continuing chain mode).
153	        // Otherwise the turn advanced and we need to rewind it.
154	        let turn_advanced = self.chain_lock.is_none();
155	
156	        // Restore the pre-move chain_lock.
157	        self.chain_lock = rec.chain_lock_before;
158	
159	        if turn_advanced {
160	            if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
161	                self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
162	            } else {
163	                self.turn_order.current -= 1;
164	            }
165	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
166	        }
167	        // Game status reset to Ongoing — caller may recompute.
168	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
169	        Ok(())
170	    }
171	
172	    /// After applying `m`, decide whether the moving piece is now in
173	    /// banqi 連吃 chain mode. Returns the locked piece's square when
174	    /// chain mode should remain / activate; `None` otherwise.
175	    ///
176	    /// Activates only for banqi + `CHAIN_CAPTURE` rules, after a capture
177	    /// move whose attacker actually landed on a square (probe and trade
178	    /// outcomes of `Move::DarkCapture` skip the lock — the attacker
179	    /// stayed put or died). The attacker must have at least one further
180	    /// legal capture from the landing square.
181	    fn compute_chain_lock_after(&self, m: &Move) -> Option<crate::coord::Square> {
182	        if self.rules.variant != Variant::Banqi {
183	            return None;
184	        }
185	        if !self.rules.house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
186	            return None;
187	        }
188	        // Where did the moving piece end up? Only chain-eligible when
189	        // the attacker actually moved to a new square.
190	        let landing = match m {
191	            Move::Capture { to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { to, .. } => *to,
192	            Move::DarkCapture { to, revealed, attacker, .. } => {
193	                // `Move::DarkCapture` resolves to one of three outcomes
194	                // at apply-time. Only `Capture` (rank ok, attacker took
195	                // defender's square) puts the attacker at `to`; `Probe`
196	                // leaves the attacker at `from` and `Trade` removes the
197	                // attacker entirely. Neither of those is chain-eligible.
198	                let attacker_piece = (*attacker)?;
199	                let revealed_piece = (*revealed)?;
200	                match dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house) {
201	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => *to,
202	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe | DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => return None,
203	                }
204	            }
205	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to)?,
206	            // Reveal / Step / EndChain don't trigger chain mode.
207	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::Step { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => return None,
208	        };
209	        let landing_pos = self.board.get(landing)?;
210	        if !landing_pos.revealed {
211	            return None;
212	        }
213	        let active_color = self.current_color();
214	        if landing_pos.piece.side != active_color {
215	            // Defensive: shouldn't happen now that DarkCapture branches
216	            // explicitly above, but stays correct if future variants add
217	            // new capture-with-reveal moves.
218	            return None;
219	        }
220	        if has_capture_from(self, landing, landing_pos.piece) {
221	            Some(landing)
222	        } else {
223	            None
224	        }
225	    }
226	
227	    fn normalize_for_apply(&self, m: &Move) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
228	        match m {
229	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None } => {
230	                let pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
231	                if pos.revealed {
232	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
233	                }
234	                Ok(Move::Reveal { at: *at, revealed: Some(pos.piece) })
235	            }
236	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
237	                let attacker = match attacker {
238	                    Some(p) => *p,
239	                    None => {
240	                        let pos = self
241	                            .board
242	                            .get(*from)
243	                            .ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
244	                        if !pos.revealed {
245	                            return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: from not revealed"));
246	                        }
247	                        pos.piece
248	                    }
249	                };
250	                let revealed = match revealed {
251	                    Some(p) => *p,
252	                    None => {
253	                        let pos = self
254	                            .board
255	                            .get(*to)
256	                            .ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty to"))?;
257	                        if pos.revealed {
258	                            return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: target already revealed"));
259	                        }
260	                        pos.piece
261	                    }
262	                };
263	                Ok(Move::DarkCapture {
264	                    from: *from,
265	                    to: *to,
266	                    revealed: Some(revealed),
267	                    attacker: Some(attacker),
268	                })
269	            }
270	            _ => Ok(m.clone()),
271	        }
272	    }
273	
274	    fn apply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
275	        match m {
276	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
277	                let mut pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
278	                if pos.revealed {
279	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
280	                }
281	                pos.revealed = true;
282	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
283	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment.
284	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
285	                    if let Some(p) = revealed {
286	                        self.side_assignment =
287	                            Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
288	                    }
289	                }
290	            }
291	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
292	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Step: empty from"))?;
293	                if self.board.get(*to).is_some() {
294	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Step: occupied to"));
295	                }
296	                self.board.set(*from, None);
297	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
298	            }
299	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured: _ } => {
300	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Capture: empty from"))?;
301	                self.board.set(*from, None);
302	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
303	            }
304	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured: _ } => {
305	                let p =
306	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("CannonJump: empty from"))?;
307	                self.board.set(*from, None);
308	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
309	            }
310	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
311	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty from"))?;
312	                if path.is_empty() {
313	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty path"));
314	                }
315	                self.board.set(*from, None);
316	                for hop in &path[..path.len() - 1] {
317	                    self.board.set(hop.to, None);
318	                }
319	                self.board.set(path.last().unwrap().to, Some(p));
320	            }
321	            Move::EndChain { at } => {
322	                // No board change — just verifies that a chain-locked piece
323	                // exists at `at`. The turn-advance machinery in
324	                // `make_move` clears `chain_lock` based on
325	                // `compute_chain_lock_after`.
326	                if self.chain_lock != Some(*at) {
327	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("EndChain: no active chain lock"));
328	                }
329	            }
330	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
331	                let revealed_piece =
332	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
333	                let attacker_piece =
334	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
335	                // Reveal the target.
336	                let mut target_pos =
337	                    self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty to"))?;
338	                if target_pos.revealed {
339	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: target already revealed"));
340	                }
341	                target_pos.revealed = true;
342	                self.board.set(*to, Some(target_pos));
343	
344	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment (mirrors Reveal).
345	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
346	                    self.side_assignment =
347	                        Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, revealed_piece.side));
348	                }
349	
350	                // Resolve outcome based on rank + DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag.
351	                let attacker_pos =
352	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
353	                let outcome =
354	                    dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house);
355	                match outcome {
356	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
357	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
358	                        self.board.set(*to, Some(attacker_pos));
359	                    }
360	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => {
361	                        // Attacker dies; target stays revealed at `to`.
362	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
363	                    }
364	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {
365	                        // Both pieces stay; target is now revealed.
366	                    }
367	                }
368	            }
369	        }
370	        Ok(())
371	    }
372	
373	    fn unapply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
374	        match m {
375	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => {
376	                let mut pos =
377	                    self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Reveal: empty"))?;
378	                pos.revealed = false;
379	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
380	                // First-flip might have set side_assignment; if this was the first move, clear it.
381	                if self.history.is_empty() {
382	                    self.side_assignment = None;
383	                }
384	            }
385	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
386	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Step: empty to"))?;
387	                self.board.set(*to, None);
388	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
389	            }
390	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
391	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Capture: empty to"))?;
392	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
393	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
394	            }
395	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
396	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Jump: empty to"))?;
397	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
398	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
399	            }
400	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
401	                let last_to = path.last().unwrap().to;
402	                let p =
403	                    self.board.get(last_to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Chain: empty last"))?;
404	                self.board.set(last_to, None);
405	                // Restore intermediate captures (in any order).
406	                for hop in path {
407	                    self.board.set(hop.to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(hop.captured)));
408	                }
409	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
410	            }
411	            Move::EndChain { .. } => {
412	                // No board change to undo. chain_lock is restored by
413	                // `unmake_move` from `MoveRecord.chain_lock_before`.
414	            }
415	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
416	                let revealed_piece =
417	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
418	                let attacker_piece =
419	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
420	                let outcome =
421	                    dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house);
422	                // Restore attacker at `from` (reveals it as it was).
423	                match outcome {
424	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
425	                        // Attacker is at `to`; remove it, restore at `from`.
426	                        self.board.set(*to, None);
427	                        self.board.set(*from, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(attacker_piece)));
428	                    }
429	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => {
430	                        // Attacker is gone; restore it at `from`.
431	                        self.board.set(*from, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(attacker_piece)));
432	                    }
433	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {
434	                        // Attacker is still at `from`. Nothing to do for it.
435	                    }
436	                }
437	                // Always re-hide the target (it was revealed during apply).
438	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(revealed_piece)));
439	                // First-flip side assignment may have been set by this move.
440	                if self.history.is_empty() {
441	                    self.side_assignment = None;
442	                }
443	            }
444	        }
445	        Ok(())
446	    }
447	
448	    /// Whether `side`'s general is currently under attack by the opponent.
449	    /// Xiangqi-specific; banqi has no general-in-check concept.
450	    pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
451	        crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
452	    }
453	
454	    /// Recompute `status` based on legal moves and draw counters.
455	    /// Caller invokes this after `make_move`; we don't auto-call to avoid
456	    /// recursing into move generation during legality filtering.
457	    ///
458	    /// TODO (PR 2): threefold repetition. Currently only no-progress and
459	    /// no-legal-moves are detected.
460	    pub fn refresh_status(&mut self) {
461	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
462	            return;
463	        }
464	        // Casual xiangqi: a missing general means the opposite side won.
465	        // Standard rules can't reach this branch (the legality filter
466	        // forbids leaving the general capturable), but checking unconditionally
467	        // is cheap and keeps the invariant for any future variant that allows
468	        // physical king capture.
469	        if self.rules.variant == Variant::Xiangqi {
470	            for &seat in self.turn_order.seats.iter() {
471	                let has_general = find_piece(&self.board, |p| {
472	                    p.kind == crate::piece::PieceKind::General && p.side == seat
473	                })
474	                .is_some();
475	                if !has_general {
476	                    self.status = GameStatus::Won {
477	                        winner: seat.opposite(),
478	                        reason: WinReason::GeneralCaptured,
479	                    };
480	                    return;
481	                }
482	            }
483	        }
484	        if self.no_progress_plies >= self.rules.draw_policy.no_progress_plies {
485	            self.status = GameStatus::Drawn { reason: DrawReason::NoProgress };
486	            return;
487	        }
488	        let moves = self.legal_moves();
489	        if moves.is_empty() {
490	            let me = self.side_to_move;
491	            self.status = match self.rules.variant {
492	                Variant::Xiangqi => {
493	                    let reason = if self.is_in_check(me) {
494	                        WinReason::Checkmate
495	                    } else {
496	                        // Asian rules: stalemate is a loss for the stalemated side.
497	                        WinReason::Stalemate
498	                    };
499	                    GameStatus::Won { winner: me.opposite(), reason }
500	                }
501	                Variant::Banqi => GameStatus::Won {
502	                    winner: me.opposite(),
503	                    reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
504	                },
505	                Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {
506	                    // PR 2: more nuanced 3-side win/elimination logic.
507	                    GameStatus::Won {
508	                        winner: me.opposite(),
509	                        reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
510	                    }
511	                }
512	            };
513	        }
514	    }
515	
516	    pub fn default_starter(variant: Variant) -> Side {
517	        match variant {
518	            Variant::Xiangqi => Side::RED,
519	            Variant::Banqi => Side::RED,
520	            Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => Side(0),
521	        }
522	    }
523	
524	    /// The piece-color the active seat actually controls.
525	    ///
526	    /// Banqi remaps after the first flip locks `side_assignment`; xiangqi
527	    /// and three-kingdom always return the seat as-is. Move generation
528	    /// for banqi must filter pieces by this color, not by `side_to_move`
529	    /// — `side_to_move` is the seat / turn-order index, which only
530	    /// coincides with the piece-color before the first reveal.
531	    #[inline]
532	    pub fn current_color(&self) -> Side {
533	        match self.side_assignment.as_ref() {
534	            Some(sa) => {
535	                let idx = self.side_to_move.0 as usize;
536	                if idx < sa.mapping.len() {
537	                    sa.mapping[idx]
538	                } else {
539	                    self.side_to_move
540	                }
541	            }
542	            None => self.side_to_move,
543	        }
544	    }
545	}
546	
547	/// Outcome of a 暗吃 (`Move::DarkCapture`) once the target is revealed.
548	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
549	pub(crate) enum DarkCaptureOutcome {
550	    /// Rank check passes — attacker takes target's square (normal capture).
551	    Capture,
552	    /// Rank check fails AND `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` is set — attacker is
553	    /// removed; defender stays revealed at `to`.
554	    Trade,
555	    /// Rank check fails, no trade flag — both pieces stay; target is
556	    /// now revealed (information probe; turn advances normally).
557	    Probe,
558	}
559	
560	/// Decide the outcome of a dark-capture given the involved pieces and
561	/// the active house rules. Used by both apply and unapply (recomputed
562	/// rather than stored in the move record).
563	pub(crate) fn dark_capture_outcome(
564	    attacker: Piece,
565	    revealed: Piece,
566	    house: crate::rules::HouseRules,
567	) -> DarkCaptureOutcome {
568	    if attacker.side == revealed.side {
569	        // Same side → never a capture; treat as probe (own piece blocks).
570	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe;
571	    }
572	    if crate::rules::banqi::can_capture(attacker.kind, revealed.kind) {
573	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture
574	    } else if house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
575	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade
576	    } else {
577	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe
578	    }
579	}
580	
581	/// Whether `attacker` standing on `from` has at least one further legal
582	/// capture in any direction. Used to decide whether banqi 連吃 chain
583	/// mode should remain active after a capture (see
584	/// `compute_chain_lock_after`). Only consults banqi rules — caller
585	/// guarantees variant + CHAIN_CAPTURE flag.
586	fn has_capture_from(state: &GameState, from: crate::coord::Square, attacker: Piece) -> bool {
587	    use crate::coord::Direction;
588	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
589	    use crate::rules::{banqi::can_capture, HouseRules};
590	
591	    let house = state.rules.house;
592	    let board = &state.board;
593	
594	    // Cannon: only via jump-over-screen.
595	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
596	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
597	            let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
598	            let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
599	            let mut cursor = screen_sq;
600	            while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
601	                match board.get(next) {
602	                    None => cursor = next,
603	                    Some(pos) => {
604	                        if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
605	                            return true;
606	                        }
607	                        break;
608	                    }
609	                }
610	            }
611	        }
612	        return false;
613	    }
614	
615	    // Orthogonal one-step (and chariot rush extension if enabled).
616	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
617	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
618	        match board.get(to) {
619	            None => {}
620	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
621	                if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
622	                    return true;
623	                }
624	            }
625	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
626	            Some(target) => {
627	                if can_capture(attacker.kind, target.piece.kind) {
628	                    return true;
629	                }
630	            }
631	        }
632	    }
633	
634	    // Chariot rush — multi-square ray; with a gap, captures any piece.
635	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Chariot && house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH) {
636	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
637	            let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
638	            if walked.is_empty() {
639	                // Rank-bound 1-step capture is the base-rule branch above.
640	                continue;
641	            }
642	            let Some(target_sq) = blocker else { continue };
643	            let Some(pos) = board.get(target_sq) else { continue };
644	            if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
645	                return true;
646	            }
647	            if !pos.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
648	                return true;
649	            }
650	        }
651	    }
652	
653	    // 馬斜 — diagonal one-step, captures any piece.
654	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Horse && house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL) {
655	        for &dir in &Direction::DIAGONAL {
656	            let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
657	            match board.get(to) {
658	                None => {}
659	                Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
660	                    if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
661	                        return true;
662	                    }
663	                }
664	                Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
665	                Some(_) => return true,
666	            }
667	        }
668	    }
669	
670	    false
671	}
672	
673	fn banqi_side_assignment(flipper: Side, revealed_side: Side) -> SideAssignment {
674	    let mut mapping = SmallVec::new();
675	    if flipper == Side::RED {
676	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
677	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
678	    } else {
679	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
680	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
681	    }
682	    SideAssignment { mapping }
683	}
684	
685	/// Find the (single) piece matching predicate. Used to locate a general.
686	pub(crate) fn find_piece<F>(board: &Board, pred: F) -> Option<crate::coord::Square>
687	where
688	    F: Fn(Piece) -> bool,
689	{
690	    for sq in board.squares() {
691	        if let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) {
692	            if pos.revealed && pred(pos.piece) {
693	                return Some(sq);
694	            }
695	        }
696	    }
697	    None
698	}
699	
700	#[cfg(test)]
701	mod state_tests {
702	    use super::*;
703	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
704	
705	    #[test]
706	    fn opening_xiangqi_status_ongoing_after_refresh() {
707	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
708	        state.refresh_status();
709	        assert_eq!(state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing);
710	    }
711	
712	    #[test]
713	    fn no_progress_plies_advance_then_reset_on_capture() {
714	        // Synthetic: build a tiny xiangqi position where Red can capture.
715	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
716	        // Skim a move that doesn't capture: cannon h2 forward.
717	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
718	        state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
719	        assert_eq!(state.no_progress_plies, 1);
720	    }
721	
722	    #[test]

724	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 9));
725	        state.no_progress_plies = 5;
726	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Reveal { .. })).unwrap();
727	        state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
728	        assert_eq!(state.no_progress_plies, 0);
729	    }
730	}
731
```

```text
1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients — it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::coord::Square;
13	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
14	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
15	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
16	
17	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
18	pub enum VisibleCell {
19	    Empty,
20	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
21	    Hidden,
22	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
23	}
24	
25	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
26	pub struct PlayerView {
27	    pub observer: Side,
28	    pub shape: BoardShape,
29	    pub width: u8,
30	    pub height: u8,
31	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
32	    /// The seat / turn-order index whose turn it currently is. For
33	    /// xiangqi this matches the piece-colour exactly; for banqi after
34	    /// the first flip it may differ from `current_color` (the seat name
35	    /// is fixed at room-join time, but who plays which colour is
36	    /// decided by the first reveal). Use `current_color` for
37	    /// "Red/Black to move" labels and `side_to_move` for seat-routing
38	    /// (e.g. is it MY turn).
39	    pub side_to_move: Side,
40	    pub status: GameStatus,
41	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
42	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
43	    /// Whether the observer's own general is currently under attack.
44	    /// Always `false` for banqi/three-kingdom (no in-check concept) and
45	    /// for spectator-style observers without a real seat. Added in
46	    /// protocol v4; older clients see the default via `serde(default)`.
47	    #[serde(default)]
48	    pub in_check: bool,
49	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock: when `Some(sq)`, the player whose
50	    /// turn it is must continue capturing with the piece at `sq` or
51	    /// issue `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` to release the lock. The
52	    /// `legal_moves` list is already filtered for this case. Added in
53	    /// protocol v5; older clients see the default `None` via
54	    /// `serde(default)`.
55	    #[serde(default)]
56	    pub chain_lock: Option<Square>,
57	    /// Piece-colour the active seat actually controls. Equal to
58	    /// `side_to_move` until a banqi first-flip locks `side_assignment`;
59	    /// from then on it reflects the *colour* being played. UIs use this
60	    /// for "Red 紅 / Black 黑 to move" labels. Added in protocol v5;
61	    /// `serde(default = "default_red")` so older payloads (which lacked
62	    /// the field) still deserialise — Red was always the starter
63	    /// pre-v5, so the default is correct for fresh games.
64	    #[serde(default = "default_red_side")]
65	    pub current_color: Side,
66	}
67	
68	fn default_red_side() -> Side {
69	    Side::RED
70	}
71	
72	impl PlayerView {
73	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
74	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
75	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
76	        let board = &state.board;
77	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
78	            .map(|i| {
79	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
80	                match board.get(sq) {
81	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
82	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
83	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
84	                }
85	            })
86	            .collect();
87	
88	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
89	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
90	        } else {
91	            MoveList::new()
92	        };
93	
94	        // Only the observer's own general counts. Banqi/3K have no general,
95	        // so `is_in_check` returns false naturally — no variant gate needed.
96	        let in_check = state.is_in_check(observer);
97	
98	        Self {
99	            observer,
100	            shape: board.shape(),
101	            width: board.width(),
102	            height: board.height(),
103	            cells,
104	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
105	            status: state.status,
106	            legal_moves,
107	            in_check,
108	            chain_lock: state.chain_lock,
109	            current_color: state.current_color(),
110	        }
111	    }
112	}
113	
114	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` / `DarkCapture` moves before they reach
115	/// the network. Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the
116	/// engine resolves the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
117	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
118	    moves
119	        .into_iter()
120	        .map(|m| match m {
121	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
122	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } => {
123	                Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: None, attacker: None }
124	            }
125	            other => other,
126	        })
127	        .collect()
128	}
129	
130	#[cfg(test)]
131	mod tests {
132	    use super::*;
133	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
134	
135	    #[test]
136	    fn fresh_banqi_view_has_all_hidden_for_any_observer() {
137	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 7));
138	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
139	        assert_eq!(view.cells.len(), 32);
140	        assert!(view.cells.iter().all(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Hidden)));
141	    }
142	
143	    #[test]

145	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
146	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);

148	            view.cells.iter().filter(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))).collect();
149	        assert_eq!(revealed.len(), 32);
150	    }
151	
152	    #[test]
153	    fn opponent_view_has_empty_legal_moves() {
154	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
155	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
156	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
157	        assert!(!red_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "side-to-move sees moves");
158	        assert!(black_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "opponent doesn't");
159	    }
160	
161	    #[test]

163	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
164	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
165	        for m in &view.legal_moves {

168	            }
169	        }

171	
172	    #[test]
173	    fn fresh_xiangqi_view_has_neither_side_in_check() {
174	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
175	        let red = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
176	        let black = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
177	        assert!(!red.in_check, "starting xiangqi: red not in check");
178	        assert!(!black.in_check, "starting xiangqi: black not in check");
179	    }
180	
181	    #[test]
182	    fn xiangqi_in_check_view_flags_observer() {
183	        // Three-chariot mating net (same as tests/fixtures/xiangqi/three-chariot-mate.pos):
184	        // Red rooks on d8/e8/f8, Black general on e9, Red general on e0.
185	        // Black is in check; red is not.
186	        let pos = "variant: xiangqi\nside_to_move: black\n\nboard:\n  . . . . k . . . .\n  . . . R R R . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . . . . . .\n  . . . . K . . . .\n";
187	        let state = GameState::from_pos_text(pos).expect("parse pos");
188	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
189	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
190	        assert!(black_view.in_check, "black observer must see in_check = true");
191	        assert!(!red_view.in_check, "red observer must see in_check = false");
192	    }
193	
194	    #[test]
195	    fn banqi_view_never_flags_in_check() {
196	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 13));
197	        for side in [Side::RED, Side::BLACK] {

205	        // The smoke-level version of the proptest in tests/view_projection.rs.
206	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 42));

211	        // invariant.)

213	        {

218	        }

## Summary

**Chess-TUI Sidebar Layout** (`ui.rs`):

1. **Local mode** (`draw_sidebar`, ~869–979 lines):
   - Block header: `" Status "`
   - Variant label (Xiangqi/Banqi label)
   - Side-to-move (piece color)
   - Check banner (xiangqi only)
   - Status line
   - Legal moves count
   - History count
   - Selected square
   - Help lines or keymap hints
   - **Constraint**: `Constraint::Length(36)` in horizontal split (line 90). Uses `Paragraph::new(lines).wrap()` pattern. A new captured block would slot before the help section (around line 948).

2. **Net mode** (`draw_sidebar_net`, ~981–1145 lines):
   - Splits into meta (top) and chat (bottom) regions via `Direction::Vertical` (line 1001)
   - Meta section: variant, your role, turn, check badge, status, legal moves, selected, server URL, connection
   - Chat pane below with message log + input row
   - **Constraint**: `MIN_META_ROWS = 14` (line 1147). A captured block would fit in meta region before chat split.

**Pattern**: `line_label_value(label, value)` helper at line 1448 wraps common "Label: value" formatting.

**Chess-TUI Glyph API** (`glyph.rs`, lines 16–97):

```rust
pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn side_name(side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn hidden(style: Style) -> &'static str
```

All return 2-character wide strings (CJK chars are rendered as double-width; ASCII padded). Call with `Style::Cjk` or `Style::Ascii`.

---

**Chess-Web Sidebar** (`components/sidebar.rs`):

- **Local mode**: Lines 18–112. Leptos `#[component]` with props: `variant`, `view: Signal<PlayerView>`, `on_new_game`, `on_undo`, `wip` flag.
- **JSX structure** (lines 88–111):
  ```
  <aside class="sidebar">
    <h3 class="variant-label">
    <Show when=!wip>
      <Show when=show_check>...</Show>
      <div class="turn-row">...</div>
      {status_view}
      <p class="muted">legal count</p>
    </Show>
    <div class="sidebar-actions">...</div>
    <FxToggles/>
    <a class="back-link">...</a>
  </aside>
  ```
- **New block**: Would fit after status and before actions (line 104).

**Chess-Web Sidebar CSS** (`style.css`, lines 295–325):
```css
.sidebar {
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.65rem;
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
}
.variant-label { font-size: 1.1rem; }
.turn-red/black/green { color: var(--red/fg/green); font-weight: 700; }
.status { font-weight: 600; }
.muted { color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.9rem; }
.sidebar-actions { display: flex; gap: 0.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; }
```

Grid layout with auto-flowing rows. Add a `.captured-row` as a new grid item.

---

**Chess-Web Board Piece Rendering** (`components/board.rs`, lines 145–162):

Pieces render as SVG `<g>` groups with class `tile piece {side_class}`, containing:
```leptos
<g class=format!("tile piece {}", side_class)>
  <circle class="tile-disc" r=TILE_RADIUS/>
  <text class="tile-glyph" text-anchor="middle">
    {glyph::glyph(p.piece.kind, p.piece.side, style)}
  </text>
</g>
```

`side_class` is `"red"`, `"black"`, or `"green"` (line 146–149). CSS classes available: `.tile.piece.red`, `.tile.piece.black`, etc.

**Chess-Web Glyph API** (`glyph.rs`):
```rust
pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn side_name(side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn hidden(style: Style) -> &'static str
```

Same as TUI. Glyphs are unpadded in web version (test at line 102 doesn't check `.len()`; ASCII is single chars).

`OnlineSidebar()` is a separate net-only component. **Local mode** uses `<Sidebar/>` (in `local.rs`, line 11 import; invoked ~70–75 in `LocalGame`). Props:

<Sidebar
  variant=variant

1	//! Piece glyph tables. Two render styles: traditional CJK (帥/將 etc.) and
2	//! ASCII single-letter (uppercase = Red, lowercase = Black). The engine has
3	//! no concept of glyphs — presentation lives entirely in the client.
4	//!
5	//! Verbatim copy of `clients/chess-tui/src/glyph.rs` (see orient.rs note).
6	
7	use chess_core::piece::{PieceKind, Side};
8	
9	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
10	pub enum Style {
11	    Cjk,
12	    Ascii,
13	}
14	
15	pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str {
16	    match style {
17	        Style::Cjk => cjk(kind, side),
18	        Style::Ascii => ascii(kind, side),
19	    }
20	}
21

1	//! Piece glyph tables. Two render styles: traditional CJK (帥/將 etc.) and
2	//! ASCII single-letter (uppercase = Red, lowercase = Black). The engine has
3	//! no concept of glyphs — presentation lives entirely in the client.
4	
5	use chess_core::piece::{PieceKind, Side};
6	
7	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
8	pub enum Style {
9	    Cjk,
10	    Ascii,
11	}
12	
13	/// Two-column-wide string for a piece. CJK characters are double-width in a
14	/// monospace terminal; ASCII glyphs are padded with a trailing space so cells
15	/// align in either mode.
16	pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str {
17	    match style {
18	        Style::Cjk => cjk(kind, side),
19	        Style::Ascii => ascii(kind, side),
20	    }
21	}
22	
23	fn cjk(kind: PieceKind, side: Side) -> &'static str {
24	    match (side, kind) {
25	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::General) => "帥",
26	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Advisor) => "仕",
27	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Elephant) => "相",
28	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot) => "俥",
29	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse) => "傌",
30	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Cannon) => "炮",
31	        (Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier) => "兵",
32	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General) => "將",
33	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Advisor) => "士",
34	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant) => "象",
35	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Chariot) => "車",
36	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse) => "馬",
37	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon) => "砲",
38	        (Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier) => "卒",
39	        // 三國暗棋 — Green seat. Reuse Black glyphs for now; PR-2 may pick a
40	        // distinct glyph set (e.g. 君/臣/...) once the variant ships.
41	        (_, PieceKind::General) => "將",
42	        (_, PieceKind::Advisor) => "士",
43	        (_, PieceKind::Elephant) => "象",
44	        (_, PieceKind::Chariot) => "車",
45	        (_, PieceKind::Horse) => "馬",
46	        (_, PieceKind::Cannon) => "砲",
47	        (_, PieceKind::Soldier) => "卒",

49	}
