 `./clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`

```text
1	//! Sidebar: variant + turn + status + history-length + undo / new-game buttons.
2	//!
3	//! `wip=true` (three-kingdom banqi until the engine ships) hides turn /
4	//! legal-move counts and disables the action buttons — the board overlay
5	//! tells the user what's happening; the sidebar just stays out of the way.
6	
7	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
8	use chess_core::piece::Side;
9	use chess_core::rules::Variant;
10	use chess_core::state::{DrawReason, GameStatus, WinReason};
11	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
12	use leptos::*;
13	
14	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
15	use crate::prefs::Prefs;
16	
17	#[component]
18	pub fn Sidebar(
19	    variant: Variant,
20	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
21	    #[prop(into)] on_new_game: Callback<()>,
22	    #[prop(into)] on_undo: Callback<()>,
23	    #[prop(default = false)] wip: bool,
24	) -> impl IntoView {
25	    let style = Style::Cjk;
26	    let variant_label = match variant {
27	        Variant::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi 象棋",
28	        Variant::Banqi => "Banqi 暗棋",
29	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "Three-Kingdom 三國暗棋",
30	    };
31	
32	    // Display the piece-colour to move, not the seat name. After a banqi
33	    // first-flip the seat (`side_to_move`) and the colour the seat plays
34	    // (`current_color`) diverge — players think in terms of colour.
35	    let turn_text = move || {
36	        let v = view.get();
37	        glyph::side_name(v.current_color, style).to_string()
38	    };
39	    let turn_class = move || {
40	        let v = view.get();
41	        match v.current_color {
42	            Side::RED => "turn-red",
43	            Side::BLACK => "turn-black",
44	            _ => "turn-green",
45	        }
46	    };
47	
48	    let status_view = move || -> View {
49	        let v = view.get();
50	        match v.status {
51	            GameStatus::Ongoing => view! { <p class="status ongoing">"Ongoing"</p> }.into_view(),
52	            GameStatus::Won { winner, reason } => {
53	                let side = glyph::side_name(winner, style);
54	                let reason = win_reason_label(reason);
55	                view! {
56	                    <p class="status won">
57	                        {format!("{} wins — {}", side, reason)}
58	                    </p>
59	                }
60	                .into_view()
61	            }
62	            GameStatus::Drawn { reason } => view! {
63	                <p class="status drawn">
64	                    {format!("Drawn — {}", draw_reason_label(reason))}
65	                </p>
66	            }
67	            .into_view(),
68	        }
69	    };
70	
71	    let legal_count = move || view.with(|v| v.legal_moves.len());
72	
73	    // Pull FX prefs from context (provided by `app.rs::App`). Both signals
74	    // default to true on first visit and persist via localStorage.
75	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
76	    let fx_confetti = prefs.fx_confetti;
77	    let fx_check_banner = prefs.fx_check_banner;
78	
79	    let show_check = move || {
80	        let v = view.get();
81	        // Check banner is xiangqi-only and gated by the user pref.
82	        fx_check_banner.get()
83	            && matches!(v.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10)
84	            && v.in_check
85	            && matches!(v.status, GameStatus::Ongoing)
86	    };
87	
88	    view! {
89	        <aside class="sidebar">
90	            <h3 class="variant-label">{variant_label}</h3>
91	            <Show when=move || !wip fallback=|| view! {
92	                <p class="status drawn">"Engine WIP — board interaction disabled."</p>
93	            }>
94	                <Show when=show_check>
95	                    <div class="check-badge" role="status">"⚠ 將軍 / CHECK"</div>
96	                </Show>
97	                <div class="turn-row">
98	                    <span class="turn-prefix">"Turn: "</span>
99	                    <span class=turn_class>{turn_text}</span>
100	                </div>
101	                {status_view}
102	                <p class="muted">{move || format!("{} legal moves", legal_count())}</p>
103	            </Show>
104	            <div class="sidebar-actions">
105	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_undo.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"Undo"</button>
106	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_new_game.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"New game"</button>
107	            </div>
108	            <FxToggles fx_confetti=fx_confetti fx_check_banner=fx_check_banner/>
109	            <a class="back-link" href="/">"← Back to picker"</a>
110	        </aside>
111	    }
112	}
113	
114	#[component]
115	fn FxToggles(fx_confetti: RwSignal<bool>, fx_check_banner: RwSignal<bool>) -> impl IntoView {
116	    view! {
117	        <div class="fx-toggles">
118	            <label>
119	                <input
120	                    type="checkbox"
121	                    prop:checked=move || fx_confetti.get()
122	                    on:change=move |ev| fx_confetti.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
123	                />
124	                <span>"Victory effects (confetti + banner)"</span>
125	            </label>
126	            <label>
127	                <input
128	                    type="checkbox"
129	                    prop:checked=move || fx_check_banner.get()
130	                    on:change=move |ev| fx_check_banner.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
131	                />
132	                <span>"將軍 / CHECK warning"</span>
133	            </label>
134	        </div>
135	    }
136	}
137	
138	fn win_reason_label(reason: WinReason) -> &'static str {
139	    match reason {
140	        WinReason::Checkmate => "checkmate 將死",
141	        WinReason::Stalemate => "stalemate 困死",
142	        WinReason::Resignation => "resignation",
143	        WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces => "only one side has pieces",
144	        WinReason::Timeout => "timeout",
145	        WinReason::GeneralCaptured => "general captured",
146	    }
147	}
148	
149	fn draw_reason_label(reason: DrawReason) -> &'static str {
150	    match reason {
151	        DrawReason::NoProgress => "no progress",
152	        DrawReason::Repetition => "repetition",
153	        DrawReason::Agreed => "agreed",
154	        DrawReason::InsufficientMaterial => "insufficient material",
155	    }
156	}
157
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
2	use chess_core::coord::Square;
3	use chess_core::piece::Side;
4	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
5	use chess_core::state::GameStatus;
6	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
7	use chess_net::protocol::{ChatLine, ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
8	use leptos::*;
9	use leptos_router::{use_params_map, use_query_map};
10	
11	use crate::components::board::Board;
12	use crate::components::chat_panel::ChatPanel;
13	use crate::components::end_overlay::EndOverlay;
14	use crate::config::room_url;
15	use crate::prefs::Prefs;
16	use crate::routes::variant_slug;
17	use crate::state::{end_chain_move, find_move, truncate_front, ClientRole};
18	use crate::ws::{connect, ConnState, WsHandle};
19	
20	const CHAT_HISTORY_CAP: usize = 50;
21	
22	#[component]
23	pub fn PlayPage() -> impl IntoView {
24	    let params = use_params_map();
25	    let query = use_query_map();
26	    let room = move || params.with(|p| p.get("room").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
27	    let password = move || query.with(|q| q.get("password").cloned().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()));
28	    let watch_only = move || {
29	        query.with(|q| q.get("role").map(|r| r.eq_ignore_ascii_case("spectator")).unwrap_or(false))
30	    };
31	
32	    // Open the WS once on mount. Spectator opt-in propagates into the URL.
33	    let url = room_url(&room(), password().as_deref(), watch_only());
34	    let (handle, incoming, conn) = connect(url);
35	
36	    let (rules, set_rules) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None);
37	    let (role, set_role) = create_signal::<Option<ClientRole>>(None);
38	    let (view_signal, set_view) = create_signal::<Option<PlayerView>>(None);
39	    let (chat, set_chat) = create_signal::<Vec<ChatLine>>(Vec::new());
40	    let (toast, set_toast) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
41	
42	    create_effect(move |_| match incoming.get() {
43	        Some(ServerMsg::Hello { observer, rules: r, view: v, .. }) => {
44	            set_role.set(Some(ClientRole::Player(observer)));
45	            set_rules.set(Some(r));
46	            set_view.set(Some(v));
47	            set_toast.set(None);
48	        }
49	        Some(ServerMsg::Spectating { rules: r, view: v, .. }) => {
50	            set_role.set(Some(ClientRole::Spectator));
51	            set_rules.set(Some(r));
52	            set_view.set(Some(v));
53	            set_toast.set(None);
54	        }
55	        Some(ServerMsg::Update { view: v }) => {
56	            set_view.set(Some(v));
57	        }
58	        Some(ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines }) => {
59	            set_chat.set(lines);
60	        }
61	        Some(ServerMsg::Chat { line }) => {
62	            set_chat.update(|buf| {
63	                buf.push(line);
64	                truncate_front(buf, CHAT_HISTORY_CAP);
65	            });
66	        }
67	        Some(ServerMsg::Error { message }) => {
68	            set_toast.set(Some(message));
69	        }
70	        Some(ServerMsg::Rooms { .. }) | None => {}
71	    });
72	
73	    let chat_handle = handle.clone();
74	    let on_chat_send: Callback<String> = Callback::new(move |text: String| {
75	        chat_handle.send(ClientMsg::Chat { text });
76	    });
77	
78	    let handle_for_board = handle.clone();
79	    let handle_for_sidebar = handle;
80	    let room_label = room();
81	
82	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
83	    let fx_confetti: Signal<bool> = prefs.fx_confetti.into();
84	    // The overlay needs a non-`Option` view signal once the welcome lands.
85	    // Wrap in a `Show` so we don't mount it pre-welcome (and so the watcher
86	    // inside doesn't see a fake "transition" on first connect).
87	    let view_for_overlay = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get());
88	    let role_for_overlay: Signal<Option<ClientRole>> = role.into();
89	
90	    view! {
91	        <section class="game-page">
92	            <div class="board-pane">
93	                <Show
94	                    when=move || view_signal.get().is_some() && role.get().is_some()
95	                    fallback=move || view! { <ConnPlaceholder room=room() conn=conn/> }
96	                >
97	                    <BoardWrapper
98	                        view_signal=view_signal
99	                        role=role
100	                        handle=handle_for_board.clone()
```
</details></tool-use>

1	use chess_core::coord::Square;
2	use chess_core::piece::Side;

5	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};

9	use crate::components::board::Board;

15	
16	#[component]
17	pub fn LocalPage() -> impl IntoView {
18	    let params = use_params_map();
19	    let query = use_query_map();
20	    let resolved = move || -> Result<(Variant, RuleSet, bool), String> {
21	        let slug = params.with(|p| p.get("variant").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
22	        let variant =
23	            parse_variant_slug(&slug).ok_or_else(|| format!("Unknown variant: {}", slug))?;
24	        let parsed = query.with(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
25	        let rules = build_rule_set(variant, &parsed);
26	        // Three-kingdom: engine ships an empty 4×8 board. Render it but
27	        // disable interaction and overlay a "WIP" banner — see
28	        // `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` and the gotcha in CLAUDE.md.
29	        let wip = matches!(variant, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi);
30	        Ok((variant, rules, wip))
31	    };
32	
33	    move || match resolved() {
34	        Ok((variant, rules, wip)) => {
35	            view! { <LocalGame variant=variant rules=rules wip=wip/> }.into_view()
36	        }
37	        Err(msg) => view! {
38	            <section class="game-page game-page--single">
39	                <div>
40	                    <a href="/" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
41	                    <h2>"Variant unavailable"</h2>
42	                    <p class="subtitle">{msg}</p>
43	                </div>
44	            </section>
45	        }
46	        .into_view(),
47	    }
48	}
49	
50	#[component]
51	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool) -> impl IntoView {
52	    let initial_rules = rules.clone();
53	    let state = create_rw_signal(GameState::new(rules));
54	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
55	
56	    // Local pass-and-play renders from a fixed Red-side observer (board never
57	    // flips on turn change). Legal moves come from the projection for the
58	    // *current* side-to-move so click-to-move uses the right list.
59	    let observer = Side::RED;
60	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
61	
62	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
63	
64	    // The chain-locked piece (if any) renders with the same affordance as
65	    // a manually-selected piece, so the user sees it visually highlighted.
66	    let chain_locked_signal: Signal<Option<Square>> =
67	        Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.chain_lock));
68	
69	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
70	        // In chain mode the engine dictates which piece moves; surface
71	        // chain_lock as the selected square so the legal-target dots
72	        // render around it.
73	        chain_locked_signal.get().or_else(|| selected.get())
74	    });
75	
76	    let on_end_chain: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
77	        if wip {
78	            return;
79	        }
80	        let v = view.get();
81	        if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
82	            state.update(|s| {
83	                s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
84	                s.refresh_status();
85	            });
86	            selected.set(None);
87	        }
88	    });
89	
90	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
91	        if wip {
92	            return;
93	        }
94	        let v = view.get();
95	
96	        // Chain mode is engine-driven: when chain_lock is set, only the
97	        // locked piece can move (captures only). Clicking the locked
98	        // piece itself is the explicit "end chain" gesture.
99	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
100	            if sq == locked {

133	#[component]
134	fn ConnPlaceholder(room: String, conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
135	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
136	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting…".to_string(),
137	        ConnState::Open => "Waiting for server greeting…".to_string(),
138	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected — refresh to reconnect.".to_string(),
139	        ConnState::Error => "Connection error — is the server running?".to_string(),
140	    };
141	    view! {
142	        <div class="conn-placeholder">
143	            <p class="muted">{format!("Room: {}", room)}</p>
144	            <p>{label}</p>
145	        </div>
146	    }
147	}
148	
149	#[component]
150	fn BoardWrapper(
151	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
152	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
153	    handle: WsHandle,
154	) -> impl IntoView {
155	    let view = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get().expect("guarded by Show"));
156	    let resolved_role = role.get_untracked().expect("guarded by Show");
157	    let obs = resolved_role.observer();
158	    let shape = view.with_untracked(|v| v.shape);
159	
160	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
161	    // Spectators see-only — no click handlers on the board for them.
162	    let is_spectator = resolved_role.is_spectator();
163	
164	    // Surface engine chain_lock as the highlighted "selected" piece, so
165	    // legal-target dots render around it during chain mode.
166	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
167	        let v = view.get();
168	        v.chain_lock.or_else(|| selected.get())
169	    });
170	
171	    let click_handle = handle.clone();
172	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
173	        if is_spectator {
174	            return;
175	        }
176	        let v = view.get();
177	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
178	            return;
179	        }
180	
181	        // Chain mode: only the locked piece may move (captures only).
182	        // Clicking the locked piece itself releases the chain.
183	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
184	            if sq == locked {
185	                if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
186	                    click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
187	                }
188	                selected.set(None);
189	                return;
190	            }
191	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
192	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
193	                selected.set(None);
194	                return;

```text
1	//! SVG board renderer. Variant-agnostic: takes a `PlayerView` + observer +
2	//! a click callback, and lays cells on the intersection grid that the TUI
3	//! also uses (see `crate::orient`).
4	
5	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
6	use chess_core::coord::Square;
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
9	use leptos::*;
10	
11	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
12	use crate::orient::{display_dims, square_at_display};
13	use crate::state::legal_targets;
14	
15	pub const CELL: i32 = 60;
16	pub const PAD: i32 = 30;
17	const RIVER_TOP_ROW: u8 = 4;
18	const RIVER_BOT_ROW: u8 = 5;
19	const TILE_RADIUS: i32 = 24;
20	
21	#[component]
22	pub fn Board(
23	    shape: BoardShape,
24	    observer: Side,
25	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
26	    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
27	    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
28	) -> impl IntoView {
29	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
30	    let view_w = (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
31	    let view_h = (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
32	    let viewbox = format!("0 0 {} {}", view_w, view_h);
33	    let style = Style::Cjk;
34	
35	    view! {
36	        <svg class="board" viewBox=viewbox preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
37	            <rect class="board-bg" x="0" y="0" width="100%" height="100%"/>
38	            <g class="grid-layer">{grid_lines(shape, rows, cols)}</g>
39	            <g class="river-layer">{river_text(shape, cols)}</g>
40	            <g class="palace-layer">{palace_diagonals(shape)}</g>
41	            <g class="overlay-layer">
42	                {move || chain_lock_marker(view.get().chain_lock, observer, shape)}
43	                {move || selection_marker(selected.get(), observer, shape)}
44	                {move || move_dots_view(&view.get(), selected.get(), observer, shape)}
45	            </g>
46	            <g class="pieces-layer">
47	                {move || pieces_view(&view.get(), observer, shape, style)}
48	            </g>
49	            <g class="cells-layer">
50	                {hit_cells(rows, cols, observer, shape, on_click)}
51	            </g>
52	        </svg>
53	    }
54	}
55	
56	#[inline]
57	fn intersection(row: u8, col: u8) -> (i32, i32) {
58	    (PAD + col as i32 * CELL, PAD + row as i32 * CELL)
59	}
60	
61	fn grid_lines(shape: BoardShape, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> View {
62	    let is_xiangqi = matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
63	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize + cols as usize * 2);
64	    for r in 0..rows {
65	        let y = PAD + r as i32 * CELL;
66	        let x1 = PAD;
67	        let x2 = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL;
68	        out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x1 y1=y x2=x2 y2=y/> }.into_view());
69	    }
70	    for c in 0..cols {
71	        let x = PAD + c as i32 * CELL;
72	        let is_border = c == 0 || c == cols - 1;
73	        let y_top = PAD;
74	        let y_bot = PAD + (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL;
75	        if is_xiangqi && !is_border {
76	            let y_river_top = PAD + RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL;
77	            let y_river_bot = PAD + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL;
78	            out.push(
79	                view! {
80	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_river_top/>
```

125	fn pieces_view(view: &PlayerView, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape, style: Style) -> View {
126	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
127	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
128	    for row in 0..rows {
129	        for col in 0..cols {
130	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
131	            let cell = view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
132	            let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
133	            match cell {
134	                VisibleCell::Empty => {}
135	                VisibleCell::Hidden => {
136	                    out.push(
137	                        view! {
138	                            <g class="tile hidden" transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
139	                                <circle class="tile-back" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
140	                            </g>
141	                        }
142	                        .into_view(),
143	                    );
144	                }
145	                VisibleCell::Revealed(p) => {
146	                    let side_class = match p.piece.side {
147	                        Side::RED => "red",
148	                        Side::BLACK => "black",
149	                        _ => "green",
150	                    };
151	                    out.push(
152	                        view! {
153	                            <g class=format!("tile piece {}", side_class) transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
154	                                <circle class="tile-disc" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
155	                                <text class="tile-glyph" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
156	                                    {glyph::glyph(p.piece.kind, p.piece.side, style)}
157	                                </text>
158	                            </g>
159	                        }
160	                        .into_view(),
161	                    );
162	                }
163	            }
164	        }
165	    }
166	    out.into_view()
167	}
168	
169	fn selection_marker(selected: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
170	    let Some(sq) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
171	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
172	    for row in 0..rows {
173	        for col in 0..cols {
174	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
175	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
176	                return view! { <circle class="selection" cx=cx cy=cy r="28"/> }.into_view();
177	            }
178	        }
179	    }
180	    ().into_view()
181	}
182	
183	fn chain_lock_marker(locked: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
184	    let Some(sq) = locked else { return ().into_view() };

134	fn ConnPlaceholder(room: String, conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
135	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
136	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting…".to_string(),

140	    };

144	            <p>{label}</p>
145	        </div>
146	    }
147	}

149	#[component]
150	fn BoardWrapper(
151	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
152	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
153	    handle: WsHandle,
154	) -> impl IntoView {
155	    let view = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get().expect("guarded by Show"));
156	    let resolved_role = role.get_untracked().expect("guarded by Show");
157	    let obs = resolved_role.observer();
158	    let shape = view.with_untracked(|v| v.shape);
159	
160	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
161	    // Spectators see-only — no click handlers on the board for them.
162	    let is_spectator = resolved_role.is_spectator();
163	
164	    // Surface engine chain_lock as the highlighted "selected" piece, so
165	    // legal-target dots render around it during chain mode.
166	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
167	        let v = view.get();
168	        v.chain_lock.or_else(|| selected.get())
169	    });
170	
171	    let click_handle = handle.clone();
172	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
173	        if is_spectator {
174	            return;
175	        }
176	        let v = view.get();
177	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
178	            return;
179	        }
180	
181	        // Chain mode: only the locked piece may move (captures only).
182	        // Clicking the locked piece itself releases the chain.
183	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
184	            if sq == locked {
185	                if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
186	                    click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
187	                }
188	                selected.set(None);
189	                return;
190	            }
191	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
192	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
193	                selected.set(None);
194	                return;
195	            }
196	            return;
197	        }
198	
199	        let cur = selected.get();
200	        if cur == Some(sq) {
201	            selected.set(None);
202	            return;
203	        }
204	        if let Some(from) = cur {
205	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
206	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
207	                selected.set(None);
208	                return;
209	            }
210	        }
211	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
212	        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))
213	            && v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
214	        if is_selectable_revealed {
215	            selected.set(Some(sq));
216	        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
217	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
218	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
219	                selected.set(None);
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-07 11:07:17Z)**_

Excellent. Now I have the complete picture. Let me provide my findings:

## Captured Pieces Recording — Analysis Report

### **1. MoveRecord / History** (`src/state/history.rs:9-20`)
**Current state:** NO dedicated capture-list field. `MoveRecord` only tracks:
- `mover: Side` (which side moved)
- `the_move: Move` (the move itself—see below)
- `no_progress_before: u16` (ply counter snapshot)
- `chain_lock_before: Option<Square>` (v5: chain-mode lock before move)

**Captured pieces are embedded inside the `Move` enum variant**, not in the record wrapper. This means capture info is available but requires iterating `state.history` and inspecting each `the_move`.

### **2. GameState** (`src/state/mod.rs:56-74`)
NO graveyard/cemetery field exists. The `GameState` struct contains:
- `board: Board`
- `history: Vec<MoveRecord>`
- `side_to_move`, `turn_order`, `status`, etc.
- `no_progress_plies: u16`
- `chain_lock: Option<Square>` (v5)

Captured pieces are ONLY preserved inside `MoveRecord.the_move` variants. There's no separate `captures: Vec<Piece>` or similar.

### **3. Move Variants** (`src/moves.rs:19-60`)
**Capture-carrying variants:**
- **`Move::Capture { from, to, captured: Piece }`** (line 29): Single capture records the `Piece`.
- **`Move::ChainCapture { from, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> }`** (line 34): Multi-hop chain. Each hop carries:
  ```rust
  pub struct ChainHop {
      pub to: Square,
      pub captured: Piece,
  }
  ```
  (lines 57–60) So every intermediate capture is preserved per-hop.
- **`Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen, captured: Piece }`** (line 37): Cannon screen-jump records the captured piece.
- **`Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> }`** (line 45): Banqi dark-capture stores both the revealed target AND the attacker. **Outcome (Capture/Trade/Probe) is recomputed at undo time** via `dark_capture_outcome()` (lines 563–579); the outcome isn't stored — see `state/mod.rs:330-368` for apply, lines 415–443 for unapply.

### **4. PlayerView Projection** (`src/view.rs:17-66`)
NO captured-pieces field exposed. `PlayerView` contains:
- `cells: Vec<VisibleCell>` (board state)
- `legal_moves`, `status`, `side_to_move`, `chain_lock`, `current_color`

**ADR-0004 compliance:** Captured pieces are safe to expose in UI—they were revealed at capture-time, so no hidden-identity leak. The view just doesn't currently expose them.

### **5. Piece / PieceKind** (`src/piece.rs:31-88`)
- `PieceKind` enum (7 kinds: General, Advisor, Elephant, Chariot, Horse, Cannon, Soldier)
- `Piece` struct: `{ side: Side, kind: PieceKind }` (line 78)
- `PieceOnSquare`: wraps `Piece` + `revealed: bool` (line 93)

**Glyph rendering:** Both `clients/chess-web/src/glyph.rs:15-20` and `clients/chess-tui/src/glyph.rs:16-20` export:
```rust
pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
```
So you already have piece→glyph mapping. Safe to reuse for "dead pieces" list.

---

## Recommendation: Architecture Choice

**Option (a) — Compute on-the-fly from history:**
- ✅ Zero protocol impact; protocol v5 already shipped
- ✅ No `GameState` mutation; cleaner
- ❌ Every UI render loops `state.history`, iterates captures
- ✅ Correct for replay (you traverse the full history anyway)

**Option (b) — Add `captures: Vec<CaptureLogEntry>` to GameState:**
- ❌ **Protocol bump required** (v5→v6) even though the field is "computed"
- ✅ O(1) access for UI sidebar render
- ⚠️ Must maintain during `make_move` / `unmake_move` (lines 104–170); be careful with undo

**For a banqi sidebar with potentially 16 captured pieces, compute-on-the-fly is fast enough.** Chain captures (up to 4 hops in the `SmallVec`) are already atomic in history, so you iterate move records and flatten them:

```
for record in state.history:
  match record.the_move {
    Capture { captured, .. } => captures.push(captured),
    ChainCapture { path, .. } => captures.extend(path.iter().map(|h| h.captured)),
    CannonJump { captured, .. } => captures.push(captured),
    DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {
      // Only add 'revealed' if outcome was Capture (recompute outcome)
      // Probe adds nothing, Trade adds only revealed
    }
    _ => {}
  }
```

**Verdict:** Stay with **(a)** unless sidebar performance becomes a bottleneck. No protocol change needed.

Perfect! Now let me gather all the essential info. Let me create a final summary:

## Summary

**Chess-TUI Sidebar Layout** (`ui.rs`):

1. **Local mode** (`draw_sidebar`, ~869–979 lines):
   - Block header: `" Status "`
   - Variant label (Xiangqi/Banqi label)
   - Side-to-move (piece color)
   - Check banner (xiangqi only)
   - Status line
   - Legal moves count
   - History count
   - Selected square
   - Help lines or keymap hints
   - **Constraint**: `Constraint::Length(36)` in horizontal split (line 90). Uses `Paragraph::new(lines).wrap()` pattern. A new captured block would slot before the help section (around line 948).

2. **Net mode** (`draw_sidebar_net`, ~981–1145 lines):
   - Splits into meta (top) and chat (bottom) regions via `Direction::Vertical` (line 1001)
   - Meta section: variant, your role, turn, check badge, status, legal moves, selected, server URL, connection
   - Chat pane below with message log + input row
   - **Constraint**: `MIN_META_ROWS = 14` (line 1147). A captured block would fit in meta region before chat split.

**Pattern**: `line_label_value(label, value)` helper at line 1448 wraps common "Label: value" formatting.

**Chess-TUI Glyph API** (`glyph.rs`, lines 16–97):

```rust
pub fn glyph(kind: PieceKind, side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn side_name(side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str
pub fn hidden(style: Style) -> &'static str
```

All return 2-character wide strings (CJK chars are rendered as double-width; ASCII padded). Call with `Style::Cjk` or `Style::Ascii`.

---

**Chess-Web Sidebar** (`components/sidebar.rs`):

- **Local mode**: Lines 18–112. Leptos `#[component]` with props: `variant`, `view: Signal<PlayerView>`, `on_new_game`, `on_undo`, `wip` flag.
- **JSX structure** (lines 88–111):
  ```

    <h3 class="variant-label">
    <Show when=!wip>
      <Show when=show_check>...</Show>
      <div class="turn-row">...</div>
      {status_view}
      <p class="muted">legal count</p>
    </Show>

```

---

**Chess-Web Board Piece Rendering** (`components/board.rs`, lines 145–162):

Pieces render as SVG `<g>` groups with class `tile piece {side_class}`, containing:
```leptos
<g class=format!("tile piece {}", side_class)>
  <circle class="tile-disc" r=TILE_RADIUS/>
  <text class="tile-glyph" text-anchor="middle">
    {glyph::glyph(p.piece.kind, p.piece.side, style)}
  </text>
</g>
```

`side_class` is `"red"`, `"black"`, or `"green"` (line 146–149). CSS classes available: `.tile.piece.red`, `.tile.piece.black`, etc.

**Chess-Web Glyph API** (`glyph.rs`):
```rust

pub fn side_name(side: Side, style: Style) -> &'static str

```

Same as TUI. Glyphs are unpadded in web version (test at line 102 doesn't check `.len()`; ASCII is single chars).

`OnlineSidebar()` is a separate net-only component. **Local mode** uses `<Sidebar/>` (in `local.rs`, line 11 import; invoked ~70–75 in `LocalGame`). Props:

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How should the captured pieces be sorted in the panel by default?**

Options:
- **Chronological (capture time)** - Most recent capture is rightmost. Best for following 暗連 chains in real time — you can see the order pieces died.
- **By rank (largest → smallest)** - Sorts by piece value (將 → 士 → 象 → 車 → 馬 → 包 → 兵). Easier to scan material balance, loses chronological info.
- **Toggle in sidebar** - Ship both, with a small toggle button. More UI work, but lets each player choose.


**Answer:** Toggle in sidebar
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```md
# Banqi: captured-pieces ("graveyard") panel in sidebar

## Context

During a banqi (暗棋) game — especially when 連吃 chain captures are
active — players can't tell which pieces have already been taken. A
single chain move can capture 2–4 pieces atomically and the only
record is the move log, which lists ICCS notation, not piece glyphs.

Add a "captured pieces" panel to both clients (chess-tui + chess-web),
showing per-side glyphs of every captured piece. Useful for xiangqi
too (material count at a glance), so we ship for both variants.

User asked for two sort orders: chronological (capture time) and by
rank (largest → smallest). Confirmed via AskUserQuestion: ship both
behind a toggle.

## Approach

Capture data already lives in `state.history` — every `Move` variant
that captures preserves the captured `Piece` for `unmake_move`. So we
add a thin engine helper that walks the history and emits a flat
`Vec<Piece>` in chronological order, expose it on `PlayerView`, and
render it in both clients with a sort toggle.

Wire shape: `PlayerView.captured: Vec<Piece>` with `#[serde(default)]`.
This is the same back-compat pattern already used for `in_check` (v4),
`chain_lock` + `current_color` (v5). No protocol bump needed — v5 just
shipped this session and tolerates field additions on `PlayerView` via
the existing serde-default convention. (If the user wants a clean
v5→v6 bump for record-keeping, that's a one-line change to whichever
const tracks the protocol number — surfaced if/when we find it.)

### Engine helper: `GameState::captured_pieces()`

`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` — add:

\```rust
/// All pieces captured so far, in chronological (history) order.
/// Used by `PlayerView` for sidebar rendering. Returns the captured
/// piece's full identity — safe under ADR-0004 because every captured
/// piece was revealed at capture time.
pub fn captured_pieces(&self) -> Vec<Piece> {
    let mut out = Vec::new();
    for record in &self.history {
        match &record.the_move {
            Move::Capture { captured, .. }
            | Move::CannonJump { captured, .. } => out.push(*captured),
            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => {
                out.extend(path.iter().map(|hop| hop.captured));
            }
            Move::DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {
                let (Some(rev), Some(att)) = (*revealed, *attacker) else { continue };
                match dark_capture_outcome(att, rev, self.rules.house) {
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => out.push(rev),
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade   => out.push(att),
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe   => {}
                }
            }
            _ => {}
        }
    }
    out
}
\```

`dark_capture_outcome` and `DarkCaptureOutcome` are already private to
`state/mod.rs` (added in the prior session — see `state/mod.rs:563`),
so the helper sits in the same file with direct access. No new public
API beyond `captured_pieces` itself.

### View projection

`crates/chess-core/src/view.rs` — add field:

\```rust
/// Pieces captured so far (chronological). Always present; clients
/// sort/group as needed. Added in protocol v5.1; older clients see
/// the empty default via `serde(default)`.
#[serde(default)]
pub captured: Vec<Piece>,
\```

Populate in `PlayerView::project()`:

\```rust
captured: state.captured_pieces(),
\```

### chess-tui rendering (`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`)

Add a `Captured` block in both `draw_sidebar` (~L869–979) and
`draw_sidebar_net` (~L981–1145), inserted before the help / chat
region. Two-line content:

\```
┌ Captured ────────────────┐
│ R 紅: 兵 兵 馬           │  (red text)
│ B 黑: 卒 卒              │  (dim/dark text)
└──────────────────────────┘
\```

Glyphs come from existing `glyph::glyph(kind, side, style)` (TUI
glyph.rs:16). Style by side using `Style::default().fg(Color::Red)`
for red glyphs and the existing dim/black colour for black.
`Paragraph::wrap` handles overflow if a row gets long.

Sort toggle:
- New `AppState` field: `captured_sort: CapturedSort` (`{ Time, Rank }`).
- New keybinding: `g` (graveyard sort) — toggles `Time ↔ Rank`. `g`
  is currently unbound (existing keys: `hjkl`/arrows, Enter/Space,
  Esc, `u`, `f`, `n`, `r`, `?`, `q`, `:`, `m`, `t`, `w`).
- New CLI flag: `--captured-sort time|rank` (default `time`) added to
  the existing arg parser in `clients/chess-tui/src/cli.rs`.
- Preserve the toggle across screen transitions via the existing
  `AppState::replace_preserving_prefs` path (same pattern as
  `--no-confetti` / `--no-check-banner`).

### chess-web rendering

New component `clients/chess-web/src/components/captured.rs`:

\```rust
#[component]
pub fn CapturedPanel(#[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>) -> impl IntoView { … }
\```

Renders two rows (Red / Black) with one `<span class="captured-piece">`
per piece. Glyph from `crate::glyph::glyph(...)`, consistent with the
board.

Sort toggle:
- `Prefs` (`clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`) gains
  `captured_sort: RwSignal<CapturedSort>` persisted to localStorage
  key `chess.ui.capturedSort` (existing keys are `chess.fx.confetti`,
  `chess.fx.checkBanner`).
- A small button in the panel header swaps between "⏱ Time" and
  "📊 Rank" labels (text-only, no extra icon library).

Wire from `Sidebar` in `components/sidebar.rs` (slot in after the
`legal_count` line, before `sidebar-actions`) AND from
`OnlineSidebar` in `pages/play.rs` (~L255–354). Both consume the same
component because the panel only depends on `view`.

CSS additions to `clients/chess-web/style.css` (alongside existing
`.sidebar` block at L295–325):

\```css
.captured-panel { display: grid; gap: 0.25rem; }
.captured-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; }
.captured-header h4 { margin: 0; font-size: 0.95rem; }
.captured-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.35rem; align-items: center; font-family: var(--cjk-font, inherit); }
.captured-row.red .captured-piece  { color: var(--red); }
.captured-row.black .captured-piece { color: var(--fg); }
.captured-piece { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 700; }
.captured-toggle { font-size: 0.8rem; padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem; }
\```

### Sort logic (shared semantics)

Both clients reuse the same ordering rules so behaviour is consistent:
- `Time`: as returned by `captured_pieces()`, grouped by side at
  render time but each side's row stays in capture order.
- `Rank`: stable-sort each side's slice by `PieceKind` rank
  (General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot > Horse > Cannon > Soldier).
  Use `chess_core::piece::PieceKind`'s implicit ordering if present;
  otherwise add `pub fn rank_value(kind: PieceKind) -> u8` next to
  the existing banqi rank helper in `crates/chess-core/src/piece.rs`
  (check first — banqi already needs piece ranks for capture rules,
  so this likely already exists as private).

The sort lives in each client's view layer (TUI rendering fn / web
component). The engine just ships the chronological list.

## Files to modify

- `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` — add `captured_pieces()`.
- `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs` — add `captured` field + projection.
- `crates/chess-core/src/piece.rs` — add `rank_value()` if not already
  exposed (banqi capture rules likely already have it; reuse, don't
  duplicate).
- `crates/chess-core/tests/captured_pieces.rs` — new test file.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/cli.rs` — `--captured-sort time|rank` flag.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` — `captured_sort` state + `g` key
  binding + preserve-across-screens.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` — render Captured block in both
  sidebar fns.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/captured.rs` — new component.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/mod.rs` — re-export.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` — slot in
  `<CapturedPanel>`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` — same in `OnlineSidebar`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs` — `captured_sort` signal +
  localStorage hydration.
- `clients/chess-web/style.css` — `.captured-panel` etc.

## Tests (`crates/chess-core/tests/captured_pieces.rs`)

- Fresh state: `view.captured.is_empty()`.
- Single `Move::Capture`: 1 entry with the right piece.
- `Move::ChainCapture` with 3 hops → 3 entries in path order.
- `Move::CannonJump` → 1 entry (xiangqi).
- DarkCapture outcomes (use a hand-written `.pos` fixture with a known
  attacker/defender pairing so we can force each outcome):
  - Capture outcome → 1 entry (revealed defender).
  - Trade outcome → 1 entry (attacker).
  - Probe outcome → 0 entries.
- Mixed sequence: ordering matches make-move chronology.
- After `unmake_move` of a capture, `captured_pieces().len()` decreases
  by the right amount (1 for `Capture`, N for `ChainCapture` with N
  hops, 0/1 for the three DarkCapture branches).

## Verification

\```bash
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test -p chess-core --test captured_pieces
cargo test --workspace
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core
\```

Manual TUI:
\```bash
cargo run -p chess-tui -- banqi --house chain,dark,rush,horse --seed 42
# Capture a few pieces. Confirm sidebar Captured panel updates.
# Press 'g' to toggle Time/Rank sort.
# Trigger a 連吃 chain via the chain_lock UX → all hops appear.
# Try a DarkCapture probe → no entry. Trade → attacker enters its row.
\```

Manual web:
\```bash
make play-web
# /local/banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse&seed=42
# Same checks; toggle button in sidebar swaps Time ↔ Rank.
# Refresh the page → toggle preference persists (localStorage).
\```

Net + spectator (captured list is public, all observers see the same):
\```bash
make play-spectator VARIANT=banqi
# Make captures on the player panes; spectator sees them too.
\```

```

 `./.claude/plans/ux-bug-vivid-meerkat.md`

```md
# Banqi: captured-pieces ("graveyard") panel in sidebar

## Context

During a banqi (暗棋) game — especially when 連吃 chain captures are
active — players can't tell which pieces have already been taken. A
single chain move can capture 2–4 pieces atomically and the only
record is the move log, which lists ICCS notation, not piece glyphs.

Add a "captured pieces" panel to both clients (chess-tui + chess-web),
showing per-side glyphs of every captured piece. Useful for xiangqi
too (material count at a glance), so we ship for both variants.

User asked for two sort orders: chronological (capture time) and by
rank (largest → smallest). Confirmed via AskUserQuestion: ship both
behind a toggle.

## Approach

Capture data already lives in `state.history` — every `Move` variant
that captures preserves the captured `Piece` for `unmake_move`. So we
add a thin engine helper that walks the history and emits a flat
`Vec<Piece>` in chronological order, expose it on `PlayerView`, and
render it in both clients with a sort toggle.

Wire shape: `PlayerView.captured: Vec<Piece>` with `#[serde(default)]`.
This is the same back-compat pattern already used for `in_check` (v4),
`chain_lock` + `current_color` (v5). No protocol bump needed — v5 just
shipped this session and tolerates field additions on `PlayerView` via
the existing serde-default convention. (If the user wants a clean
v5→v6 bump for record-keeping, that's a one-line change to whichever
const tracks the protocol number — surfaced if/when we find it.)

### Engine helper: `GameState::captured_pieces()`

`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` — add:

\```rust
/// All pieces captured so far, in chronological (history) order.
/// Used by `PlayerView` for sidebar rendering. Returns the captured
/// piece's full identity — safe under ADR-0004 because every captured
/// piece was revealed at capture time.
pub fn captured_pieces(&self) -> Vec<Piece> {
    let mut out = Vec::new();
    for record in &self.history {
        match &record.the_move {
            Move::Capture { captured, .. }
            | Move::CannonJump { captured, .. } => out.push(*captured),
            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => {
                out.extend(path.iter().map(|hop| hop.captured));
            }
            Move::DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {
                let (Some(rev), Some(att)) = (*revealed, *attacker) else { continue };
                match dark_capture_outcome(att, rev, self.rules.house) {
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => out.push(rev),
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade   => out.push(att),
                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe   => {}
                }
            }
            _ => {}
        }
    }
    out
}
\```

`dark_capture_outcome` and `DarkCaptureOutcome` are already private to
`state/mod.rs` (added in the prior session — see `state/mod.rs:563`),
so the helper sits in the same file with direct access. No new public
API beyond `captured_pieces` itself.

### View projection

`crates/chess-core/src/view.rs` — add field:

\```rust
/// Pieces captured so far (chronological). Always present; clients
/// sort/group as needed. Added in protocol v5.1; older clients see
/// the empty default via `serde(default)`.
#[serde(default)]
pub captured: Vec<Piece>,
\```

Populate in `PlayerView::project()`:

\```rust
captured: state.captured_pieces(),
\```

### chess-tui rendering (`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`)

Add a `Captured` block in both `draw_sidebar` (~L869–979) and
`draw_sidebar_net` (~L981–1145), inserted before the help / chat
region. Two-line content:

\```
┌ Captured ────────────────┐
│ R 紅: 兵 兵 馬           │  (red text)
│ B 黑: 卒 卒              │  (dim/dark text)
└──────────────────────────┘
\```

Glyphs come from existing `glyph::glyph(kind, side, style)` (TUI
glyph.rs:16). Style by side using `Style::default().fg(Color::Red)`
for red glyphs and the existing dim/black colour for black.
`Paragraph::wrap` handles overflow if a row gets long.

Sort toggle:
- New `AppState` field: `captured_sort: CapturedSort` (`{ Time, Rank }`).
- New keybinding: `g` (graveyard sort) — toggles `Time ↔ Rank`. `g`
  is currently unbound (existing keys: `hjkl`/arrows, Enter/Space,
  Esc, `u`, `f`, `n`, `r`, `?`, `q`, `:`, `m`, `t`, `w`).
- New CLI flag: `--captured-sort time|rank` (default `time`) added to
  the existing arg parser in `clients/chess-tui/src/cli.rs`.
- Preserve the toggle across screen transitions via the existing
  `AppState::replace_preserving_prefs` path (same pattern as
  `--no-confetti` / `--no-check-banner`).

### chess-web rendering

New component `clients/chess-web/src/components/captured.rs`:

\```rust
#[component]
pub fn CapturedPanel(#[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>) -> impl IntoView { … }
\```

Renders two rows (Red / Black) with one `<span class="captured-piece">`
per piece. Glyph from `crate::glyph::glyph(...)`, consistent with the
board.

Sort toggle:
- `Prefs` (`clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`) gains
  `captured_sort: RwSignal<CapturedSort>` persisted to localStorage
  key `chess.ui.capturedSort` (existing keys are `chess.fx.confetti`,
  `chess.fx.checkBanner`).
- A small button in the panel header swaps between "⏱ Time" and
  "📊 Rank" labels (text-only, no extra icon library).

Wire from `Sidebar` in `components/sidebar.rs` (slot in after the
`legal_count` line, before `sidebar-actions`) AND from
`OnlineSidebar` in `pages/play.rs` (~L255–354). Both consume the same
component because the panel only depends on `view`.

CSS additions to `clients/chess-web/style.css` (alongside existing
`.sidebar` block at L295–325):

\```css
.captured-panel { display: grid; gap: 0.25rem; }
.captured-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; }
.captured-header h4 { margin: 0; font-size: 0.95rem; }
.captured-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.35rem; align-items: center; font-family: var(--cjk-font, inherit); }
.captured-row.red .captured-piece  { color: var(--red); }
.captured-row.black .captured-piece { color: var(--fg); }
.captured-piece { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 700; }
.captured-toggle { font-size: 0.8rem; padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem; }
\```

### Sort logic (shared semantics)

Both clients reuse the same ordering rules so behaviour is consistent:
- `Time`: as returned by `captured_pieces()`, grouped by side at
  render time but each side's row stays in capture order.
- `Rank`: stable-sort each side's slice by `PieceKind` rank
  (General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot > Horse > Cannon > Soldier).
  Use `chess_core::piece::PieceKind`'s implicit ordering if present;
  otherwise add `pub fn rank_value(kind: PieceKind) -> u8` next to
  the existing banqi rank helper in `crates/chess-core/src/piece.rs`
  (check first — banqi already needs piece ranks for capture rules,
  so this likely already exists as private).

The sort lives in each client's view layer (TUI rendering fn / web
component). The engine just ships the chronological list.

## Files to modify

- `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` — add `captured_pieces()`.
- `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs` — add `captured` field + projection.
- `crates/chess-core/src/piece.rs` — add `rank_value()` if not already
  exposed (banqi capture rules likely already have it; reuse, don't
  duplicate).
- `crates/chess-core/tests/captured_pieces.rs` — new test file.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/cli.rs` — `--captured-sort time|rank` flag.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` — `captured_sort` state + `g` key
  binding + preserve-across-screens.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` — render Captured block in both
  sidebar fns.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/captured.rs` — new component.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/mod.rs` — re-export.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` — slot in
  `<CapturedPanel>`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` — same in `OnlineSidebar`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs` — `captured_sort` signal +
  localStorage hydration.
- `clients/chess-web/style.css` — `.captured-panel` etc.

## Tests (`crates/chess-core/tests/captured_pieces.rs`)

- Fresh state: `view.captured.is_empty()`.
- Single `Move::Capture`: 1 entry with the right piece.
- `Move::ChainCapture` with 3 hops → 3 entries in path order.
- `Move::CannonJump` → 1 entry (xiangqi).
- DarkCapture outcomes (use a hand-written `.pos` fixture with a known
  attacker/defender pairing so we can force each outcome):
  - Capture outcome → 1 entry (revealed defender).
  - Trade outcome → 1 entry (attacker).
  - Probe outcome → 0 entries.
- Mixed sequence: ordering matches make-move chronology.
- After `unmake_move` of a capture, `captured_pieces().len()` decreases
  by the right amount (1 for `Capture`, N for `ChainCapture` with N
  hops, 0/1 for the three DarkCapture branches).

## Verification

\```bash
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test -p chess-core --test captured_pieces
cargo test --workspace
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core
\```

Manual TUI:
\```bash
cargo run -p chess-tui -- banqi --house chain,dark,rush,horse --seed 42
# Capture a few pieces. Confirm sidebar Captured panel updates.
# Press 'g' to toggle Time/Rank sort.
# Trigger a 連吃 chain via the chain_lock UX → all hops appear.
# Try a DarkCapture probe → no entry. Trade → attacker enters its row.
\```

Manual web:
\```bash
make play-web
# /local/banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse&seed=42
# Same checks; toggle button in sidebar swaps Time ↔ Rank.
# Refresh the page → toggle preference persists (localStorage).
\```

Net + spectator (captured list is public, all observers see the same):
\```bash
make play-spectator VARIANT=banqi
# Make captures on the player panes; spectator sees them too.
\```

# Banqi: captured-pieces ("graveyard") panel in sidebar

## Context

During a banqi (暗棋) game — especially when 連吃 chain captures are
active — players can't tell which pieces have already been taken. A
single chain move can capture 2–4 pieces atomically and the only

User asked for two sort orders: chronological (capture time) and by

behind a toggle.

Capture data already lives in `state.history` — every `Move` variant

Wire shape: `PlayerView.captured: Vec<Piece>` with `#[serde(default)]`.

shipped this session and tolerates field additions on `PlayerView` via
the existing serde-default convention. (If the user wants a clean

const tracks the protocol number — surfaced if/when we find it.)

### Engine helper: `GameState::captured_pieces()`

`crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` — add:

        match &record.the_move {
            Move::Capture { captured, .. }
            | Move::CannonJump { captured, .. } => out.push(*captured),
            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => {
                out.extend(path.iter().map(|hop| hop.captured));
            }
            Move::DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {

13	use crate::board::Board;
14	use crate::error::CoreError;
15	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
16	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceOnSquare, Side};
17	use crate::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
18	
19	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
20	pub enum GameStatus {
21	    Ongoing,
22	    Won { winner: Side, reason: WinReason },
23	    Drawn { reason: DrawReason },
24	}
25	
26	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
27	pub enum WinReason {
28	    Checkmate,
29	    Stalemate,
30	    Resignation,
31	    OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
32	    Timeout,
33	    /// Casual xiangqi (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`): the loser's general was
34	    /// physically captured rather than ending in checkmate. In standard
35	    /// rules this state is unreachable because the legality filter rejects
36	    /// any move that would leave the general capturable.
37	    GeneralCaptured,
38	}
39	
40	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
41	pub enum DrawReason {
42	    NoProgress,
43	    Repetition,
44	    Agreed,
45	    InsufficientMaterial,
46	}
47	
48	/// Side ↔ piece-color assignment for banqi (set after the first flip).
49	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
50	pub struct SideAssignment {
51	    /// `mapping[side.0 as usize]` = the piece-color that seat controls.
52	    pub mapping: SmallVec<[Side; 3]>,
53	}
54	
55	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
56	pub struct GameState {
57	    pub rules: RuleSet,
58	    pub board: Board,
59	    pub side_to_move: Side,
60	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
61	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
62	    pub status: GameStatus,
63	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
64	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
65	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
66	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock. `Some(sq)` means the moving piece at
67	    /// `sq` just made a chain-eligible capture and may continue capturing
68	    /// — the turn does NOT auto-advance until the player issues
69	    /// `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` (or makes another chain-eligible
70	    /// capture which keeps the lock active). `#[serde(default)]` so
71	    /// pre-chain-mode snapshots load.
72	    #[serde(default)]
73	    pub chain_lock: Option<crate::coord::Square>,
74	}
75	
76	impl GameState {
77	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
78	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
79	    }
80	
81	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
82	    ///
83	    /// When `chain_lock` is set (banqi 連吃 mid-chain), the legal-move
84	    /// list is filtered to capture-only moves originating from the
85	    /// locked square, plus `Move::EndChain` as the explicit terminator.
86	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
87	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
88	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
89	        if let Some(lock) = self.chain_lock {
90	            out.retain(|m| match m {
91	                Move::Capture { from, .. }
92	                | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
93	                | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. }
94	                | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } => *from == lock,
95	                _ => false,
96	            });
97	            out.push(Move::EndChain { at: lock });
98	        }
99	        out
100	    }
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Move enum.
2	//!
3	//! Flat enum (not a trait) so serde, equality, and pattern matching work
4	//! without ceremony. The `Reveal` variant carries `Option<Piece>` because
5	//! the network ABI distinguishes pre-flip (`None` from client) from
6	//! post-flip (`Some` from authoritative server). See ADR-0004.
7	
8	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
9	use smallvec::SmallVec;
10	
11	use crate::coord::Square;
12	use crate::piece::Piece;
13	
14	/// One logical move from a player's perspective.
15	///
16	/// `ChainCapture` is a single move even though it encodes a sequence —
17	/// undo pops once, history records once, the network ships one message.
18	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
19	pub enum Move {
20	    /// Banqi: flip the face-down piece at `at`. The identity is `None`
21	    /// when the client requests the flip and `Some(_)` once the
22	    /// authoritative engine has resolved it.
23	    Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> },
24	
25	    /// Plain non-capturing slide.
26	    Step { from: Square, to: Square },
27	
28	    /// Capture (single hop). `captured` is recorded so undo can restore.
29	    Capture { from: Square, to: Square, captured: Piece },
30	
31	    /// Chain capture (banqi house rule 連吃 / 暗連). `path` is the sequence
32	    /// of hops, each of which is a capture. Last hop's `to` is the final
33	    /// landing square. Length ≥ 1.
34	    ChainCapture { from: Square, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> },
35	
36	    /// Cannon capture-by-jump. `screen` is the piece jumped over.
37	    CannonJump { from: Square, to: Square, screen: Square, captured: Piece },
38	
39	    /// Banqi house rule `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE): atomically reveal a
40	    /// face-down piece and resolve a capture against it.
41	    /// `revealed` and `attacker` are `None` on the wire (client → server)
42	    /// and `Some(_)` once the authoritative engine has filled them in.
43	    /// Outcome (capture / probe / trade) is decided at apply-time from
44	    /// banqi rank rules + `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` flag.
45	    DarkCapture { from: Square, to: Square, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> },
46	
47	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode terminator. After a chain-eligible capture,
48	    /// `state.chain_lock` is set and the turn does NOT auto-advance —
49	    /// the player keeps moving the same piece via further captures.
50	    /// `Move::EndChain { at }` is the explicit "I'm done" pass: it clears
51	    /// `chain_lock` and advances the turn. `at` is the locked piece's
52	    /// square (carried so undo can restore).
53	    EndChain { at: Square },
54	}
55	
56	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
57	pub struct ChainHop {
58	    pub to: Square,
59	    pub captured: Piece,
60	}
61	
62	/// Move-list type alias — sized to avoid heap allocation in typical positions.
63	pub type MoveList = SmallVec<[Move; 32]>;
64	
65	impl Move {
66	    /// Origin square of the move (where the moving piece started).
67	    /// `Reveal` returns the flip square. `EndChain` returns the locked
68	    /// piece's square (the piece doesn't move).
69	    #[inline]
70	    pub fn origin_square(&self) -> Square {
71	        match self {
72	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
73	            Move::EndChain { at } => *at,
74	            Move::Step { from, .. }
75	            | Move::Capture { from, .. }
76	            | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. }
77	            | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
78	            | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. } => *from,
79	        }
80	    }
81	
82	    /// Destination square (where the moving piece ended). `Reveal` and
83	    /// `EndChain` have none (the piece doesn't move).
84	    #[inline]
85	    pub fn to_square(&self) -> Option<Square> {
86	        match self {
87	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => None,
88	            Move::Step { to, .. }
89	            | Move::Capture { to, .. }
90	            | Move::CannonJump { to, .. }
91	            | Move::DarkCapture { to, .. } => Some(*to),
92	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to),
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	    /// Whether this move resets the no-progress counter (capture or reveal).
97	    /// `EndChain` is a turn pass — it does NOT reset no_progress.

```text
525	    ///
526	    /// Banqi remaps after the first flip locks `side_assignment`; xiangqi
527	    /// and three-kingdom always return the seat as-is. Move generation
528	    /// for banqi must filter pieces by this color, not by `side_to_move`
529	    /// — `side_to_move` is the seat / turn-order index, which only
530	    /// coincides with the piece-color before the first reveal.
531	    #[inline]
532	    pub fn current_color(&self) -> Side {
533	        match self.side_assignment.as_ref() {
534	            Some(sa) => {
535	                let idx = self.side_to_move.0 as usize;
536	                if idx < sa.mapping.len() {
537	                    sa.mapping[idx]
538	                } else {
539	                    self.side_to_move
540	                }
541	            }
542	            None => self.side_to_move,
543	        }
544	    }
545	}
546	
547	/// Outcome of a 暗吃 (`Move::DarkCapture`) once the target is revealed.
548	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
549	pub(crate) enum DarkCaptureOutcome {
550	    /// Rank check passes — attacker takes target's square (normal capture).
551	    Capture,
552	    /// Rank check fails AND `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` is set — attacker is
553	    /// removed; defender stays revealed at `to`.
554	    Trade,
555	    /// Rank check fails, no trade flag — both pieces stay; target is
556	    /// now revealed (information probe; turn advances normally).
557	    Probe,
558	}
559	
560	/// Decide the outcome of a dark-capture given the involved pieces and
561	/// the active house rules. Used by both apply and unapply (recomputed
562	/// rather than stored in the move record).
563	pub(crate) fn dark_capture_outcome(
564	    attacker: Piece,
565	    revealed: Piece,
566	    house: crate::rules::HouseRules,
567	) -> DarkCaptureOutcome {
568	    if attacker.side == revealed.side {
569	        // Same side → never a capture; treat as probe (own piece blocks).
570	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe;
571	    }
572	    if crate::rules::banqi::can_capture(attacker.kind, revealed.kind) {
573	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture
574	    } else if house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
575	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade
576	    } else {
577	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe
578	    }
579	}
580	
581	/// Whether `attacker` standing on `from` has at least one further legal
582	/// capture in any direction. Used to decide whether banqi 連吃 chain
583	/// mode should remain active after a capture (see
584	/// `compute_chain_lock_after`). Only consults banqi rules — caller
585	/// guarantees variant + CHAIN_CAPTURE flag.
586	fn has_capture_from(state: &GameState, from: crate::coord::Square, attacker: Piece) -> bool {
587	    use crate::coord::Direction;
588	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
589	    use crate::rules::{banqi::can_capture, HouseRules};
590	
591	    let house = state.rules.house;
592	    let board = &state.board;
593	
594	    // Cannon: only via jump-over-screen.
595	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
596	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
597	            let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
598	            let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
599	            let mut cursor = screen_sq;
600	            while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
601	                match board.get(next) {
602	                    None => cursor = next,
603	                    Some(pos) => {
604	                        if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
605	                            return true;
606	                        }
607	                        break;
608	                    }
609	                }
610	            }
611	        }
612	        return false;
613	    }
614
```

```text
1	//! Banqi `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE) move-gen + apply/unapply tests.
2	//! Covers both the probe variant (default) and the trade variant
3	//! (DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE).
4	
5	use chess_core::board::Board;
6	use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
7	use chess_core::moves::Move;
8	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
9	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
10	use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
11	use smallvec::smallvec;
12	
13	fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
14	    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
15	    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
16	    for sq in squares {
17	        state.board.set(sq, None);
18	    }
19	    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
20	    state
21	}
22	
23	fn place_revealed(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
24	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
25	}
26	
27	fn place_hidden(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
28	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(Piece::new(side, kind))));
29	}
30	
31	#[test]
32	fn dark_capture_emitted_when_flag_on_target_face_down() {
33	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
34	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
35	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
36	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
37	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
38	
39	    let dark: Vec<Move> =
40	        state.legal_moves().into_iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { .. })).collect();
41	    assert!(!dark.is_empty(), "expected at least one DarkCapture move");
42	    assert!(dark
43	        .iter()
44	        .any(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == target)));
45	}
46	
47	#[test]
48	fn no_dark_capture_when_flag_off() {
49	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
50	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
51	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
52	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
53	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
54	
55	    let dark =
56	        state.legal_moves().into_iter().filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { .. })).count();
57	    assert_eq!(dark, 0, "no DarkCapture moves without the flag");
58	}
59	
60	#[test]
61	fn dark_capture_succeeds_when_attacker_outranks() {
62	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
63	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
64	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
65	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
66	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
67	
68	    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: h, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
69	    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
70	    // Horse at target, h is empty.
71	    assert!(state.board.get(h).is_none(), "attacker moved");
72	    let landed = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
73	    assert!(landed.revealed);
74	    assert_eq!(landed.piece.kind, PieceKind::Horse);
75	    assert_eq!(landed.piece.side, Side::RED);
76	}
77	
78	#[test]
79	fn dark_capture_probe_keeps_attacker_when_outranked() {
80	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
81	    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
82	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
83	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
84	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);
85	
86	    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
87	    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
88	    // Probe: attacker stays put; target is now revealed.
89	    let attacker = state.board.get(s).unwrap();
90	    assert!(attacker.revealed);
91	    assert_eq!(attacker.piece.kind, PieceKind::Soldier);
92	    let revealed_target = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
93	    assert!(revealed_target.revealed, "target must be revealed after probe");
94	    assert_eq!(revealed_target.piece.kind, PieceKind::Elephant);
95	}
96	
97	#[test]
98	fn dark_capture_trade_kills_attacker_when_outranked() {
99	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
100	        HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
101	        0,
102	    ));
103	    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
104	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
105	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
106	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);
107	
108	    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
109	    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
110	    // Trade: attacker dies; target stays revealed in place.
111	    assert!(state.board.get(s).is_none(), "trade variant: attacker must be removed");
112	    let target_pos = state.board.get(target).unwrap();
113	    assert!(target_pos.revealed);
114	    assert_eq!(target_pos.piece.kind, PieceKind::Elephant);
115	    assert_eq!(target_pos.piece.side, Side::BLACK);
116	}
117	
118	#[test]
119	fn dark_capture_make_unmake_round_trip_capture_path() {
120	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
121	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
122	    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
123	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
124	    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
125	    let snapshot = state.clone();
126	
127	    let m = Move::DarkCapture { from: h, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None };
128	    state.make_move(&m).unwrap();
129	    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
130	    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
131	    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, snapshot.side_to_move);
132	}
133

183	}

```text
1	//! 連吃 chain mode (engine-state, not just atomic): after a chain-eligible
2	//! capture, `chain_lock` activates and the turn does NOT auto-advance until
3	//! the player either continues capturing in any direction or issues
4	//! `Move::EndChain`.
5	
6	use chess_core::board::Board;
7	use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
8	use chess_core::moves::Move;
9	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
10	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
11	use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
12	use smallvec::smallvec;
13	
14	fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
15	    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
16	    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
17	    for sq in squares {
18	        state.board.set(sq, None);
19	    }
20	    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
21	    state
22	}
23	
24	fn place(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
25	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
26	}
27	
28	#[test]
29	fn capture_into_chain_keeps_turn_with_same_player() {
30	    // Red horse at a1 captures a2 (black soldier), and from a2 can keep
31	    // capturing a3 (another black soldier) — chain mode activates.
32	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
33	    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
34	    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
35	    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
36	    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
37	    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
38	    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
39	
40	    let single = Move::Capture {
41	        from: h,
42	        to: s1,
43	        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
44	    };
45	    state.make_move(&single).unwrap();
46	
47	    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, Some(s1), "chain_lock activates on capturing piece's new square");
48	    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::RED, "turn does NOT advance — still RED");
49	    assert_eq!(state.current_color(), Side::RED);
50	}
51	
52	#[test]
53	fn chain_legal_moves_filter_to_captures_from_locked_square_plus_end_chain() {
54	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
55	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
56	    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
57	    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
58	    let s_side = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
59	    let other = state.board.sq(File(3), Rank(0));
60	    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
61	    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
62	    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
63	    place(&mut state.board, s_side, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
64	    place(&mut state.board, other, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
65	
66	    let cap = Move::Capture {
67	        from: h,
68	        to: s1,
69	        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
70	    };
71	    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
72	
73	    let legal = state.legal_moves();
74	    // Only moves originating at s1 (the chain-locked square) + EndChain.
75	    for m in legal.iter() {
76	        match m {
77	            Move::EndChain { at } => assert_eq!(*at, s1),
78	            other => assert_eq!(other.origin_square(), s1, "all locked legal moves must start at s1"),
79	        }
80	    }
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs`

```text
104	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
105	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
106	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
107	        }
108	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
109	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
110	        let chain_lock_before = self.chain_lock;
111	
112	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
113	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
114	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
115	
116	        self.history.push(MoveRecord {
117	            mover,
118	            the_move: normalized.clone(),
119	            no_progress_before,
120	            chain_lock_before,
121	        });
122	
123	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
124	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
125	        } else {
126	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
127	        }
128	
129	        // Decide whether the moving piece enters / continues chain mode.
130	        // Banqi-only, gated on CHAIN_CAPTURE flag, only after a real
131	        // capture that landed an attacker on a square where it can
132	        // capture again. EndChain explicitly clears the lock.
133	        self.chain_lock = self.compute_chain_lock_after(&normalized);
134	
135	        // Turn advances UNLESS we just entered / are continuing chain
136	        // mode (chain_lock now Some).
137	        if self.chain_lock.is_none() {
138	            self.turn_order.advance();
139	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
140	        }
141	
142	        Ok(())
143	    }
144	
145	    /// Undo the last move.
146	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
147	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
148	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
149	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
150	
151	        // If chain_lock is currently Some, the move did NOT advance the
152	        // turn (the player was either entering or continuing chain mode).
153	        // Otherwise the turn advanced and we need to rewind it.
154	        let turn_advanced = self.chain_lock.is_none();
155	
156	        // Restore the pre-move chain_lock.
157	        self.chain_lock = rec.chain_lock_before;
158	
159	        if turn_advanced {
160	            if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
161	                self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
162	            } else {
163	                self.turn_order.current -= 1;
164	            }
165	            self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
166	        }
167	        // Game status reset to Ongoing — caller may recompute.
168	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
169	        Ok(())
170	    }
171	
172	    /// After applying `m`, decide whether the moving piece is now in
173	    /// banqi 連吃 chain mode. Returns the locked piece's square when
174	    /// chain mode should remain / activate; `None` otherwise.
175	    ///
176	    /// Activates only for banqi + `CHAIN_CAPTURE` rules, after a capture
177	    /// move whose attacker actually landed on a square (probe and trade
178	    /// outcomes of `Move::DarkCapture` skip the lock — the attacker
```
</details></tool-use>
