<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```rs
//! `GameState::captured_pieces()` and `PlayerView::captured` cover the
//! sidebar "graveyard" panel. The list is computed from history every
//! time, so the same code path is responsible for `Capture`,
//! `CannonJump`, `ChainCapture` (atomic), and `DarkCapture` (probe /
//! trade / capture).

use chess_core::board::Board;
use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
use chess_core::moves::{ChainHop, Move};
use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
use smallvec::smallvec;

fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
    for sq in squares {
        state.board.set(sq, None);
    }
    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
    state
}

fn place_revealed(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

fn place_hidden(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

#[test]
fn fresh_state_has_no_captured_pieces() {
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
    assert!(state.captured_pieces().is_empty());
    assert!(PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED).captured.is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn fresh_xiangqi_has_no_captured_pieces() {
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
    assert!(state.captured_pieces().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn single_capture_records_the_dead_piece() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let captured = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    state.make_move(&Move::Capture { from: h, to: target, captured }).unwrap();

    assert_eq!(state.captured_pieces(), vec![captured]);
}

#[test]
fn chain_capture_records_each_hop_in_path_order() {
    // Atomic ChainCapture variant — three hops, each a different piece.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let from = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
    let h1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let h2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    let h3 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, from, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h3, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse);

    let path = smallvec![
        ChainHop { to: h1, captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier) },
        ChainHop { to: h2, captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon) },
        ChainHop { to: h3, captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse) },
    ];
    state.make_move(&Move::ChainCapture { from, path }).unwrap();

    let captured = state.captured_pieces();
    assert_eq!(captured.len(), 3);
    assert_eq!(captured[0].kind, PieceKind::Soldier);
    assert_eq!(captured[1].kind, PieceKind::Cannon);
    assert_eq!(captured[2].kind, PieceKind::Horse);
    assert!(captured.iter().all(|p| p.side == Side::BLACK));
}

#[test]
fn cannon_jump_records_the_captured_piece() {
    // Banqi 4×8 board is fine — Move::CannonJump apply just consults
    // squares, not the variant.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let cannon = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
    let screen = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, cannon, Side::RED, PieceKind::Cannon);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, screen, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse);

    let captured = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse);
    state
        .make_move(&Move::CannonJump { from: cannon, to: target, screen, captured })
        .unwrap();

    assert_eq!(state.captured_pieces(), vec![captured]);
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_with_capture_outcome_records_revealed_defender() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    state
        .make_move(&Move::DarkCapture { from: h, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None })
        .unwrap();

    let captured = state.captured_pieces();
    assert_eq!(captured.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(captured[0], Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier));
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_probe_records_no_captured_piece() {
    // Soldier attacks hidden Elephant → probe (no DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag).
    // Both pieces stay; nothing dies; graveyard stays empty.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);

    state
        .make_move(&Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None })
        .unwrap();

    assert!(state.captured_pieces().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn dark_capture_trade_records_dead_attacker() {
    // Same probe setup but with DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag → attacker dies.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
        HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
        0,
    ));
    let s = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, s, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_hidden(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Elephant);

    state
        .make_move(&Move::DarkCapture { from: s, to: target, revealed: None, attacker: None })
        .unwrap();

    let captured = state.captured_pieces();
    assert_eq!(captured.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(captured[0], Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier));
}

#[test]
fn order_is_chronological_across_mixed_move_types() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let r1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
    let r2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let r3 = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
    let b1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(0));
    let b2 = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(1));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, r1, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, r2, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, r3, Side::RED, PieceKind::Cannon);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, b1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, b2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse);

    // 1. Red horse takes Black soldier.
    let cap_b1 = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    state.make_move(&Move::Capture { from: r1, to: b1, captured: cap_b1 }).unwrap();
    // After Red moves the seat advances to BLACK.
    state.side_to_move = Side::BLACK;
    state.turn_order.current = 1;
    // 2. Black horse takes Red chariot.
    let cap_r2 = Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
    state.make_move(&Move::Capture { from: b2, to: r2, captured: cap_r2 }).unwrap();
    state.side_to_move = Side::RED;
    state.turn_order.current = 0;
    // 3. Red cannon-jumps Black horse (now at r2 from step 2) using r1's
    //    horse as screen.
    let cap_b2 = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse);
    state
        .make_move(&Move::CannonJump { from: r3, to: r2, screen: b1, captured: cap_b2 })
        .unwrap();

    let captured = state.captured_pieces();
    assert_eq!(captured, vec![cap_b1, cap_r2, cap_b2], "chronological order preserved");
}

#[test]
fn unmake_removes_only_the_last_capture() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let from = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(0));
    let h1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let h2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, from, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon);

    // Two-hop atomic chain — undoing once removes BOTH dead pieces.
    let path = smallvec![
        ChainHop { to: h1, captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier) },
        ChainHop { to: h2, captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon) },
    ];
    state.make_move(&Move::ChainCapture { from, path }).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.captured_pieces().len(), 2);
    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert!(state.captured_pieces().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn player_view_exposes_captured_field_with_same_data() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let captured = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    state.make_move(&Move::Capture { from: h, to: target, captured }).unwrap();

    // Same list for both observers — capture history is public.
    let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
    let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
    assert_eq!(red_view.captured, vec![captured]);
    assert_eq!(black_view.captured, vec![captured]);
}

#[test]
fn captured_field_serde_roundtrips() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let target = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place_revealed(&mut state.board, target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    let captured = Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    state.make_move(&Move::Capture { from: h, to: target, captured }).unwrap();

    let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
    let json = serde_json::to_string(&view).unwrap();
    let back: PlayerView = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(back.captured, view.captured);

    // Older clients without the `captured` field still deserialize cleanly.
    let mut value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    value.as_object_mut().unwrap().remove("captured");
    let trimmed = serde_json::to_string(&value).unwrap();
    let view_v5: PlayerView = serde_json::from_str(&trimmed).unwrap();
    assert!(view_v5.captured.is_empty(), "missing field defaults to empty Vec");
}
```

```text
202	pub enum Screen {
203	    Picker(PickerView),
204	    Game(Box<GameView>),
205	    Net(Box<NetView>),
206	    HostPrompt(HostPromptView),
207	    Lobby(Box<LobbyView>),

209	}

211	pub struct AppState {
212	    pub screen: Screen,
213	    pub style: Style,
214	    pub use_color: bool,
215	    pub observer: Side,
216	    pub help_open: bool,
217	    pub rules_open: bool,
218	    /// True while the y/N quit-confirm dialog is shown. Set when the user
219	    /// presses 'q' / Ctrl-C during an in-progress game (status `Ongoing` and
220	    /// at least one move played). Picker / game-over `q` skip the prompt.
221	    pub quit_confirm_open: bool,
222	    /// Rect of the board widget last drawn (terminal coords). Used for
223	    /// mouse-click hit-testing. ui.rs writes this each frame.
224	    pub board_rect: Option<RectPx>,
225	    pub should_quit: bool,
226	    /// User pref: render confetti + big banner on game end. Default true,
227	    /// disable via `--no-confetti`. Preserved across screen transitions.
228	    pub show_confetti: bool,
229	    /// User pref: render the "將軍 / CHECK" banner when the side-to-move's
230	    /// general is under attack. Default true, disable via `--no-check-banner`.
231	    /// Xiangqi-only — banqi never sets `in_check`.
232	    pub show_check_banner: bool,
233	    /// Active confetti burst. Spawned by `ui.rs` once the board area is
234	    /// known (so we can place particles in the board sub-rect); cleared
235	    /// when the burst expires.
236	    pub confetti_anim: Option<ConfettiAnim>,
237	    /// Set by `note_status_transition` when the game just ended and a
238	    /// confetti burst should fire on the next draw. `ui.rs` consumes the
239	    /// flag once the board rect is known.
240	    pub confetti_pending: bool,
241	    /// Last observed status of the local game (`Game` screen) — used to
242	    /// detect Ongoing → ended transitions for the confetti trigger.
243	    pub prev_status_local: Option<GameStatus>,
244	    /// Last observed status of the net game (`Net` screen) — same as
245	    /// `prev_status_local` but tracked separately because a Net rematch
246	    /// resets the status without dropping the screen.

248	}

255	    /// Width in terminal cols of one cell (glyph + padding).
256	    pub cell_cols: u16,
257	    /// Height in terminal rows of one cell (rank row + between row).

260	    pub left_pad: u16,
261	    /// Offset (rows) of the first row from rect.y.

266	    pub fn new_picker(style: Style, use_color: bool, observer: Side) -> Self {
267	        Self {
268	            screen: Screen::Picker(PickerView { cursor: 0 }),
269	            style,
270	            use_color,
271	            observer,
272	            help_open: false,
273	            rules_open: false,

281	    /// Default values for the FX-related fields, factored out so each
282	    /// constructor can `..Self::fx_defaults()` instead of repeating five
283	    /// lines of init. Both `show_*` flags default to true; `main.rs`
284	    /// flips them back off when the user passes `--no-*`.
285	    fn fx_defaults() -> Self {
286	        // Construct a placeholder; only the FX fields are read via the
287	        // `..` syntax. The other fields are immediately overridden by the
288	        // caller, so picking arbitrary defaults here is fine.
289	        Self {
290	            screen: Screen::Picker(PickerView { cursor: 0 }),
291	            style: Style::Cjk,
292	            use_color: true,
293	            observer: Side::RED,
294	            help_open: false,

299	            show_confetti: true,
300	            show_check_banner: true,
301	            confetti_anim: None,
302	            confetti_pending: false,
303	            prev_status_local: None,
304	            prev_status_net: None,
305	        }
306	    }

312	        let cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);

320	                        .into(),
321	                ),
322	                coord_input: None,
323	            })),
324	            style,
325	            use_color,
326	            observer,
327	            help_open: false,
328	            rules_open: false,
329	            quit_confirm_open: false,
330	            board_rect: None,
331	            should_quit: false,
332	            ..Self::fx_defaults()
333	        }
334	    }
335	
336	    pub fn new_net(url: String, style: Style, use_color: bool) -> Self {
337	        let client = NetClient::spawn(url.clone());
338	        Self {
339	            screen: Screen::Net(Box::new(NetView {
340	                client,
341	                url,
342	                last_view: None,
343	                rules: None,
344	                role: None,
345	                cursor: (0, 0),
346	                selected: None,
347	                last_msg: Some("Connecting…".into()),
348	                connected: false,
349	                chat: VecDeque::with_capacity(CHAT_HISTORY_CAP),
350	                chat_input: None,
351	                coord_input: None,
352	            })),
353	            style,
354	            use_color,
355	            // Pre-welcome, we render as Red until the server tells us our role.
356	            observer: Side::RED,
357	            help_open: false,
358	            rules_open: false,
359	            quit_confirm_open: false,
360	            board_rect: None,
361	            should_quit: false,
362	            ..Self::fx_defaults()
363	        }
364	    }
365	
366	    /// Skip the picker and land on the host-prompt screen so the user can
367	    /// type a server URL. Used by the `--lobby` flag with no host argument
368	    /// — currently main always passes a URL, so this is the safety net for
369	    /// future entrypoints (e.g. picker → "Connect to server…").
370	    pub fn new_host_prompt(style: Style, use_color: bool, observer: Side) -> Self {
371	        Self {
372	            screen: Screen::HostPrompt(HostPromptView { buf: "ws://".into(), error: None }),
373	            style,
374	            use_color,
375	            observer,
376	            help_open: false,
377	            rules_open: false,
378	            quit_confirm_open: false,
379	            board_rect: None,
380	            should_quit: false,
381	            ..Self::fx_defaults()
382	        }
383	    }
384	
385	    /// Open the lobby browser against `host` (e.g. `"ws://127.0.0.1:7878"`).
386	    pub fn new_lobby(host: String, style: Style, use_color: bool, observer: Side) -> Self {
387	        let client = NetClient::spawn(format!("{host}/lobby"));
388	        Self {
389	            screen: Screen::Lobby(Box::new(LobbyView {
390	                client,
391	                host,
392	                rooms: Vec::new(),
393	                cursor: 0,
394	                last_msg: Some("Connecting to lobby…".into()),
395	                connected: false,
396	                pending_join: None,
397	            })),

399	            use_color,
400	            observer,
401	            help_open: false,
402	            rules_open: false,
403	            quit_confirm_open: false,
404	            board_rect: None,
405	            should_quit: false,
406	            ..Self::fx_defaults()
407	        }
408	    }
409	
410	    /// Detect Ongoing → ended transitions and arm the confetti burst for the
411	    /// next draw. Called automatically at the end of `dispatch` (Local) and
412	    /// `tick_net` (Net); rematches that move from Won/Drawn → Ongoing are
413	    /// silent (we update the remembered status without firing).
414	    fn note_status_transition(&mut self, cur: Option<GameStatus>, is_net: bool) {
415	        let prev = if is_net { self.prev_status_net } else { self.prev_status_local };
416	        let was_ongoing = matches!(prev, Some(GameStatus::Ongoing));
417	        let now_ended =
418	            matches!(cur, Some(GameStatus::Won { .. }) | Some(GameStatus::Drawn { .. }));
419	        if was_ongoing && now_ended && self.show_confetti {
420	            self.confetti_pending = true;
421	        }
422	        if is_net {
423	            self.prev_status_net = cur;
424	        } else {
425	            self.prev_status_local = cur;
426	        }
427	    }
428	
429	    fn current_local_status(&self) -> Option<GameStatus> {
430	        match &self.screen {
431	            Screen::Game(g) => Some(g.state.status),
432	            _ => None,
433	        }
434	    }
435	
436	    fn current_net_status(&self) -> Option<GameStatus> {
437	        match &self.screen {
438	            Screen::Net(n) => n.last_view.as_ref().map(|v| v.status),
439	            _ => None,
440	        }
441	    }
442	
443	    /// Replace `self` with `fresh` while preserving the user's FX prefs
444	    /// (`show_confetti`, `show_check_banner`). Constructors always default
445	    /// FX to ON; this helper carries the user's actual choices across screen
446	    /// transitions like `NewGame` → picker, lobby → game, etc. Tracking
447	    /// state (`prev_status_*`, active `confetti_anim`) is reset because the
448	    /// new screen is starting fresh.
449	    fn replace_preserving_prefs(&mut self, fresh: AppState) {
450	        let confetti = self.show_confetti;
451	        let check = self.show_check_banner;
452	        *self = fresh;
453	        self.show_confetti = confetti;
454	        self.show_check_banner = check;
455	    }
456	
457	    /// Drain ws events from the worker thread(s) and apply them to the
458	    /// active `NetView` / `LobbyView`. Called once per main-loop tick
459	    /// (no-op outside Net / Lobby modes).
460	    pub fn tick_net(&mut self) {
461	        match &mut self.screen {
462	            Screen::Net(n) => {
463	                while let Ok(evt) = n.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
464	                    apply_net_event(n, evt);
465	                }
466	            }
467	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
468	                while let Ok(evt) = l.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
469	                    apply_lobby_event(l, evt);
```

```text
1	//! `chess-tui` — ratatui frontend for chess-core.
2	//!
3	//! Run modes:
4	//!   chess-tui                                       # variant picker
5	//!   chess-tui xiangqi
6	//!   chess-tui banqi --preset taiwan --seed 42
7	//!   chess-tui --style ascii xiangqi
8	//!   chess-tui --no-color xiangqi
9	//!   chess-tui --as black xiangqi                    # debug: render as Black
10	
11	mod app;
12	mod banner;
13	mod confetti;
14	mod glyph;
15	mod input;
16	mod net;
17	mod orient;
18	mod text_input;
19	mod ui;
20	mod url;
21	
22	use std::io::{self, Stdout};
23	use std::time::Duration;
24	
25	use anyhow::{anyhow, Context, Result};
26	use chess_core::piece::Side;
27	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
28	use clap::{Parser, Subcommand, ValueEnum};
29	use crossterm::event::{self, Event};
30	use crossterm::execute;
31	use crossterm::terminal::{
32	    disable_raw_mode, enable_raw_mode, EnterAlternateScreen, LeaveAlternateScreen,
33	};
34	use ratatui::backend::CrosstermBackend;
35	use ratatui::Terminal;
36	
37	use crate::app::AppState;
38	use crate::glyph::Style;
39	use crate::input::{from_key, from_mouse, Action};
40	use crate::url::{normalize_connect_url, normalize_lobby_url};
41	
42	#[derive(Parser, Debug)]
43	#[command(name = "chess-tui", about = "ratatui frontend for chess-core")]
44	struct Cli {
45	    /// Render style.
46	    #[arg(long, value_enum, default_value_t = StyleArg::Cjk)]
47	    style: StyleArg,
48	
49	    /// Disable ANSI color (keeps the chosen glyph set).
50	    #[arg(long)]
51	    no_color: bool,
52	
53	    /// Disable end-of-game confetti + big VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW overlay.
54	    /// Sidebar status text is unaffected.
55	    #[arg(long)]
56	    no_confetti: bool,
57	
58	    /// Disable the "將軍 / CHECK" sidebar warning. Xiangqi-only — banqi
59	    /// has no general so the banner never fires there anyway.
60	    #[arg(long)]
61	    no_check_banner: bool,
62	
63	    /// Render from this side's perspective (debug; default RED). Ignored
64	    /// when `--connect` is set — the server assigns a side on join.
65	    #[arg(long = "as", value_enum, default_value_t = SideArg::Red)]
66	    observer: SideArg,
67	
68	    /// Connect to a chess-net-server instead of starting a local game.
69	    /// Example: --connect ws://127.0.0.1:7878 (the trailing /ws is added
70	    /// automatically if missing). Use ws://host/ws/<room-id> to target a
71	    /// specific room on a multi-room server; pair with --password if the
72	    /// room is locked.
73	    #[arg(long)]
74	    connect: Option<String>,
75	
76	    /// Open the lobby browser for the given chess-net server. Example:
77	    /// --lobby ws://127.0.0.1:7878 — picks a room from the live list (or
78	    /// creates one) instead of joining a fixed URL. Mutually exclusive
79	    /// with --connect; if both are provided, --lobby wins.
80	    #[arg(long)]
81	    lobby: Option<String>,
82	
83	    /// Password for password-locked rooms when paired with --connect. The
84	    /// in-TUI lobby browser prompts for the password directly, so this
85	    /// flag is mostly for scripted reconnects.
86	    #[arg(long)]
87	    password: Option<String>,
88	
89	    #[command(subcommand)]
90	    cmd: Option<Cmd>,
91	}
92	
93	#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]
94	enum Cmd {
95	    /// Xiangqi (9×10). Casual rules by default — moves that leave your own
96	    /// general capturable are allowed; the game ends when the general is
97	    /// actually captured. Pass `--strict` for the standard self-check filter.
98	    Xiangqi {
99	        /// Strict mode: standard rules. Moves that would leave your own
100	        /// general in check are rejected.
101	        #[arg(long)]
102	        strict: bool,
103	    },
104	    /// Banqi (4×8 face-down).
105	    Banqi {
106	        /// Preset bundle of house rules.
107	        #[arg(long, value_enum)]
108	        preset: Option<PresetArg>,
109	        /// Comma-separated house rules: chain,dark,rush,horse-diagonal,cannon-fast.
110	        #[arg(long)]
111	        house: Option<String>,
112	        /// Deterministic shuffle seed.
113	        #[arg(long)]
114	        seed: Option<u64>,
115	    },
116	}
117	
118	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
119	enum StyleArg {
120	    Cjk,
121	    Ascii,
122	}
123	
124	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
125	enum SideArg {
126	    Red,
127	    Black,
128	}
129	
130	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
131	enum PresetArg {
132	    Purist,
133	    Taiwan,
134	    Aggressive,
135	}
136	
137	fn main() -> Result<()> {
138	    let cli = Cli::parse();
139	
140	    let style = match cli.style {
141	        StyleArg::Cjk => Style::Cjk,
142	        StyleArg::Ascii => Style::Ascii,
143	    };
144	    let observer = match cli.observer {
145	        SideArg::Red => Side::RED,
146	        SideArg::Black => Side::BLACK,
147	    };
148	    let use_color = !cli.no_color;
149	
150	    let show_confetti = !cli.no_confetti;
151	    let show_check_banner = !cli.no_check_banner;
152	
153	    let mut app = if let Some(host) = cli.lobby.as_deref() {
154	        let url = normalize_lobby_url(host).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
155	        // Strip the /lobby suffix before storing as `host` so the lobby
156	        // can re-derive `/ws/<id>` for joins.
157	        let host = url.trim_end_matches("/lobby").to_string();
158	        AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer)
159	    } else if let Some(url) = cli.connect.as_deref() {
160	        let mut url = normalize_connect_url(url).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
161	        if let Some(pw) = cli.password.as_deref() {
162	            let sep = if url.contains('?') { '&' } else { '?' };
163	            url = format!("{url}{sep}password={}", crate::url::urlencode(pw));
164	        }
165	        AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color)
166	    } else {
167	        match &cli.cmd {
168	            None => AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer),
169	            Some(Cmd::Xiangqi { strict }) => {
170	                let rules = if *strict { RuleSet::xiangqi() } else { RuleSet::xiangqi_casual() };
171	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
172	            }
173	            Some(Cmd::Banqi { preset, house, seed }) => {
174	                let rules = build_banqi_rules(preset.as_ref(), house.as_deref(), *seed)?;
175	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
176	            }
177	        }
178	    };
179	
180	    app.show_confetti = show_confetti;
181	    app.show_check_banner = show_check_banner;
182	    run(app)
183	}
184	
185	fn build_banqi_rules(
186	    preset: Option<&PresetArg>,
187	    house: Option<&str>,
188	    seed: Option<u64>,
189	) -> Result<RuleSet> {
190	    let mut flags = HouseRules::empty();
191	    if let Some(p) = preset {
192	        flags |= match p {
193	            PresetArg::Purist => chess_core::rules::PRESET_PURIST,
194	            PresetArg::Taiwan => chess_core::rules::PRESET_TAIWAN,
195	            PresetArg::Aggressive => chess_core::rules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE,
196	        };
197	    }
198	    if let Some(s) = house {
199	        for tok in s.split(',') {
200	            flags |= match tok.trim() {
201	                "chain" => HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE,
202	                "dark" | "dark-chain" | "dark-capture" => HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE,
203	                "dark-trade" | "trade" => HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
204	                "rush" | "chariot-rush" => HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
205	                "horse" | "horse-diagonal" | "diag" => HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL,
206	                "cannon-fast" | "fast-cannon" => HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE,
207	                other => return Err(anyhow!("unknown house rule: {other}")),
208	            };
209	        }
210	    }
211	    let flags = chess_core::rules::house::normalize(flags);
212	    Ok(match seed {
213	        Some(s) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(flags, s),
214	        None => RuleSet::banqi(flags),
215	    })
216	}
217	
218	fn run(mut app: AppState) -> Result<()> {
219	    let mut terminal = setup_terminal()?;
220	    let result = run_loop(&mut terminal, &mut app);
221	    teardown_terminal(&mut terminal).ok();
222	    result
223	}
224	
225	fn setup_terminal() -> Result<Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>> {
226	    enable_raw_mode().context("enable raw mode")?;
227	    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
228	    execute!(stdout, EnterAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture)
229	        .context("enter alt screen")?;
230	    let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Keyboard / mouse events → `Action`. Pure mapping; no state mutation.
2	
3	use crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers, MouseButton, MouseEvent, MouseEventKind};
4	
5	/// Where the user's focus is right now. Drives the keymap branch in
6	/// [`from_key`]. `Game` covers both local hot-seat (`Screen::Game`) and
7	/// online (`Screen::Net`) play.
8	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
9	pub enum InputMode {
10	    Picker,
11	    Lobby,
12	    Text,
13	    Game,
14	}
15	
16	/// Flavor of the coordinate-input prompt opened from `Game` mode.
17	/// `Instant` = pure text buffer, no board feedback.
18	/// `Live` = each keystroke re-parses; valid square prefixes update the
19	/// `selected` highlight, and a complete `from+to` move also moves the
20	/// cursor onto the destination square as a preview.
21	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
22	pub enum CoordKind {
23	    Instant,
24	    Live,
25	}
26	
27	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
28	pub enum Action {
29	    None,
30	    Quit,
31	    /// Confirm an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed y/Y).
32	    ConfirmYes,
33	    /// Cancel an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed anything else).
34	    ConfirmNo,
35	    HelpToggle,
36	    RulesToggle,
37	    NewGame,
38	    /// "Back one screen" — Esc semantics outside game/picker. The
39	    /// dispatcher decides what to back into per screen.
40	    Back,
41	    // Picker / Lobby (list-cursor)
42	    PickerUp,
43	    PickerDown,
44	    PickerSelect,
45	    // Lobby
46	    LobbyCreate,
47	    LobbyRefresh,
48	    /// Spectate the highlighted room (`?role=spectator`).
49	    LobbyWatch,
50	    // Text input (HostPrompt / CreateRoom)
51	    TextInput(char),
52	    TextBackspace,
53	    FocusNext,
54	    FocusPrev,
55	    /// Submit a text-input form (Enter while in `Text` mode).
56	    Submit,
57	    // Game
58	    CursorUp,
59	    CursorDown,
60	    CursorLeft,
61	    CursorRight,
62	    SelectOrCommit,
63	    Cancel,
64	    Undo,
65	    Flip,
66	    /// Open the chat input editor (Net mode, players only).
67	    ChatStart,
68	    /// Open the coordinate-input prompt (Game / Net, players only). The
69	    /// kind picks instant-vs-live preview behaviour.
70	    CoordStart(CoordKind),
71	    /// Mouse click in terminal coords. The app resolves it against the board
72	    /// rect captured by the most recent draw().
73	    Click {
74	        term_col: u16,
75	        term_row: u16,
76	    },
77	}
78	
79	pub fn from_key(ev: KeyEvent, mode: InputMode, quit_confirm_open: bool) -> Action {
80	    // Quit-confirm dialog hijacks input: y/Y confirms, anything else cancels.
81	    if quit_confirm_open {
82	        return match ev.code {
83	            KeyCode::Char('y') | KeyCode::Char('Y') => Action::ConfirmYes,
84	            _ => Action::ConfirmNo,
85	        };
86	    }
87	    if ev.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && matches!(ev.code, KeyCode::Char('c')) {
88	        return Action::Quit;
89	    }
90	    match mode {
91	        InputMode::Picker => match ev.code {
92	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
93	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
94	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
95	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
96	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
97	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Quit,
98	            _ => Action::None,
99	        },
100	        InputMode::Lobby => match ev.code {
101	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
102	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
103	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
104	            KeyCode::Char('c') => Action::LobbyCreate,
105	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::LobbyRefresh,
106	            KeyCode::Char('w') => Action::LobbyWatch,
107	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
108	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
109	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
110	            _ => Action::None,
111	        },
112	        InputMode::Text => match ev.code {
113	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
114	            KeyCode::Enter => Action::Submit,
115	            KeyCode::Backspace => Action::TextBackspace,
116	            KeyCode::Tab => Action::FocusNext,
117	            KeyCode::BackTab => Action::FocusPrev,
118	            KeyCode::Char(c) => Action::TextInput(c),
119	            _ => Action::None,
120	        },
121	        InputMode::Game => match ev.code {
122	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
123	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
124	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::RulesToggle,
125	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::CursorUp,
126	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::CursorDown,
127	            KeyCode::Left | KeyCode::Char('h') => Action::CursorLeft,
128	            KeyCode::Right | KeyCode::Char('l') => Action::CursorRight,
129	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::SelectOrCommit,
130	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Cancel,
131	            KeyCode::Char('u') => Action::Undo,
132	            KeyCode::Char('f') => Action::Flip,
133	            KeyCode::Char('n') => Action::NewGame,
134	            KeyCode::Char('t') => Action::ChatStart,
135	            KeyCode::Char(':') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant),
136	            KeyCode::Char('m') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live),
137	            _ => Action::None,
138	        },
139	    }
140	}
141	
142	pub fn from_mouse(ev: MouseEvent) -> Action {
143	    match ev.kind {
144	        MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => {
145	            Action::Click { term_col: ev.column, term_row: ev.row }
146	        }
147	        _ => Action::None,
148	    }
149	}
150
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! App state + dispatch. The AppState holds a `GameState`, UI cursor, and a
2	//! short-lived flash message; actions from `input.rs` mutate it.
3	
4	use std::collections::VecDeque;
5	
6	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
7	use chess_core::coord::Square;
8	use chess_core::moves::Move;
9	use chess_core::piece::Side;

14	
15	const CHAT_HISTORY_CAP: usize = 50;
16	const CHAT_INPUT_MAX: usize = 256;
17	const COORD_INPUT_MAX: usize = 16;
18	
19	use crate::confetti::ConfettiAnim;
20	use crate::glyph::Style;

23	use crate::orient;

25	use crate::url::{normalize_host_url, urlencode, valid_room_id};
26	
27	/// Variant + preset choices in the picker, in display order.
28	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
29	pub enum PickerEntry {
30	    Xiangqi,
31	    XiangqiStrict,
32	    BanqiPurist,
33	    BanqiTaiwan,
34	    BanqiAggressive,
35	    /// Open the host prompt → lobby browser → online play flow.
36	    ConnectToServer,
37	    Quit,
38	}
39	
40	impl PickerEntry {
41	    pub const ALL: [PickerEntry; 7] = [
42	        PickerEntry::Xiangqi,
43	        PickerEntry::XiangqiStrict,
44	        PickerEntry::BanqiPurist,
45	        PickerEntry::BanqiTaiwan,
46	        PickerEntry::BanqiAggressive,
47	        PickerEntry::ConnectToServer,
48	        PickerEntry::Quit,
49	    ];
50	
51	    pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
52	        match self {
53	            PickerEntry::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi (象棋)",
54	            PickerEntry::XiangqiStrict => "Xiangqi (象棋, strict — must defend check)",
55	            PickerEntry::BanqiPurist => "Banqi (暗棋) — purist",
56	            PickerEntry::BanqiTaiwan => "Banqi (暗棋) — Taiwan house rules",
57	            PickerEntry::BanqiAggressive => "Banqi (暗棋) — aggressive house rules",
58	            PickerEntry::ConnectToServer => "Connect to server… (online)",
59	            PickerEntry::Quit => "Quit",
60	        }
```

```text
869	fn draw_sidebar(
870	    frame: &mut Frame,
871	    area: Rect,
872	    g: &GameView,
873	    view: &PlayerView,
874	    style: Style,
875	    help_open: bool,
876	    show_check_banner: bool,
877	) {
878	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Status ");
879	    let inner = block.inner(area);
880	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
881	
882	    let mut lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::new();
883	
884	    // Game-over banner (takes priority).
885	    if let Some(banner) = game_over_banner(&view.status, style) {
886	        for l in banner {
887	            lines.push(l);
888	        }
889	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
890	    }
891	
892	    let variant_label = match view.shape {
893	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => {
894	            // Casual is the TUI default; strict is the alternative.
895	            if g.state.rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
896	                "Xiangqi (象棋)"
897	            } else {
898	                "Xiangqi (象棋, strict)"
899	            }
900	        }
901	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => "Banqi (暗棋)",
902	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => "三國暗棋",
903	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => "Custom",
904	    };
905	    lines.push(line_label_value("Variant:", variant_label));
906	
907	    // Side-to-move only makes sense while the game is ongoing; once it's
908	    // Won/Drawn the banner above already shows the winner. Display the
909	    // piece-colour (`current_color`), which diverges from `side_to_move`
910	    // (the seat) after a banqi first-flip.
911	    if matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
912	        let stm_color = side_color(view.current_color);
913	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
914	            Span::styled("Side to move:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
915	            Span::raw(" "),
916	            Span::styled(
917	                glyph::side_name(view.current_color, style),
918	                TuiStyle::default().fg(stm_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
919	            ),
920	        ]));
921	
922	        if show_check_banner
923	            && matches!(view.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10)
924	            && g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)
925	        {
926	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
927	                "  ⚠ CHECK 將軍 — your general is under attack",
928	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Red).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
929	            )));
930	        }
931	    }
932	
933	    let (status_text, status_color) = format_status_short(view.status, style);
934	    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
935	        Span::styled("Status:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
936	        Span::raw(" "),
937	        Span::styled(status_text, TuiStyle::default().fg(status_color)),
938	    ]));
939	    lines.push(line_label_value("Legal moves:", &view.legal_moves.len().to_string()));
940	    lines.push(line_label_value("History:", &g.state.history.len().to_string()));
941	
942	    let selected_label = match g.selected {
943	        Some(sq) => chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_square(&g.state.board, sq),
944	        None => "—".into(),
945	    };
946	    lines.push(line_label_value("Selected:", &selected_label));
947	
948	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
949	    if let Some(msg) = &g.last_msg {
950	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(msg.clone(), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow))));
951	    }
952	
953	    if help_open {
954	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
955	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
956	            "Help",
957	            TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
958	        )));
959	        for ln in HELP_LINES {
960	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::raw(*ln)));
961	        }
962	    } else {
963	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
964	        if let Some(ci) = &g.coord_input {
965	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![
966	                Span::styled("> ", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
967	                Span::styled(format!("{}_", ci.buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
968	            ]));
969	        } else {
970	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
971	                "?=help, r=rules, : / m=coord, n=new, q=quit",
972	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
973	            )));
974	        }
975	    }
976	
977	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
978	    frame.render_widget(para, inner);
979	}
```

```text
981	fn draw_sidebar_net(
982	    frame: &mut Frame,
983	    area: Rect,
984	    n: &NetView,
985	    view: &PlayerView,
986	    style: Style,
987	    help_open: bool,
988	    show_check_banner: bool,
989	) {
990	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Status (net) ");
991	    let inner = block.inner(area);
992	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
993	
994	    // Split the sidebar into "meta info" (top) and "chat region" (bottom).
995	    // The chat region keeps a scrolling log + a single input row when the
996	    // user is composing. Meta height is dynamic (grows to fit) and chat
997	    // gets the rest.
998	    let chat_input_active = n.chat_input.is_some();
999	    let chat_h = inner.height.saturating_sub(MIN_META_ROWS).max(MIN_CHAT_ROWS);
1000	    let meta_h = inner.height.saturating_sub(chat_h);
1001	    let chunks = Layout::default()
1002	        .direction(Direction::Vertical)
1003	        .constraints([Constraint::Length(meta_h), Constraint::Length(chat_h)])
1004	        .split(inner);
1005	
1006	    let mut lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::new();
1007	
1008	    if let Some(banner) = game_over_banner(&view.status, style) {
1009	        for l in banner {
1010	            lines.push(l);
1011	        }
1012	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
1013	    }
1014	
1015	    let variant_label = match (view.shape, n.rules.as_ref()) {
1016	        (BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10, Some(r)) => {
1017	            if r.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
1018	                "Xiangqi (象棋)"
1019	            } else {
1020	                "Xiangqi (象棋, strict)"
1021	            }
1022	        }
1023	        (BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10, None) => "Xiangqi (象棋)",
1024	        (BoardShape::Banqi4x8, _) => "Banqi (暗棋)",
1025	        (BoardShape::ThreeKingdom, _) => "三國暗棋",
1026	        (BoardShape::Custom { .. }, _) => "Custom",
1027	    };
1028	    lines.push(line_label_value("Variant:", variant_label));
1029	
1030	    match n.role {
1031	        Some(NetRole::Player(side)) => {
1032	            let c = side_color(side);
1033	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1034	                Span::styled("You:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
1035	                Span::raw(" "),
1036	                Span::styled(
1037	                    glyph::side_name(side, style),
1038	                    TuiStyle::default().fg(c).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1039	                ),
1040	            ]));
1041	        }
1042	        Some(NetRole::Spectator) => {
1043	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1044	                Span::styled("You:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
1045	                Span::raw(" "),
1046	                Span::styled(
1047	                    "Spectator (read-only)",
1048	                    TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1049	                ),
1050	            ]));
1051	        }
1052	        None => {}
1053	    }
1054	
1055	    if matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
1056	        // "Your turn?" depends on the observer's SEAT (side_to_move) but
1057	        // the displayed colour is the piece-colour the seat plays
1058	        // (`current_color`). For banqi the two diverge after the first
1059	        // flip; for xiangqi they're identical.
1060	        let stm_color = side_color(view.current_color);
1061	        let observer_side = n.role.and_then(|r| match r {
1062	            NetRole::Player(s) => Some(s),
1063	            NetRole::Spectator => None,
1064	        });
1065	        let label = match observer_side {
1066	            Some(obs) if obs == view.side_to_move => "Your turn:",
1067	            Some(_) => "Opponent:",
1068	            None => "To move:",
1069	        };
1070	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1071	            Span::styled(label, TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
1072	            Span::raw(" "),
1073	            Span::styled(
1074	                glyph::side_name(view.current_color, style),
1075	                TuiStyle::default().fg(stm_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1076	            ),
1077	        ]));
1078	
1079	        // Check banner: `view.in_check` is set by the server in v4+ from the
1080	        // observer's POV (or Red's POV for spectators). So Players read it
1081	        // as "my own general"; Spectators read it as "Red's general".
1082	        if show_check_banner && matches!(view.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) && view.in_check {
1083	            let msg = match n.role {
1084	                Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => "  ⚠ CHECK 將軍 — your general is under attack",
1085	                Some(NetRole::Spectator) => "  ⚠ CHECK 將軍 — Red's general is under attack",
1086	                None => "  ⚠ CHECK 將軍",
1087	            };
1088	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1089	                msg,
1090	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Red).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1091	            )));
1092	        }
1093	    }
1094	
1095	    let (status_text, status_color) = format_status_short(view.status, style);
1096	    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1097	        Span::styled("Status:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
1098	        Span::raw(" "),
1099	        Span::styled(status_text, TuiStyle::default().fg(status_color)),
1100	    ]));
1101	    lines.push(line_label_value("Legal moves:", &view.legal_moves.len().to_string()));
1102	
1103	    let selected_label = match n.selected {
1104	        Some(sq) => format!("sq {}", sq.0),
1105	        None => "—".into(),
1106	    };
1107	    lines.push(line_label_value("Selected:", &selected_label));
1108	
1109	    lines.push(line_label_value("Server:", &n.url));
1110	    lines.push(line_label_value("Connection:", if n.connected { "live" } else { "disconnected" }));
1111	
1112	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
1113	    if let Some(msg) = &n.last_msg {
1114	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(msg.clone(), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow))));
1115	    }
1116	
1117	    if help_open {
1118	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
1119	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1120	            "Help",
1121	            TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1122	        )));
1123	        for ln in HELP_LINES_NET {
1124	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::raw(*ln)));
1125	        }
1126	    } else {
1127	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
1128	        if let Some(ci) = &n.coord_input {
1129	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1130	                Span::styled("> ", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1131	                Span::styled(format!("{}_", ci.buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1132	            ]));
1133	        } else {
1134	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1135	                "?=help, r=rules, t=chat, : / m=coord, q=quit",
1136	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
1137	            )));
1138	        }
1139	    }
1140	
1141	    let meta_para = Paragraph::new(lines).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
1142	    frame.render_widget(meta_para, chunks[0]);
1143	
1144	    draw_chat_pane(frame, chunks[1], n, chat_input_active);
1145	}
1146	
1147	const MIN_META_ROWS: u16 = 14;
1148	const MIN_CHAT_ROWS: u16 = 6;
```

```text
85	}
86	
87	fn draw_game(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
88	    let chunks = Layout::default()
89	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
90	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
91	        .split(area);
92	
93	    let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen else {
94	        return;
95	    };
96	    let g_ref: &GameView = g.as_ref();
97	    let observer = app.observer;
98	    let style = app.style;
99	    let use_color = app.use_color;
100	    let help_open = app.help_open;
101	    let show_check_banner = app.show_check_banner;
102	
103	    let view = PlayerView::project(&g_ref.state, g_ref.state.side_to_move);
104	    let mut rect = app.board_rect;
105	    draw_board(
106	        frame,
107	        chunks[0],
108	        observer,
109	        style,
110	        use_color,
111	        &view,
112	        g_ref.cursor,
113	        g_ref.selected,
114	        &mut rect,
115	    );
116	    app.board_rect = rect;
117	    draw_sidebar(frame, chunks[1], g_ref, &view, style, help_open, show_check_banner);
118	
119	    // Confetti + endgame banner. The board area is `chunks[0]`. We do this
120	    // after the sidebar render so the layer paints on top of the board only,
121	    // and after we've taken the `&app.screen` immutable borrow above.
122	    let board_area = chunks[0];
123	    let endgame_kind = endgame_kind_local(&view.status, observer);
124	    render_confetti_and_banner(frame, board_area, app, endgame_kind, style, use_color);
125	}
126	
127	fn draw_net(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
128	    let chunks = Layout::default()
129	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
130	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
131	        .split(area);
132	
133	    let Screen::Net(n) = &app.screen else {
134	        return;
135	    };
136	    let n_ref: &NetView = n.as_ref();
137	    let style = app.style;
138	    let use_color = app.use_color;
139	    let help_open = app.help_open;
140	    let show_check_banner = app.show_check_banner;
141	    let observer = n_ref.role.map(|r| r.observer()).unwrap_or(app.observer);
142	
143	    match n_ref.last_view.as_ref() {
144	        Some(view) => {
145	            let view_status = view.status;
146	            let role = n_ref.role;
147	            let mut rect = app.board_rect;
148	            draw_board(
149	                frame,
150	                chunks[0],
151	                observer,
152	                style,
153	                use_color,
154	                view,
155	                n_ref.cursor,
156	                n_ref.selected,
157	                &mut rect,
158	            );
159	            app.board_rect = rect;
160	            draw_sidebar_net(frame, chunks[1], n_ref, view, style, help_open, show_check_banner);
161	
162	            let board_area = chunks[0];
163	            let endgame_kind = endgame_kind_net(&view_status, role);
164	            render_confetti_and_banner(frame, board_area, app, endgame_kind, style, use_color);
165	        }
166	        None => {
167	            draw_connecting_placeholder(frame, chunks[0], n_ref);
168	            draw_sidebar_net_idle(frame, chunks[1], n_ref);
169	        }
170	    }
171	}
172	
173	fn draw_connecting_placeholder(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, n: &NetView) {
174	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Net ");
175	    let lines = vec![
176	        Line::from(""),
177	        Line::from(Span::styled("  Connecting…", TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD))),
178	        Line::from(Span::styled(format!("  → {}", n.url), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Gray))),
179	        Line::from(""),
180	        Line::from(Span::styled(
181	            n.last_msg.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "  (waiting for server hello)".into()),
182	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow),
183	        )),
184	    ];
185	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
186	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
187	}
188	
189	fn draw_sidebar_net_idle(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, n: &NetView) {
190	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Status ");
191	    let lines = vec![
192	        Line::from(Span::styled("Connecting…", TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD))),
193	        Line::from(format!("URL: {}", n.url)),
194	        Line::from(""),
195	        Line::from(Span::styled("Press q to give up.", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray))),
196	    ];
197	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
198	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
199	}
200	
201	fn draw_host_prompt(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, h: &HostPromptView, help_open: bool) {
202	    let title =
203	        Span::styled("Connect to chess-net", TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD));
204	    let mut lines = vec![
205	        Line::from(title),
206	        Line::from(""),
207	        Line::from(Span::raw("Server URL (e.g. ws://127.0.0.1:7878):")),
208	        Line::from(vec![
209	            Span::raw("  "),
210	            Span::styled(format!("{}_", h.buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
211	        ]),
212	        Line::from(""),
213	    ];
214	    if let Some(err) = h.error.as_deref() {
215	        lines
216	            .push(Line::from(Span::styled(format!("× {err}"), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Red))));
217	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
218	    }
219	    if help_open {
220	        for ln in HELP_LINES_HOST_PROMPT {
221	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(*ln, TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray))));
222	        }
223	    } else {
224	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
225	            "Enter to connect, Esc to go back, ? for help.",
226	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
227	        )));
228	    }
229	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Online ");
230	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
231	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
232	}
233	
234	fn draw_lobby(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, l: &LobbyView, help_open: bool) {
235	    // Two columns: room list (left) + status/help (right). When the user is
236	    // mid-password-prompt, overlay a small modal.
237	    let chunks = Layout::default()
238	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
239	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
240	        .split(area);
241	
242	    let header_style = TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray);
243	    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::with_capacity(l.rooms.len() + 4);
244	    lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
245	        format!("Lobby — {}", l.host),
246	        TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
247	    )));
248	    lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
249	        format!("{:<20} {:<14} {:<6} {:<6} {:<8}", "Room", "Variant", "Seats", "Watch", "Status"),
250	        header_style,
251	    )));
252	    if l.rooms.is_empty() {
253	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
254	            "  (no rooms yet — press 'c' to create one)",
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`

```text
1	//! User-facing FX preferences, persisted in `localStorage`.
2	//!
3	//! Two switches, both default ON:
4	//! * `fx_confetti` — show end-of-game confetti + VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW overlay.
5	//! * `fx_check_banner` — show the "將軍 / CHECK" badge in the sidebar when
6	//!   the side-to-move is in check (xiangqi only).
7	//!
8	//! Storage keys are stable (`chess.fx.confetti`, `chess.fx.checkBanner`)
9	//! so a user toggling once carries the choice across sessions. Missing
10	//! values read as `true`. We persist `"1"` / `"0"` rather than booleans
11	//! so future debugging via DevTools is obvious.
12	//!
13	//! All `web_sys` calls are wrapped in `Option`/`Result` chains so a
14	//! sandboxed context with no `window` (or storage disabled) silently
15	//! falls back to in-memory defaults — no panics.
16	//!
17	//! This module is wasm32-only; the workspace native check skips it.
18	
19	use leptos::*;
20	
21	const KEY_CONFETTI: &str = "chess.fx.confetti";
22	const KEY_CHECK_BANNER: &str = "chess.fx.checkBanner";
23	
24	#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
25	pub struct Prefs {
26	    pub fx_confetti: RwSignal<bool>,
27	    pub fx_check_banner: RwSignal<bool>,
28	}
29	
30	impl Prefs {
31	    /// Hydrate from `localStorage` and arm `create_effect` watchers that
32	    /// persist any future change. Designed to be called once at app
33	    /// boot and `provide_context`-shared with all routes.
34	    pub fn hydrate() -> Self {
35	        let fx_confetti = create_rw_signal(read_bool(KEY_CONFETTI, true));
36	        let fx_check_banner = create_rw_signal(read_bool(KEY_CHECK_BANNER, true));
37	
38	        // Persist on change. The closures only run inside the browser, so
39	        // calling `localStorage` on every flip is safe and cheap.
40	        create_effect(move |_| {
41	            let v = fx_confetti.get();
42	            write_bool(KEY_CONFETTI, v);
43	        });
44	        create_effect(move |_| {
45	            let v = fx_check_banner.get();
46	            write_bool(KEY_CHECK_BANNER, v);
47	        });
48	
49	        Self { fx_confetti, fx_check_banner }
50	    }
51	}
52	
53	fn local_storage() -> Option<web_sys::Storage> {
54	    web_sys::window().and_then(|w| w.local_storage().ok().flatten())
55	}
56	
57	fn read_bool(key: &str, default_value: bool) -> bool {
58	    let Some(storage) = local_storage() else { return default_value };
59	    match storage.get_item(key) {
60	        Ok(Some(s)) => s != "0",
61	        _ => default_value,
62	    }
63	}
64	
65	fn write_bool(key: &str, value: bool) {
66	    if let Some(storage) = local_storage() {
67	        let _ = storage.set_item(key, if value { "1" } else { "0" });
68	    }
69	}
70
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! `chess-web` — Leptos + WASM frontend for chess-core.
2	//!
3	//! Native (`cargo check --workspace`) compiles only the pure-logic modules
4	//! (orient, glyph, routes, state); the leptos UI and the WS layer are
5	//! wasm32-gated so the workspace target check stays clean. Build for the
6	//! browser with `trunk serve` (or `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
7	//! -p chess-web`).
8	
9	pub mod glyph;
10	pub mod orient;
11	pub mod routes;
12	pub mod state;
13	
14	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
15	mod app;
16	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
17	mod components;
18	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
19	mod config;
20	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
21	mod pages;
22	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
23	mod prefs;
24	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
25	mod ws;
26	
27	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
28	use wasm_bindgen::prelude::wasm_bindgen;
29	
30	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
31	#[wasm_bindgen(start)]
32	pub fn start() {
33	    console_error_panic_hook::set_once();
34	    leptos::mount_to_body(app::App);
35	}
```

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable —
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::moves::Move;
6	use chess_core::piece::Side;
7	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
8	
9	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
10	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
11	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
12	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
13	pub enum ClientRole {
14	    Player(Side),
15	    Spectator,
16	}
17	
18	impl ClientRole {
19	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
20	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
24	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
25	    }
26	
27	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
28	        // Spectators render from RED's POV — matches what chess-net's
29	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
30	        match self {
31	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
32	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
33	        }
34	    }
35	}
36	
37	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
38	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
39	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
40	    if buf.len() > max {
41	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
42	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
47	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
48	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
49	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
50	}
51	
52	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
53	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
54	        return false;
55	    }
56	    match mv.to_square() {
57	        Some(t) => t == to,
58	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
59	    }
60	}
61	
62	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render dots).
63	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
64	    view.legal_moves
65	        .iter()
66	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
67	        .map(|m| match m.to_square() {
68	            Some(t) => t,
69	            None => match m {
70	                Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
71	                _ => unreachable!("Move::to_square() returned None for a non-Reveal move"),
72	            },
73	        })
74	        .collect()
75	}
76	
77	/// True if `view.chain_lock` is set and the click on `sq` should commit
78	/// `Move::EndChain` (e.g. user clicks the locked piece itself to release
79	/// the chain). Otherwise the click should attempt a further capture.
80	pub fn end_chain_move(view: &PlayerView) -> Option<Move> {
81	    view.chain_lock.map(|at| Move::EndChain { at })
82	}
83	
84	#[cfg(test)]
85	mod tests {
86	    use super::*;
87	    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
88	    use chess_core::state::GameState;
89	
90	    #[test]
91	    fn find_move_locates_xiangqi_step() {
92	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
93	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
94	        // Red soldier at file 0 rank 3 should have a step forward to rank 4.
95	        let from = Square(3 * 9); // rank 3, file 0
96	        let to = Square(4 * 9); // rank 4, file 0
97	        let mv = find_move(&view, from, to);
98	        assert!(matches!(mv, Some(Move::Step { .. })), "expected step from soldier, got {:?}", mv);
99	    }
100	
101	    #[test]
102	    fn legal_targets_for_chariot_in_corner() {
103	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
104	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
105	        // Red chariot at (file 0, rank 0) — Square(0). Should have several
106	        // legal destinations along its file (rank moves blocked by horse).
107	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(0));
108	        assert!(!targets.is_empty(), "chariot at corner must have legal moves");
109	    }
110	
111	    #[test]
112	    fn legal_targets_empty_for_non_piece_square() {
113	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
114	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
115	        // Square(5*9 + 4) — a river-ish empty square. Empty piece → no targets.
116	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(5 * 9 + 4));
117	        assert!(targets.is_empty());
118	    }
119	
120	    #[test]
121	    fn truncate_front_drops_oldest_when_over_cap() {
122	        let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
123	        truncate_front(&mut v, 3);
124	        assert_eq!(v, vec![3, 4, 5]);
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn truncate_front_noop_when_under_cap() {
129	        let mut v = vec![1, 2];
130	        truncate_front(&mut v, 5);
131	        assert_eq!(v, vec![1, 2]);
132	    }
133	
134	    #[test]
135	    fn client_role_observer_defaults_red_for_spectator() {
136	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK).observer(), Side::BLACK);
137	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Spectator.observer(), Side::RED);
138	        assert!(ClientRole::Spectator.is_spectator());
139	        assert!(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED).is_player());
140	    }
141	
142	    #[test]
143	    fn end_chain_move_returns_some_when_chain_lock_set() {
144	        use chess_core::board::Board;
145	        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
146	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare};
147	        use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
148	        use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
149	        use smallvec::smallvec;
150	
151	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
152	        let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
153	        for sq in squares {
154	            state.board.set(sq, None);
155	        }
156	        state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
157	        let _ = Board::new(state.board.shape()); // sanity
158	
159	        let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
160	        let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
161	        let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
162	        state.board.set(h, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse))));
163	        state
164	            .board
165	            .set(s1, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
166	        state
167	            .board
168	            .set(s2, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
169	
170	        let cap = Move::Capture {
171	            from: h,
172	            to: s1,
173	            captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
174	        };
175	        state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
176	        assert!(state.chain_lock.is_some());
177	
178	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
179	        assert_eq!(view.chain_lock, Some(s1));
180	        assert!(matches!(end_chain_move(&view), Some(Move::EndChain { at }) if at == s1));
181	    }
182	
183	    #[test]
184	    fn end_chain_move_returns_none_when_no_chain_lock() {
185	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
186	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
187	        assert!(end_chain_move(&view).is_none());
188	    }
189	}
190
```

```rs
//! Sidebar "captured pieces" panel ("graveyard").
//!
//! Two rows — Red dead / Black dead — rendered as glyph spans coloured
//! by side. The header includes a tiny toggle that flips the active
//! sort order between chronological (default) and rank. The signal
//! lives on `Prefs` so the choice persists in `localStorage`.

use chess_core::piece::Piece;
use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
use leptos::*;

use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
use crate::prefs::Prefs;
use crate::state::{split_and_sort_captured, CapturedSort};

#[component]
pub fn CapturedPanel(#[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>) -> impl IntoView {
    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
    let sort_signal = prefs.captured_sort;
    let style = Style::Cjk;

    let red_pieces = move || {
        let v = view.get();
        let (red, _black) = split_and_sort_captured(&v.captured, sort_signal.get());
        red
    };
    let black_pieces = move || {
        let v = view.get();
        let (_red, black) = split_and_sort_captured(&v.captured, sort_signal.get());
        black
    };

    let toggle_label = move || match sort_signal.get() {
        CapturedSort::Time => "⏱ time",
        CapturedSort::Rank => "📊 rank",
    };
    let on_toggle = move |_| sort_signal.update(|s| *s = s.toggled());

    view! {
        <section class="captured-panel">
            <div class="captured-header">
                <h4>"Captured 死亡"</h4>
                <button class="captured-toggle btn" on:click=on_toggle title="Toggle sort: time / rank">
                    {toggle_label}
                </button>
            </div>
            <CapturedRow side_class="red"   label="R 紅" pieces=Signal::derive(red_pieces)   style=style/>
            <CapturedRow side_class="black" label="B 黑" pieces=Signal::derive(black_pieces) style=style/>
        </section>
    }
}

#[component]
fn CapturedRow(
    side_class: &'static str,
    label: &'static str,
    #[prop(into)] pieces: Signal<Vec<Piece>>,
    style: Style,
) -> impl IntoView {
    let row_class = format!("captured-row {side_class}");
    let glyphs = move || {
        let ps = pieces.get();
        if ps.is_empty() {
            view! { <span class="captured-empty">"—"</span> }.into_view()
        } else {
            ps.into_iter()
                .map(|p| {
                    let g = glyph::glyph(p.kind, p.side, style).to_string();
                    view! { <span class="captured-piece">{g}</span> }
                })
                .collect_view()
        }
    };
    view! {
        <div class=row_class>
            <span class="captured-label">{label}</span>
            {glyphs}
        </div>
    }
}

```

```text
255	fn OnlineSidebar(
256	    room: String,
257	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
258	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
259	    rules: ReadSignal<Option<RuleSet>>,
260	    conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>,
261	    handle: WsHandle,
262	) -> impl IntoView {
263	    let role_label = move || match role.get() {
264	        Some(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED)) => "You play Red 紅".to_string(),
265	        Some(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK)) => "You play Black 黑".to_string(),
266	        Some(ClientRole::Player(_)) => "You play Green 綠".to_string(),
267	        Some(ClientRole::Spectator) => "Spectator — read-only".to_string(),
268	        None => "Awaiting seat assignment".to_string(),
269	    };
270	    let role_class = move || match role.get() {
271	        Some(ClientRole::Spectator) => "spectator-badge",
272	        _ => "",
273	    };
274	
275	    let variant_label = move || match rules.get() {
276	        Some(r) => variant_slug(r.variant).to_string(),
277	        None => "—".to_string(),
278	    };
279	
280	    let turn_label = move || match view_signal.get() {
281	        Some(v) => match v.current_color {
282	            Side::RED => "Red 紅 to move",
283	            Side::BLACK => "Black 黑 to move",
284	            _ => "Green 綠 to move",
285	        }
286	        .to_string(),
287	        None => "—".to_string(),
288	    };
289	
290	    let game_over = move || {
291	        matches!(
292	            view_signal.get().map(|v| v.status),
293	            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. })
294	        )
295	    };
296	    let is_player = move || role.get().map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false);
297	    let resign_disabled = move || !is_player() || game_over();
298	    let rematch_disabled = move || !is_player() || !game_over();
299	
300	    let conn_label = move || match conn.get() {
301	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting…",
302	        ConnState::Open => "Connected",
303	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected",
304	        ConnState::Error => "Error",
305	    };
306	
307	    let resign = {
308	        let handle = handle.clone();
309	        move |_| {
310	            handle.send(ClientMsg::Resign);
311	        }
312	    };
313	    let rematch = {
314	        let handle = handle.clone();
315	        move |_| {
316	            handle.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
317	        }
318	    };
319	
320	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
321	    let fx_confetti = prefs.fx_confetti;
322	    let fx_check_banner = prefs.fx_check_banner;
323	    let show_check = move || {
324	        let Some(v) = view_signal.get() else { return false };
325	        fx_check_banner.get()
326	            && matches!(v.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10)
327	            && v.in_check
328	            && matches!(v.status, GameStatus::Ongoing)
329	    };
330	
331	    view! {
332	        <aside class="sidebar">
333	            <h3 class="variant-label">{format!("Online — room {}", room)}</h3>
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 11:27:52Z)**_

All done. Final summary:

**Engine (chess-core):**
- New `GameState::captured_pieces()` helper in `state/mod.rs` — walks history and emits `Vec<Piece>` covering `Capture`, `ChainCapture`, `CannonJump`, and the three `DarkCapture` outcomes.
- New `PlayerView.captured: Vec<Piece>` field with `#[serde(default)]` (back-compat with v5 clients — the field is additive on top of v5).
- `crates/chess-core/tests/captured_pieces.rs` — 12 new tests.

**chess-tui:**
- `CapturedSort` enum, `--captured-sort time|rank` CLI flag, `g` key toggles in-game.
- New `Captured` block rendered in both Local and Net sidebars (`R 紅:` / `B 黑:` rows of glyphs coloured by side, header echoes the active sort label).
- `MIN_META_ROWS` bumped 14→17 so the Net sidebar leaves room for the panel above the chat pane.
- Sort preserved across screen transitions via `replace_preserving_prefs`.

**chess-web:**
- `CapturedSort` + `split_and_sort_captured` + `piece_rank_value` added to the pure-logic `state.rs` (4 native tests).
- `Prefs::captured_sort` persisted in `localStorage` under `chess.ui.capturedSort`.
- New `<CapturedPanel>` component used in both `<Sidebar>` (local) and `<OnlineSidebar>` (net + spectator), with a small toggle button in its header.
- CSS additions: `.captured-panel`, `.captured-row.red/.black`, `.captured-piece`, `.captured-toggle`.

**Verified:**
- `cargo fmt --check` clean
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- Workspace tests all pass (chess-core: 12 new captured_pieces tests; chess-web state: 4 new tests)
- `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core` clean
- chess-web wasm build clean

---

```text
62	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
63	
64	    // The chain-locked piece (if any) renders with the same affordance as

66	    let chain_locked_signal: Signal<Option<Square>> =
67	        Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.chain_lock));
68	
69	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
70	        // In chain mode the engine dictates which piece moves; surface
71	        // chain_lock as the selected square so the legal-target dots
72	        // render around it.
73	        chain_locked_signal.get().or_else(|| selected.get())
74	    });
75	
76	    let on_end_chain: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
77	        if wip {
78	            return;
79	        }
80	        let v = view.get();
81	        if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
82	            state.update(|s| {
83	                s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
84	                s.refresh_status();
85	            });
86	            selected.set(None);
87	        }
88	    });
89	
90	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
91	        if wip {
92	            return;
93	        }
94	        let v = view.get();
95	
96	        // Chain mode is engine-driven: when chain_lock is set, only the
97	        // locked piece can move (captures only). Clicking the locked
98	        // piece itself is the explicit "end chain" gesture.
99	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
100	            if sq == locked {
101	                if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
102	                    state.update(|s| {
103	                        s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
104	                        s.refresh_status();
105	                    });
106	                }
107	                selected.set(None);
108	                return;
109	            }
110	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
111	                state.update(|s| {
112	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal chain capture");
113	                    s.refresh_status();
114	                });
115	                selected.set(None);
116	                return;
117	            }
118	            // Click somewhere else during chain mode: ignore (the user
119	            // must end the chain or capture). Selection is irrelevant.
120	            return;
121	        }
122	
123	        let cur = selected.get();
124	
125	        if cur == Some(sq) {
126	            selected.set(None);
127	            return;
128	        }
129	
130	        if let Some(from) = cur {
131	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
132	                state.update(|s| {
133	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal move from view");
134	                    s.refresh_status();
135	                });
136	                selected.set(None);
137	                return;
138	            }
139	        }
140	
141	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
142	        // Selectable iff the engine emits a legal move from this square — this
143	        // works regardless of `side_assignment` remapping (banqi after the
144	        // first flip), since `legal_moves` is already filtered by piece-color.
145	        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))
146	            && v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
147	        if is_selectable_revealed {
148	            selected.set(Some(sq));
149	        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
150	            // Banqi flip — engine emits `Reveal { at }` whose origin and

153	                state.update(|s| {
154	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal reveal");

156	                });
157	                selected.set(None);
158	            }
159	        } else {
160	            selected.set(None);
161	        }
162	    });

165	        if wip {
166	            return;
167	        }

169	            let _ = s.unmake_move();
170	            s.refresh_status();
171	        });
172	        selected.set(None);
173	    });

178	            state.set(GameState::new(initial_rules.clone()));
179	            selected.set(None);
```
