```text
20	
21	#[component]
22	pub fn Board(
23	    shape: BoardShape,
24	    observer: Side,
25	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
26	    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
27	    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
28	) -> impl IntoView {
29	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
30	    let view_w = (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
31	    let view_h = (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
32	    let viewbox = format!("0 0 {} {}", view_w, view_h);
33	    let style = Style::Cjk;
34	
35	    view! {
36	        <svg class="board" viewBox=viewbox preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
37	            <rect class="board-bg" x="0" y="0" width="100%" height="100%"/>
38	            <g class="grid-layer">{grid_lines(shape, rows, cols)}</g>
39	            <g class="river-layer">{river_text(shape, cols)}</g>
40	            <g class="palace-layer">{palace_diagonals(shape)}</g>
41	            <g class="overlay-layer">
42	                {move || chain_lock_marker(view.get().chain_lock, observer, shape)}
43	                {move || selection_marker(selected.get(), observer, shape)}
44	                {move || move_dots_view(&view.get(), selected.get(), observer, shape)}
45	            </g>
46	            <g class="pieces-layer">
47	                {move || pieces_view(&view.get(), observer, shape, style)}
48	            </g>
49	            <g class="cells-layer">
50	                {hit_cells(rows, cols, observer, shape, on_click)}
51	            </g>
52	        </svg>
53	    }
54	}
55	
56	#[inline]
57	fn intersection(row: u8, col: u8) -> (i32, i32) {
58	    (PAD + col as i32 * CELL, PAD + row as i32 * CELL)
59	}
60	
61	fn grid_lines(shape: BoardShape, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> View {
62	    let is_xiangqi = matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
63	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize + cols as usize * 2);
64	    for r in 0..rows {
65	        let y = PAD + r as i32 * CELL;
66	        let x1 = PAD;
67	        let x2 = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL;
68	        out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x1 y1=y x2=x2 y2=y/> }.into_view());
69	    }
70	    for c in 0..cols {
71	        let x = PAD + c as i32 * CELL;
72	        let is_border = c == 0 || c == cols - 1;
73	        let y_top = PAD;
74	        let y_bot = PAD + (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL;
75	        if is_xiangqi && !is_border {
76	            let y_river_top = PAD + RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL;
77	            let y_river_bot = PAD + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL;
78	            out.push(
79	                view! {
80	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_river_top/>
81	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_river_bot x2=x y2=y_bot/>
82	                }
83	                .into_view(),
84	            );
85	        } else {
86	            out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_bot/> }.into_view());
87	        }
88	    }
89	    out.into_view()
90	}
91	
92	fn river_text(shape: BoardShape, cols: u8) -> View {
93	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
94	        return ().into_view();
95	    }
96	    let cx = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL / 2;
97	    let cy = PAD + (RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL) / 2;
98	    view! {
99	        <text class="river-text" x=cx y=cy text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
100	            "楚 河 漢 界"
101	        </text>
102	    }
103	    .into_view()
104	}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
181	    fn compute_chain_lock_after(&self, m: &Move) -> Option<crate::coord::Square> {
182	        if self.rules.variant != Variant::Banqi {
183	            return None;
184	        }
185	        if !self.rules.house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
186	            return None;
187	        }
188	        // Where did the moving piece end up? Only chain-eligible when
189	        // the attacker actually moved to a new square.
190	        let landing = match m {
191	            Move::Capture { to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { to, .. } => *to,
192	            Move::DarkCapture { to, revealed, attacker, .. } => {
193	                // `Move::DarkCapture` resolves to one of three outcomes
194	                // at apply-time. Only `Capture` (rank ok, attacker took
195	                // defender's square) puts the attacker at `to`; `Probe`
196	                // leaves the attacker at `from` and `Trade` removes the
197	                // attacker entirely. Neither of those is chain-eligible.
198	                let attacker_piece = (*attacker)?;
199	                let revealed_piece = (*revealed)?;
200	                match dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house) {
201	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => *to,
202	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe | DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => return None,
203	                }
204	            }
205	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to)?,
206	            // Reveal / Step / EndChain don't trigger chain mode.
207	            Move::Reveal { .. } | Move::Step { .. } | Move::EndChain { .. } => return None,
208	        };
209	        let landing_pos = self.board.get(landing)?;
210	        if !landing_pos.revealed {
211	            return None;
212	        }
213	        let active_color = self.current_color();
214	        if landing_pos.piece.side != active_color {
215	            // Defensive: shouldn't happen now that DarkCapture branches
216	            // explicitly above, but stays correct if future variants add
217	            // new capture-with-reveal moves.
218	            return None;
219	        }
220	        if has_capture_from(self, landing, landing_pos.piece) {
221	            Some(landing)
222	        } else {
223	            None
224	        }
225	    }
226	
227	    fn normalize_for_apply(&self, m: &Move) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
228	        match m {
229	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None } => {
230	                let pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
231	                if pos.revealed {
232	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
233	                }
234	                Ok(Move::Reveal { at: *at, revealed: Some(pos.piece) })
235	            }
236	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
237	                let attacker = match attacker {
238	                    Some(p) => *p,
239	                    None => {
240	                        let pos = self
241	                            .board
242	                            .get(*from)
243	                            .ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
244	                        if !pos.revealed {
245	                            return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: from not revealed"));
246	                        }
247	                        pos.piece
248	                    }
249	                };
250	                let revealed = match revealed {
251	                    Some(p) => *p,
252	                    None => {
253	                        let pos = self
254	                            .board
255	                            .get(*to)
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/src/orient.rs`

```text
1	//! Display orientation. Engine coords are physical (Red on rank 0); the
2	//! renderer rotates / transposes per shape so each observer sees their own
3	//! pieces at the bottom of the screen, and banqi displays in traditional
4	//! 8×4 layout.
5	//!
6	//! Verbatim copy of `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs` — `backlog/promote-client-shared.md`
7	//! tracks promoting both copies into a shared crate once a third client appears
8	//! or the copies first diverge. CI catches drift because the round-trip tests
9	//! are duplicated here too.
10	
11	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
12	use chess_core::coord::Square;
13	use chess_core::piece::Side;
14	
15	/// Dimensions of the display grid in (rows, cols). Banqi transposes; xiangqi
16	/// keeps model dimensions as-is.
17	pub fn display_dims(shape: BoardShape) -> (u8, u8) {
18	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
19	    match shape {
20	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (w, h), // 4 rows × 8 cols
21	        _ => (h, w),                    // 10 rows × 9 cols for xiangqi
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	/// Square (model coord) → (display_row, display_col).
26	#[allow(dead_code)]
27	pub fn project_cell(sq: Square, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> (u8, u8) {
28	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
29	    let f = (sq.0 as u8) % w;
30	    let r = (sq.0 as u8) / w;
31	    match shape {
32	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => match observer {
33	            Side::RED => (h - 1 - r, f),   // rank 9 at top, rank 0 at bottom
34	            Side::BLACK => (r, w - 1 - f), // rank 0 at top, files reversed
35	            _ => (h - 1 - r, f),           // 三國暗棋 fallback
36	        },
37	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (f, r), // transpose: rank → col, file → row
38	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => (h - 1 - r, f),
39	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => (h - 1 - r, f),
40	    }
41	}
42	
43	/// Display (row, col) → Square. Returns `None` when out of bounds or the
44	/// shape masks that cell off (Custom / ThreeKingdom).
45	pub fn square_at_display(row: u8, col: u8, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> Option<Square> {
46	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
47	    let (dr, dc) = display_dims(shape);
48	    if row >= dr || col >= dc {
49	        return None;
50	    }
51	    let (f, r) = match shape {
52	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => match observer {
53	            Side::RED => (col, h - 1 - row),
54	            Side::BLACK => (w - 1 - col, row),
55	            _ => (col, h - 1 - row),
56	        },
57	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (row, col),
58	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => (col, h - 1 - row),
59	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => (col, h - 1 - row),
60	    };
61	    if f >= w || r >= h {
62	        return None;
63	    }
64	    let idx = (r as u16) * (w as u16) + (f as u16);
65	    if !shape.is_playable(idx as usize) {
66	        return None;
67	    }
68	    Some(Square(idx))
69	}
70	
71	#[cfg(test)]
72	mod tests {
73	    use super::*;
74	
75	    fn all_squares(shape: BoardShape) -> impl Iterator<Item = Square> {
76	        let n = shape.cell_count() as u16;
77	        (0..n).map(Square).filter(move |s| shape.is_playable(s.0 as usize))
78	    }
79	
80	    #[test]
81	    fn xiangqi_red_round_trip() {
82	        let shape = BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10;
83	        for sq in all_squares(shape) {
84	            let (r, c) = project_cell(sq, Side::RED, shape);
85	            assert_eq!(square_at_display(r, c, Side::RED, shape), Some(sq));
86	        }
87	    }
88	
89	    #[test]
90	    fn xiangqi_black_round_trip() {
91	        let shape = BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10;
92	        for sq in all_squares(shape) {
93	            let (r, c) = project_cell(sq, Side::BLACK, shape);
94	            assert_eq!(square_at_display(r, c, Side::BLACK, shape), Some(sq));
95	        }
96	    }
97	
98	    #[test]
99	    fn xiangqi_red_and_black_are_180_rotations() {
100	        let shape = BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10;
101	        let (dr, dc) = display_dims(shape);
102	        for sq in all_squares(shape) {
103	            let (rr, rc) = project_cell(sq, Side::RED, shape);
104	            let (br, bc) = project_cell(sq, Side::BLACK, shape);
105	            assert_eq!(rr + br, dr - 1);
106	            assert_eq!(rc + bc, dc - 1);
107	        }
108	    }
109	
110	    #[test]
111	    fn xiangqi_red_corner_layout() {
112	        let shape = BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10;
113	        let sq = Square(0);
114	        let (r, c) = project_cell(sq, Side::RED, shape);
115	        assert_eq!((r, c), (9, 0));
116	
117	        let sq = Square(9 * 9);
118	        let (r, c) = project_cell(sq, Side::RED, shape);
119	        assert_eq!((r, c), (0, 0));
120	    }
121	
122	    #[test]
123	    fn banqi_round_trip_and_dims() {
124	        let shape = BoardShape::Banqi4x8;
125	        assert_eq!(display_dims(shape), (4, 8));
126	        for sq in all_squares(shape) {
127	            let (r, c) = project_cell(sq, Side::RED, shape);
128	            assert!(r < 4 && c < 8, "banqi cell out of display: ({}, {})", r, c);
129	            assert_eq!(square_at_display(r, c, Side::RED, shape), Some(sq));
130	        }
131	    }
132	
133	    #[test]
134	    fn out_of_bounds_returns_none() {
135	        let shape = BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10;
136	        assert!(square_at_display(10, 0, Side::RED, shape).is_none());
137	        assert!(square_at_display(0, 9, Side::RED, shape).is_none());
138	    }
139	}
140
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 11:42:27Z)**_

I think I see the residual-marks issue: the selection ring renders **under** the pieces in SVG order (overlay-layer comes before pieces-layer in board.rs:41-48). When you select a piece, its tile-disc covers most of the orange ring, so visually you only see the green dots — they look orphaned. Quick to fix.

Plan:
1. **Residual marks**: split the overlay so the selection ring (and chain-lock ring) render **above** pieces.
2. **Cemetery layout**: move the web captured panel from the sidebar to a horizontal strip directly below the board, keeping the time/rank toggle.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 11:43:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Where should the web captured-pieces panel live?**

Options:
- **Below board only (recommended)** - Two horizontal strips directly under the board: 黑 dead on top (Red's trophies) and 紅 dead on bottom (Black's trophies). Remove the sidebar version. Cleanest, matches your suggestion.
- **Below board + keep sidebar version** - Keep both. Sidebar version stays compact for narrow viewports; below-board strip is the prettier display.
- **Above + below board (mirrored)** - Put each side's dead pieces near the OPPOSING player's edge (chess.com convention) — Red dead above (Black's side), Black dead below (Red's side). Truly symmetric to board orientation.


**Answer:** Below board only (recommended)
</details></tool-use>

```text
85	    // The overlay needs a non-`Option` view signal once the welcome lands.
86	    // Wrap in a `Show` so we don't mount it pre-welcome (and so the watcher
87	    // inside doesn't see a fake "transition" on first connect).
88	    let view_for_overlay = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get());
89	    let role_for_overlay: Signal<Option<ClientRole>> = role.into();
90	
91	    view! {
92	        <section class="game-page">
93	            <div class="board-pane">
94	                <Show
95	                    when=move || view_signal.get().is_some() && role.get().is_some()
96	                    fallback=move || view! { <ConnPlaceholder room=room() conn=conn/> }
97	                >
98	                    <BoardWrapper
99	                        view_signal=view_signal
100	                        role=role
101	                        handle=handle_for_board.clone()
102	                    />
103	                    <EndOverlay
104	                        view=Signal::derive(move || view_for_overlay.get().expect("guarded by Show"))
105	                        role=role_for_overlay
106	                        enabled=fx_confetti
107	                    />
108	                </Show>
109	                <Show when=move || toast.get().is_some()>
110	                    <div class="toast" on:click=move |_| set_toast.set(None)>
111	                        {move || toast.get().unwrap_or_default()}
112	                    </div>
113	                </Show>
114	            </div>
115	            <div class="right-column">
116	                <OnlineSidebar
117	                    room=room_label
118	                    role=role
119	                    view_signal=view_signal
120	                    rules=rules
121	                    conn=conn
122	                    handle=handle_for_sidebar
123	                />
124	                <ChatPanel
125	                    role=role.into()
126	                    log=chat.into()
127	                    on_send=on_chat_send
128	                />
129	            </div>
130	        </section>
131	    }
132	}
133	
134	#[component]
135	fn ConnPlaceholder(room: String, conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
136	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
137	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting…".to_string(),
138	        ConnState::Open => "Waiting for server greeting…".to_string(),
139	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected — refresh to reconnect.".to_string(),
140	        ConnState::Error => "Connection error — is the server running?".to_string(),
141	    };
142	    view! {
143	        <div class="conn-placeholder">
144	            <p class="muted">{format!("Room: {}", room)}</p>
145	            <p>{label}</p>
146	        </div>
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	#[component]
151	fn BoardWrapper(
152	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
153	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
154	    handle: WsHandle,
155	) -> impl IntoView {
156	    let view = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get().expect("guarded by Show"));
157	    let resolved_role = role.get_untracked().expect("guarded by Show");
158	    let obs = resolved_role.observer();
159	    let shape = view.with_untracked(|v| v.shape);
160	
161	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
162	    // Spectators see-only — no click handlers on the board for them.
163	    let is_spectator = resolved_role.is_spectator();
164	
165	    // Surface engine chain_lock as the highlighted "selected" piece, so
166	    // legal-target dots render around it during chain mode.
167	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
168	        let v = view.get();
169	        v.chain_lock.or_else(|| selected.get())
170	    });
171	
172	    let click_handle = handle.clone();
173	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
174	        if is_spectator {
175	            return;
176	        }
177	        let v = view.get();
178	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
179	            return;
180	        }
181	
182	        // Chain mode: only the locked piece may move (captures only).
183	        // Clicking the locked piece itself releases the chain.
184	        if let Some(locked) = v.chain_lock {
185	            if sq == locked {
186	                if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
187	                    click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
188	                }
189	                selected.set(None);
190	                return;
191	            }
192	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, locked, sq) {
193	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
194	                selected.set(None);
195	                return;
196	            }
197	            return;
198	        }
199	
200	        let cur = selected.get();
201	        if cur == Some(sq) {
202	            selected.set(None);
203	            return;
204	        }
205	        if let Some(from) = cur {
206	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
207	                click_handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
208	                selected.set(None);
209	                return;
210	            }
211	        }
212	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
213	        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))
214	            && v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): selection ring above pieces, captured strip below board

Two related polish fixes for the just-shipped graveyard panel:

* Move selection + chain-lock rings above the pieces layer in board.rs
  so they're not hidden behind a piece's tile-disc — fixes the report
  of "available-path leftover marks" that were really just orphan
  legal-target dots paired with an invisible selection ring.
* Repurpose `CapturedPanel` → `CapturedStrip` rendered directly under
  the board (Black dead row first / Red dead row second), with each
  piece a small disc-icon mirroring the board style. Hidden until the
  first capture so opening positions stay clean. Drop the duplicate
  sidebar block in both Sidebar and OnlineSidebar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 11:46:37Z)**_

Committed. Two commits on `main` ahead of `origin/main`:

- `b1539dd` — original captured-pieces panel work (TUI sidebar + initial web sidebar block)
- `8920764` — selection ring promoted above pieces (fixes the orphan-dot look) + web panel relocated to a horizontal strip directly below the board, Black row above Red row, each piece as a small disc-icon mirroring the board style. Hidden until the first capture.

For the **TUI wrap behaviour** you flagged as "可能也還行吧" — leaving as-is for now; if it gets noisy in long games we can switch the row to two-up wrap or limit display to the most-recent N pieces.

```text
64	
65	fn gen_for_face_up_piece(
66	    state: &GameState,
67	    from: Square,
68	    piece: Piece,
69	    house: HouseRules,
70	    out: &mut MoveList,
71	) {
72	    let board = &state.board;
73	
74	    // 1-step orthogonal (and the 連吃 chains it seeds).
75	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
76	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
77	        match board.get(to) {
78	            None => {
79	                // Empty: 1-step slide.
80	                out.push(Move::Step { from, to });
81	            }
82	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
83	                // Face-down piece — blocks unless DARK_CAPTURE is on.
84	                if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
85	                    out.push(Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: None, attacker: None });
86	                }
87	            }
88	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == piece.side => {
89	                // Own piece: blocked.
90	            }
91	            Some(target) => {
92	                if can_capture(piece.kind, target.piece.kind) {
93	                    out.push(Move::Capture { from, to, captured: target.piece });
94	                    if house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
95	                        gen_chain_extensions(board, piece, from, to, dir, target.piece, out);
96	                    }
97	                }
98	            }
99	        }
100	    }
101	
102	    // 馬斜: horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures ignore rank.
103	    if piece.kind == PieceKind::Horse && house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL) {
104	        gen_horse_diagonal(board, from, piece, house, out);
105	    }
106	
107	    // Chariot rush replaces the chariot's 1-step capture with a ray.
108	    // We've already emitted the 1-step Step/Capture above; the ray emits
109	    // additional sliding moves. The 1-step capture is still in the list
110	    // (a strict subset of CHARIOT_RUSH's possibilities).
111	    if piece.kind == PieceKind::Chariot && house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH) {
112	        gen_chariot_rush(board, from, piece.side, house, out);
113	    }
114	
115	    // Cannon: jump-over-screen captures (always, this is base banqi).
116	    if piece.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
117	        gen_cannon_jumps(board, from, piece.side, out);
118	    }
119	}
120	
121	/// 馬斜 — horse adds diagonal one-step moves alongside the orthogonal
122	/// rule. Diagonal captures ignore rank (any piece). Hidden diagonal
123	/// targets become DarkCapture when the dark-capture flag is on.
124	fn gen_horse_diagonal(
125	    board: &Board,
126	    from: Square,
127	    piece: Piece,
128	    house: HouseRules,
129	    out: &mut MoveList,
130	) {
131	    for &dir in &Direction::DIAGONAL {
132	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
133	        match board.get(to) {
134	            None => out.push(Move::Step { from, to }),
135	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
136	                if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
137	                    out.push(Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: None, attacker: None });
138	                }
139	            }
140	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == piece.side => {
141	                // Own piece: blocked.
142	            }
143	            Some(target) => {
144	                // Any-piece diagonal capture (rank ignored).
145	                out.push(Move::Capture { from, to, captured: target.piece });
146	            }
147	        }
148	    }
149	}
150	
151	fn gen_chariot_rush(
152	    board: &Board,
153	    from: Square,
154	    side: Side,
155	    house: HouseRules,
156	    out: &mut MoveList,
157	) {
158	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
159	        let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
160	        // Steps 1..N are non-capturing slides. Step 1 was already emitted
161	        // by the base rule, but it's harmless to push duplicates — `make_move`
162	        // is idempotent w.r.t. matching moves and the legal-moves consumer
163	        // dedups via SmallVec ordering. Actually let's avoid duplicates:
164	        for sq in walked.iter().skip(1) {
165	            out.push(Move::Step { from, to: *sq });
166	        }
167	        if let Some(target_sq) = blocker {
168	            if let Some(pos) = board.get(target_sq) {
169	                if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != side {
170	                    // Multi-square capture (1-step capture already emitted by base rule).
171	                    // Rank IGNORED: with a gap, the chariot may capture any piece.
172	                    if !walked.is_empty() {
173	                        out.push(Move::Capture { from, to: target_sq, captured: pos.piece });
174	                    }
175	                } else if !pos.revealed
176	                    && !walked.is_empty()
177	                    && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE)
178	                {
179	                    // 車衝暗吃: chariot rush onto a face-down blocker via gap.
180	                    out.push(Move::DarkCapture {
181	                        from,
182	                        to: target_sq,
183	                        revealed: None,
184	                        attacker: None,
185	                    });
186	                }
187	            }
188	        }
189	    }
190	}
191	
192	fn gen_cannon_jumps(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
193	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
194	        // Find the screen (first occupied square in this direction).
195	        let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
196	        let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
197	
198	        // Continue past the screen looking for a target.
199	        let mut cursor = screen_sq;
200	        while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
201	            match board.get(next) {
202	                None => {
203	                    cursor = next;
204	                }
205	                Some(pos) => {
206	                    // Cannon captures any face-up enemy regardless of rank.
207	                    if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != side {
208	                        out.push(Move::CannonJump {
209	                            from,
210	                            to: next,
211	                            screen: screen_sq,
212	                            captured: pos.piece,
213	                        });
214	                    }
215	                    break;
216	                }
217	            }
218	        }
219	    }
220	}
221	
222	/// Extend a 1-step capture into chains of length 2, 3, … along the same
223	/// direction. Each emitted ChainCapture has `from = seed_from` and a path
224	/// starting with the seed hop, plus one or more additional captures.
225	fn gen_chain_extensions(
226	    board: &Board,
227	    moving: Piece,
228	    seed_from: Square,
229	    seed_to: Square,
230	    dir: Direction,
231	    seed_captured: Piece,
232	    out: &mut MoveList,
233	) {
234	    let mut path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> = SmallVec::new();
235	    path.push(ChainHop { to: seed_to, captured: seed_captured });
236	
237	    extend_recursive(board, moving, seed_from, dir, seed_to, &mut path, out);
238	}
239	
240	fn extend_recursive(
241	    board: &Board,
242	    moving: Piece,
243	    origin: Square,
244	    dir: Direction,
245	    cursor: Square,
246	    path: &mut SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]>,
247	    out: &mut MoveList,
248	) {
249	    let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) else { return };
250	    let Some(target) = board.get(next) else { return };
251	    if !target.revealed {
252	        // Chains stop at face-down tiles in this round. Chains-with-dark-hops
253	        // (true 暗連) is deferred — see plan's Phase 2 backlog.
254	        return;
255	    }
256	    if target.piece.side == moving.side {
257	        return; // own piece blocks
258	    }
259	    if !can_capture(moving.kind, target.piece.kind) {
260	        return; // outranked
261	    }
262	    path.push(ChainHop { to: next, captured: target.piece });
263	    out.push(Move::ChainCapture { from: origin, path: path.clone() });
264	    extend_recursive(board, moving, origin, dir, next, path, out);
265	    path.pop();
266	}
267	
268	// ---- Capture rank logic ------------------------------------------------------
269	
270	/// Whether `attacker` may capture `defender` under standard banqi rank rules.
271	/// Cannons are not handled here — they capture via `gen_cannon_jumps`.
272	pub fn can_capture(attacker: PieceKind, defender: PieceKind) -> bool {
273	    use PieceKind::*;
274	    match (attacker, defender) {
275	        // Cannon's only legal capture is the jump; not via this path.
276	        (Cannon, _) => false,
277	        // Soldier-beats-General special case.
278	        (Soldier, General) => true,
279	        (General, Soldier) => false,
280	        // Otherwise: outrank the defender (≥).
281	        (a, d) => a.banqi_rank() >= d.banqi_rank(),
282	    }
283	}
284

286	mod tests {
287	    use super::*;
288	    use crate::piece::PieceOnSquare;
289	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
```

```text
231	    on_click: Callback<Square>,
232	) -> View {

235	        for col in 0..cols {
236	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };

241	                    <rect

243	                        x=x y=y width=CELL height=CELL
244	                        on:click=move |_| on_click.call(sq)
245	                    />
246	                }
247	                .into_view(),
248	            );
249	        }
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:01:12Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:01:13Z)**_

Confirmed engine bugs: 

1. `gen_horse_diagonal` line 134 emits diagonal **Step** — user wants diagonal moves only when capturing.
2. `gen_cannon_jumps` doesn't emit `DarkCapture` for jumping over a screen onto a hidden tile.
3. `dark_capture_outcome` runs `can_capture(Cannon, _)` which is always `false` — so even if cannon's dark-capture were emitted, it would always probe instead of capturing.
4. Cannon also shouldn't emit DarkCapture for 1-step adjacent (banqi cannon captures only via jump).

Let me fix these and add tests, then improve selection visibility.

```text
547	    ///
548	    /// Powers the sidebar "graveyard" panel in both clients. Returns
549	    /// each captured piece's full identity — safe under ADR-0004
550	    /// because every captured piece was revealed at capture time.
551	    /// `Move::DarkCapture` outcomes are recomputed (Probe contributes
552	    /// nothing, Trade contributes the dead attacker, Capture contributes
553	    /// the revealed defender), matching `unmake_move`'s recomputation.
554	    pub fn captured_pieces(&self) -> Vec<Piece> {
555	        let mut out = Vec::new();
556	        for record in &self.history {
557	            match &record.the_move {
558	                Move::Capture { captured, .. } | Move::CannonJump { captured, .. } => {
559	                    out.push(*captured);
560	                }
561	                Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => {
562	                    out.extend(path.iter().map(|hop| hop.captured));
563	                }
564	                Move::DarkCapture { revealed, attacker, .. } => {
565	                    let (Some(rev), Some(att)) = (*revealed, *attacker) else { continue };
566	                    match dark_capture_outcome(att, rev, self.rules.house) {
567	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => out.push(rev),
568	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => out.push(att),
569	                        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe => {}
570	                    }
571	                }
572	                _ => {}
573	            }
574	        }
575	        out
576	    }
577	}
578	
579	/// Outcome of a 暗吃 (`Move::DarkCapture`) once the target is revealed.
580	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
581	pub(crate) enum DarkCaptureOutcome {
582	    /// Rank check passes — attacker takes target's square (normal capture).
583	    Capture,
584	    /// Rank check fails AND `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` is set — attacker is
585	    /// removed; defender stays revealed at `to`.
586	    Trade,
587	    /// Rank check fails, no trade flag — both pieces stay; target is
588	    /// now revealed (information probe; turn advances normally).
589	    Probe,
590	}
```

```text
591	
592	/// Decide the outcome of a dark-capture given the involved pieces and
593	/// the active house rules. Used by both apply and unapply (recomputed
594	/// rather than stored in the move record).
595	pub(crate) fn dark_capture_outcome(
596	    attacker: Piece,
597	    revealed: Piece,
598	    house: crate::rules::HouseRules,
599	) -> DarkCaptureOutcome {
600	    if attacker.side == revealed.side {
601	        // Same side → never a capture; treat as probe (own piece blocks).
602	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe;
603	    }
604	    if crate::rules::banqi::can_capture(attacker.kind, revealed.kind) {
605	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture
606	    } else if house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
607	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade
608	    } else {
609	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe
610	    }
611	}
```

```text
373	    fn unapply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
374	        match m {
375	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => {
376	                let mut pos =
377	                    self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Reveal: empty"))?;
378	                pos.revealed = false;
379	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
380	                // First-flip might have set side_assignment; if this was the first move, clear it.
381	                if self.history.is_empty() {
382	                    self.side_assignment = None;
383	                }
384	            }
385	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
386	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Step: empty to"))?;
387	                self.board.set(*to, None);
388	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
389	            }
390	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
391	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Capture: empty to"))?;
392	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
393	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
394	            }
395	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
396	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Jump: empty to"))?;
397	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
398	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
399	            }
400	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
401	                let last_to = path.last().unwrap().to;
402	                let p =
403	                    self.board.get(last_to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Chain: empty last"))?;
404	                self.board.set(last_to, None);
405	                // Restore intermediate captures (in any order).
406	                for hop in path {
407	                    self.board.set(hop.to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(hop.captured)));
408	                }
409	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
410	            }
411	            Move::EndChain { .. } => {
412	                // No board change to undo. chain_lock is restored by
413	                // `unmake_move` from `MoveRecord.chain_lock_before`.
414	            }
415	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
416	                let revealed_piece =
417	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
418	                let attacker_piece =
419	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
420	                let outcome =
421	                    dark_capture_outcome(attacker_piece, revealed_piece, self.rules.house);
422	                // Restore attacker at `from` (reveals it as it was).
423	                match outcome {
424	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
```

```text
630	    house: crate::rules::HouseRules,
631	    from: crate::coord::Square,
632	    to: crate::coord::Square,
633	    board: &crate::board::Board,
634	) -> DarkCaptureOutcome {
635	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
636	    if attacker.side == revealed.side {
637	        // Same side → never a capture; treat as probe (own piece blocks).
638	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe;
639	    }
640	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
641	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture;
642	    }
643	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Horse
644	        && house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL)
645	        && is_diagonal_step(board, from, to)
646	    {
647	        return DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture;
648	    }
649	    if crate::rules::banqi::can_capture(attacker.kind, revealed.kind) {
650	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture
651	    } else if house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
652	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade
653	    } else {
654	        DarkCaptureOutcome::Probe
655	    }
656	}
657	
658	fn is_diagonal_step(
659	    board: &crate::board::Board,
660	    from: crate::coord::Square,
661	    to: crate::coord::Square,
662	) -> bool {
663	    crate::coord::Direction::DIAGONAL.iter().any(|&d| board.step(from, d) == Some(to))
664	}
665	
666	/// Whether `attacker` standing on `from` has at least one further legal
667	/// capture in any direction. Used to decide whether banqi 連吃 chain
668	/// mode should remain active after a capture (see
669	/// `compute_chain_lock_after`). Only consults banqi rules — caller
670	/// guarantees variant + CHAIN_CAPTURE flag.
671	fn has_capture_from(state: &GameState, from: crate::coord::Square, attacker: Piece) -> bool {
672	    use crate::coord::Direction;
673	    use crate::piece::PieceKind;
674	    use crate::rules::{banqi::can_capture, HouseRules};
675	
676	    let house = state.rules.house;
677	    let board = &state.board;
678	
679	    // Cannon: only via jump-over-screen.
680	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Cannon {
681	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
682	            let (_walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
683	            let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
684	            let mut cursor = screen_sq;
685	            while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
686	                match board.get(next) {
687	                    None => cursor = next,
688	                    Some(pos) => {
689	                        if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
690	                            return true;
691	                        }
692	                        break;
693	                    }
694	                }
695	            }
696	        }
697	        return false;
698	    }
699	
700	    // Orthogonal one-step (and chariot rush extension if enabled).
701	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
702	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
703	        match board.get(to) {
704	            None => {}
705	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
706	                if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
707	                    return true;
708	                }
709	            }
710	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
711	            Some(target) => {
712	                if can_capture(attacker.kind, target.piece.kind) {
713	                    return true;
714	                }
715	            }
716	        }
717	    }
718	
719	    // Chariot rush — multi-square ray; with a gap, captures any piece.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
719	    // Chariot rush — multi-square ray; with a gap, captures any piece.
720	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Chariot && house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH) {
721	        for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
722	            let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
723	            if walked.is_empty() {
724	                // Rank-bound 1-step capture is the base-rule branch above.
725	                continue;
726	            }
727	            let Some(target_sq) = blocker else { continue };
728	            let Some(pos) = board.get(target_sq) else { continue };
729	            if pos.revealed && pos.piece.side != attacker.side {
730	                return true;
731	            }
732	            if !pos.revealed && house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
733	                return true;
734	            }
735	        }
736	    }
737	
738	    // 馬斜 — diagonal one-step, captures any piece.
739	    if attacker.kind == PieceKind::Horse && house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL) {
740	        for &dir in &Direction::DIAGONAL {
741	            let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
742	            match board.get(to) {
743	                None => {}
744	                Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
745	                    if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) {
746	                        return true;
747	                    }
748	                }
749	                Some(target) if target.piece.side == attacker.side => {}
750	                Some(_) => return true,
751	            }
752	        }
753	    }
754	
755	    false
756	}
757	
758	fn banqi_side_assignment(flipper: Side, revealed_side: Side) -> SideAssignment {
759	    let mut mapping = SmallVec::new();
760	    if flipper == Side::RED {
761	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
762	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
763	    } else {
764	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
765	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
766	    }
767	    SideAssignment { mapping }
768	}
769	
770	/// Find the (single) piece matching predicate. Used to locate a general.
771	pub(crate) fn find_piece<F>(board: &Board, pred: F) -> Option<crate::coord::Square>
772	where
773	    F: Fn(Piece) -> bool,
774	{
775	    for sq in board.squares() {
776	        if let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) {
777	            if pos.revealed && pred(pos.piece) {
778	                return Some(sq);
779	            }
780	        }
781	    }
782	    None
783	}
```

```text
1	//! 馬斜 (HORSE_DIAGONAL): horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal
2	//! captures ignore rank.
3	
4	use chess_core::board::Board;
5	use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
6	use chess_core::moves::Move;
7	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
8	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
9	use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
10	use smallvec::smallvec;
11	
12	fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
13	    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
14	    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
15	    for sq in squares {
16	        state.board.set(sq, None);
17	    }
18	    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
19	    state
20	}
21	
22	fn place_revealed(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
23	    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
24	}
25	
26	#[test]
27	fn horse_emits_diagonal_steps_only_with_flag() {
28	    // Without HORSE_DIAGONAL: horse at (1,1) sees only 4 orthogonal Steps.
29	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
30	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
31	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
32	    let steps_no_flag = state
33	        .legal_moves()
34	        .into_iter()
35	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, .. } if *from == h))
36	        .count();
37	    assert_eq!(steps_no_flag, 4);
38	
39	    // With HORSE_DIAGONAL: 4 ortho + 4 diag = 8 Steps.
40	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
41	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
42	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
43	    let steps_with_flag = state
44	        .legal_moves()
45	        .into_iter()
46	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, .. } if *from == h))
47	        .count();
48	    assert_eq!(steps_with_flag, 8, "horse with diagonal should have 8 step moves");
49	}
50	
51	#[test]
52	fn horse_diagonal_captures_higher_rank() {
53	    // Horse rank = 2, General rank = 6. Without rank-ignore, horse cannot
54	    // capture General. With HORSE_DIAGONAL on a diagonal target, it can.
55	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
56	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
57	    let g_sq = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
58	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
59	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, g_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General);
60	
61	    let captures: Vec<_> = state
62	        .legal_moves()
63	        .into_iter()
64	        .filter_map(|m| match m {
65	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } if from == h && to == g_sq => Some(captured),
66	            _ => None,
67	        })
68	        .collect();
69	    assert!(
70	        !captures.is_empty(),
71	        "馬斜 should let horse capture diagonally adjacent general (rank ignored)"
72	    );
73	    assert_eq!(captures[0].kind, PieceKind::General);
74	}
75	
76	#[test]
77	fn horse_orthogonal_captures_still_follow_rank_rules() {
78	    // Without HORSE_DIAGONAL, horse vs General orthogonal: horse(2) < general(6),
79	    // so no capture. Confirm we didn't accidentally rank-ignore orthogonal too.
80	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
81	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
82	    let g_sq = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
83	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
84	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, g_sq, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General);
85	
86	    let captures = state
87	        .legal_moves()
88	        .into_iter()
89	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == g_sq))
90	        .count();
91	    assert_eq!(captures, 0, "orthogonal horse-vs-general should still be rank-blocked");
92	}
93	
94	#[test]
95	fn horse_diagonal_blocked_by_own_piece() {
96	    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, 0));
97	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
98	    let mate = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
99	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
100	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, mate, Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier);
101	
102	    let move_to_mate = state
103	        .legal_moves()
104	        .into_iter()
105	        .filter(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { from, to } | Move::Capture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == mate))
106	        .count();
107	    assert_eq!(move_to_mate, 0, "own piece blocks diagonal");
108	}
109	
110	#[test]
111	fn horse_diagonal_dark_capture_emitted_with_both_flags() {
112	    let rules = RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL | HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, 0);
113	    let mut state = empty_banqi(rules);
114	    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
115	    let target = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
116	    place_revealed(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
117	    state
118	        .board
119	        .set(target, Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
120	
121	    let dark = state
122	        .legal_moves()
123	        .into_iter()
124	        .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } if *from == h && *to == target));
125	    assert!(dark.is_some(), "diagonal hidden target should produce DarkCapture");
126	}
127
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:09:34Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit fixes

```bash
git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(banqi): cannon-jump dark-capture + horse-diagonal capture-only

Two engine bugs caught while play-testing the new rules:

* **Cannon dark-capture had no jump path.** `gen_cannon_jumps` only
  emitted CannonJump for revealed enemies past a screen — hidden
  targets past the screen produced no move at all. Worse, the only
  cannon DarkCapture path that DID exist was the orthogonal 1-step
  arm, which doesn't match standard banqi (cannons capture only via
  jump) AND fed a `dark_capture_outcome` that always returned Probe
  for cannons (rank check `can_capture(Cannon, _)` is hard-coded
  false). Net effect: a cannon could never actually capture a
  face-down piece, even via what looked like a legal jump. Fix:
  - skip cannon emission of 1-step DarkCapture in `gen_for_face_up_piece`
  - emit DarkCapture from `gen_cannon_jumps` for jump-onto-hidden
  - special-case `attacker.kind == Cannon` in `dark_capture_outcome`
    so the cannon-jump dark-capture always resolves to Capture
    (consistent with the standard rule that cannon jumps ignore rank)

* **Horse-diagonal allowed non-capturing diagonal moves.** User-facing
  rule is "馬斜 — diagonal *only* when capturing"; the previous gen
  emitted a diagonal `Move::Step` onto empty squares. Drop that arm
  so 馬斜 is purely an attack augmentation. The dark-capture path
  also now bypasses rank for diagonal hits (mirrors the
  already-correct `gen_horse_diagonal` Capture arm).

Threading: `dark_capture_outcome` now takes `from`/`to`/`board` so it
can detect diagonal displacement; all four callsites updated.
`has_capture_from` (chain-mode lookahead) gains the matching cannon
hidden-target branch so 炮連跳暗吃 can chain into another capture.

Tests (chess-core/tests/banqi_dark_capture.rs and
…banqi_horse_diagonal.rs) cover: cannon-no-adjacent-DarkCapture,
cannon-jump-emits-DarkCapture, cannon-jump-bypasses-rank,
cannon-roundtrip, horse-no-diagonal-step, horse-diagonal-bypass,
horse-orthogonal-still-rank-bound.

Also boosts the web `.selection` ring to a brighter orange + soft
glow + faint fill so the highlighted square is unmistakable against
the beige board (the previous accent ring was hard to see, which
was being read as "phantom legal-move dots").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

```text
[main 4e19b61] fix(banqi): cannon-jump dark-capture + horse-diagonal capture-only
 5 files changed, 276 insertions(+), 49 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:09:49Z)**_

Committed `4e19b61`. Summary of what's now on `main` (3 commits ahead of `origin/main`):

**Engine corrections** (`4e19b61`)
- **Cannon dark-capture**: emits in `gen_cannon_jumps` (jump-over-screen onto a hidden tile) and bypasses rank in `dark_capture_outcome` — fixes "炮暗吃 翻開的馬沒成功".
- **Cannon adjacent**: 1-step `DarkCapture` no longer emitted for cannons — they capture only via jump.
- **Horse-diagonal**: removed the diagonal `Move::Step` emission so 馬斜 is capture-only; the diagonal dark-capture also bypasses rank (orthogonal horse dark-capture still rank-bound).
- **Chain-mode lookahead**: `has_capture_from` now sees the cannon hidden-target case so 炮連跳暗吃 can chain.
- **9 new tests** covering each path: `cannon_does_not_emit_adjacent_dark_capture`, `cannon_jump_emits_dark_capture_against_hidden_target`, `cannon_dark_capture_via_jump_always_captures_regardless_of_rank`, `cannon_dark_capture_via_jump_round_trips`, `horse_diagonal_does_not_emit_non_capturing_diagonal_steps`, `horse_diagonal_dark_capture_against_higher_rank_succeeds`, `horse_orthogonal_dark_capture_still_obeys_rank` (plus existing 7).

**Selection visibility** (same commit): brighter orange ring + soft glow + faint translucent fill on `.selection` — should be unmistakable against the beige board now. If you reload the dev server, the "phantom dots" effect should disappear because the actual highlighted square is now clearly highlighted (and if you genuinely see green dots without any highlight, that's a real bug to follow up on).

For the **TUI wrap on long graveyard rows** — leaving as-is per your "可能也還行吧"; can revisit if it gets noisy.

---

 `./clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs`

```text
50	
51	#[component]
52	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool) -> impl IntoView {
53	    let initial_rules = rules.clone();
54	    let state = create_rw_signal(GameState::new(rules));
55	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
56	
57	    // Local pass-and-play renders from a fixed Red-side observer (board never
58	    // flips on turn change). Legal moves come from the projection for the
59	    // *current* side-to-move so click-to-move uses the right list.
60	    let observer = Side::RED;
61	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
62	
63	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
64	
65	    // The chain-locked piece (if any) renders with the same affordance as
66	    // a manually-selected piece, so the user sees it visually highlighted.
67	    let chain_locked_signal: Signal<Option<Square>> =
68	        Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.chain_lock));
69	
70	    let effective_selected: Signal<Option<Square>> = Signal::derive(move || {
71	        // In chain mode the engine dictates which piece moves; surface
72	        // chain_lock as the selected square so the legal-target dots
73	        // render around it.
74	        chain_locked_signal.get().or_else(|| selected.get())
75	    });
76	
77	    let on_end_chain: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
78	        if wip {
79	            return;
80	        }
81	        let v = view.get();
82	        if let Some(mv) = end_chain_move(&v) {
83	            state.update(|s| {
84	                s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal end-chain");
85	                s.refresh_status();
86	            });
87	            selected.set(None);
88	        }
89	    });
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
198	
199	fn move_dots_view(
200	    view: &PlayerView,
201	    selected: Option<Square>,
202	    observer: Side,
203	    shape: BoardShape,
204	) -> View {
205	    let Some(from) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
206	    let targets = legal_targets(view, from);
207	    if targets.is_empty() {
208	        return ().into_view();
209	    }
210	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
211	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
212	    for row in 0..rows {
213	        for col in 0..cols {
214	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
215	            if targets.contains(&sq) {
216	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
217	                // Distinct dot for "move to my own square" (banqi reveal) — same
218	                // visual treatment for now; refined in commit 3.
219	                out.push(view! { <circle class="move-dot" cx=cx cy=cy r="9"/> }.into_view());
220	            }
221	        }
```

```bash
fix(chess-web): clear selection on side-switch, ring capturable targets

* When the seat-to-move flips (e.g. last hop of a 連吃 chain ends the
  player's turn), explicitly reset `selected` via a `create_effect`
  watcher in both `LocalGame` and `BoardWrapper`. Catches the residual
  green-dots case the user reported after a 士 chain ended and the
  turn switched to Black — the previous side's `selected` was kept by
  some path I couldn't trace from the click handler alone, so a
  side-flip safety net is the durable fix.

* In `move_dots_view`, distinguish target types: empty squares get the
  existing small dot, capturable squares (revealed enemy OR face-down
  tile under DARK_CAPTURE) get a larger green ring around the piece.
  Mirrors what the chess-tui sidebar already shows — players can now
  see at a glance which enemies / hidden tiles a selected piece can
  take, not just where it can slide to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:18:10Z)**_

Committed `a7b9d4a`. Now 4 commits ahead of `origin/main`:

- `b1539dd` — captured-pieces panel (TUI sidebar + initial web sidebar block)
- `8920764` — selection ring above pieces, web panel moved below board
- `4e19b61` — banqi engine fixes (cannon dark-capture-via-jump, horse-diagonal capture-only) + 9 new tests, brighter selection ring
- `a7b9d4a` — clear selection on side-switch + ring around capturable targets

What this turn delivered:

1. **Residual green-dot fix**: defensive `create_effect` watching `view.side_to_move` — whenever the seat flips it clears `selected`. Survives any click path that doesn't reset on its own (including the chain-mode tail you described).
2. **Capturable square highlight**: empty targets keep the small dot; revealed-enemy and hidden tiles (under `DARK_CAPTURE`) now get a larger green ring framing the piece. Mirrors what the TUI was already showing in image #16.

Tell me if a fresh dev-server reload still shows the residual dots — if so, the bug is somewhere I haven't found yet and the side-switch effect should at least mask it.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:24:53Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:24:53Z)**_

Got it — found the bug. `legal_targets` panics in chain mode because it doesn't handle `Move::EndChain` (which is in `legal_moves` when `chain_lock` is set, has `origin_square = chain_lock` and `to_square = None`, falling into a `unreachable!()` arm). The closure panic prevents leptos from updating the DOM, so the previous chain step's dots stay frozen.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:24:54Z)**_

```text
62	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render dots).
63	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
64	    view.legal_moves
65	        .iter()
66	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
67	        .map(|m| match m.to_square() {
68	            Some(t) => t,
69	            None => match m {
70	                Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
71	                _ => unreachable!("Move::to_square() returned None for a non-Reveal move"),
72	            },
73	        })
74	        .collect()
75	}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit chain-mode panic fix

```bash
git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess add clients/chess-web/src/state.rs && git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): legal_targets must skip Move::EndChain (chain-mode panic)

Root cause of the "stale move-dots after a chain capture" symptom the
user kept hitting: `legal_targets` iterates `view.legal_moves` and
maps each move's `to_square()`. In banqi 連吃 chain mode the engine
adds `Move::EndChain { at: chain_lock }` to `legal_moves` —
`origin_square == chain_lock` so it passes the from-filter, but
`to_square` returns `None` and EndChain wasn't a `Reveal`, dropping
into an `unreachable!()` arm.

The panic happened inside the leptos closure that builds the dot
layer, so the reactive system never got to update that subtree —
the previous chain step's dots/rings stayed frozen on screen, even
after the chain ended and the side flipped (image 17 + image 18 are
both the same frozen render). Switching to `filter_map` and skipping
EndChain (it's the explicit "release the lock" gesture, not a
movement target) keeps the closure clean and the DOM live.

Test (`state::tests::legal_targets_in_chain_mode_excludes_end_chain_and_does_not_panic`)
constructs a real chain-mode position, projects the view, asserts
EndChain is in `legal_moves`, then runs `legal_targets` — without
the fix this was a panic; with the fix it returns just the next-hop
capture target. Locks the regression in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

```text
[main 16f2bdd] fix(chess-web): legal_targets must skip Move::EndChain (chain-mode panic)
 1 file changed, 64 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:27:24Z)**_

Committed `16f2bdd` — the actual root cause of every "phantom dots" report you've hit:

`legal_targets` was panicking on `Move::EndChain` (chain-mode terminator). `EndChain.to_square()` returns `None` and it's not a `Reveal`, so it fell into `unreachable!()`. Because the panic happened inside leptos's reactive closure that builds the dot layer, the DOM subtree just stopped updating from then on — leaving the *previous* chain step's markers frozen across captures and across the side switch you saw in images 17 and 18.

Fix: `filter_map` the legal-moves list and skip `EndChain` explicitly (it's the explicit "release the lock" gesture triggered by clicking the locked piece, not a movement target).

Test added: `state::tests::legal_targets_in_chain_mode_excludes_end_chain_and_does_not_panic` constructs a real chain-mode position and asserts `legal_targets` returns the next-hop capture without panicking.

Now 5 commits ahead of `origin/main`. After a fresh reload, the markers should refresh on every chain step and clear when the side switches. If they don't, that's a different bug — let me know.

---

```text
465	    observer: Side,

473	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Board ");
474	    let inner = block.inner(area);
475	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
476	
477	    let shape = view.shape;
478	    let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
479	    let model_w = shape.dimensions().0;
480	
481	    // Build legal-target highlight set when a piece is selected.
482	    let highlight: std::collections::HashSet<Square> = match selected {
483	        Some(from) => view

497	
498	    let river_after = if matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) { Some(4u8) } else { None };
499	
500	    for display_row in 0..rows {
501	        lines.push(rank_row(
502	            view,
503	            observer,
504	            shape,
505	            display_row,
506	            cols,
507	            model_w,
508	            &highlight,
509	            cursor,
510	            selected,
511	            style,
512	            use_color,
513	            border_style,
514	        ));
515	
516	        // Between row (skip after last rank)
517	        if display_row + 1 == rows {
518	            // no between row after the final rank
519	        } else if river_after == Some(display_row) {
520	            lines.push(river_line(cols, style));
521	        } else {
522	            lines.push(between_row(observer, shape, display_row, cols, style, border_style));
523	        }
524	    }
525	
526	    // File header (bottom)
527	    lines.push(file_header_line(observer, shape));
528	
529	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines);
530	    frame.render_widget(para, inner);
531	
532	    *board_rect = Some(RectPx {
533	        x: inner.x,
534	        y: inner.y,
535	        cell_cols: CELL_COLS,
536	        cell_rows: CELL_ROWS,
537	        // file header (1)
538	        top_pad: 1,
539	        // rank label (3)
```

```text
600	/// pieces (which use a 2-col glyph); false for empty intersections (1 col).
601	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
602	fn intersection_or_piece(
603	    view: &PlayerView,
604	    sq: Square,
605	    model_w: u8,
606	    shape: BoardShape,
607	    highlight: &std::collections::HashSet<Square>,
608	    cursor: (u8, u8),
609	    selected: Option<Square>,
610	    style: Style,
611	    use_color: bool,
612	    display_row: u8,
613	    display_col: u8,
614	) -> (String, TuiStyle, bool) {
615	    let cell = &view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
616	    let is_cursor = (display_row, display_col) == cursor;
617	    let is_selected = selected == Some(sq);
618	    let is_target = highlight.contains(&sq);
619	
620	    let (text, is_glyph_w2) = match cell {
621	        VisibleCell::Empty => {
622	            let palace_center = is_palace_center(sq, model_w, shape);
623	            (intersection_glyph(palace_center, style).to_string(), false)
624	        }
625	        VisibleCell::Hidden => (glyph::hidden(style).to_string(), true),
626	        VisibleCell::Revealed(pos) => {
627	            (glyph::glyph(pos.piece.kind, pos.piece.side, style).to_string(), true)
628	        }
629	    };
630	
631	    let mut s = TuiStyle::default();
632	    if use_color {
633	        if let VisibleCell::Revealed(pos) = cell {
634	            s = s.fg(side_color(pos.piece.side));
635	            if !pos.revealed {
636	                s = s.add_modifier(Modifier::DIM);
637	            }
638	            if pos.piece.kind == PieceKind::General {
639	                s = s.add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD);
640	            }
641	        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
642	            s = s.fg(Color::DarkGray);
643	        } else if is_palace_center(sq, model_w, shape) {
644	            s = s.fg(Color::Yellow);
645	        } else {
646	            s = s.fg(Color::DarkGray);
647	        }
648	    }
649	    if is_target {
650	        s = s.bg(Color::Rgb(40, 80, 40));
651	    }
652	    if is_selected {
653	        s = s.bg(Color::Rgb(80, 60, 20)).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD);
654	    }
655	    if is_cursor {
656	        s = s.bg(Color::Rgb(60, 60, 100)).add_modifier(Modifier::REVERSED);
657	    }
658	    (text, s, is_glyph_w2)
659	}
660	
661	fn is_palace_center(sq: Square, model_w: u8, shape: BoardShape) -> bool {
662	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
663	        return false;
664	    }
665	    let f = (sq.0 % model_w as u16) as u8;
666	    let r = (sq.0 / model_w as u16) as u8;
667	    f == 4 && (r == 1 || r == 8)
668	}
669	
670	fn intersection_glyph(palace_center: bool, style: Style) -> &'static str {
671	    match (palace_center, style) {
672	        (true, Style::Cjk) => "╳",
673	        (true, Style::Ascii) => "X",
674	        (false, Style::Cjk) => "┼",
675	        (false, Style::Ascii) => "+",
676	    }
677	}
678	
679	fn horizontal_connector(content_is_w2: bool, style: Style) -> &'static str {
680	    let dash: char = match style {
681	        Style::Cjk => '─',
682	        Style::Ascii => '-',
683	    };
684	    if content_is_w2 {
685	        // 2-col content (piece) leaves 2 cols for connector.
686	        match dash {
687	            '─' => "──",
688	            _ => "--",
689	        }
```

```bash
fix(chess-tui): surface 連吃 chain mode (highlight, targets, hint)

Local + Net board renderers now fold `view.chain_lock` into the
"effective selected" they pass to `draw_board`, so:

* the chain-locked piece keeps a visible highlight (no more "Selected:
  —" with nothing on the board lit up after the first chain hop)
* the legal next-hop captures show their green target backgrounds
  automatically — the user can see where the chain can continue
* the locked square uses a brighter orange-ish bg distinct from a
  user-driven brown selection, so it's obvious the engine is forcing
  this state rather than the player

Sidebars (Local + Net) gain a one-line chain banner under the
"Selected:" row when `chain_lock` is set, naming the locked square
in ICCS and reminding the user how to escape: Enter / click on the
locked piece, or Esc, both end the chain (already wired in app.rs
— this just surfaces it). No engine change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 12:36:11Z)**_

Committed `ea231fd`. Now 7 commits ahead of `origin/main`.

What changed for the TUI chain UX:

- Both `draw_game` and `draw_net` now compute `effective_selected = view.chain_lock.or(g.selected)` before calling `draw_board` — the locked piece stays highlighted between hops and the green legal-target backgrounds for the next captures show automatically.
- The chain-locked square gets a **distinct orange-ish bg** (RGB 140,80,20) vs the regular brown selection (RGB 80,60,20), so it's clear the engine is forcing this state.
- A new chain banner appears under the "Selected:" row in both sidebars: `⛓ 連吃 chain on c3` + a hint line: `Enter / click locked = end · Esc = end · target = continue`. The Esc/Enter keybindings were already wired in `app.rs`; this just surfaces them.

Re your "理論上是維持selected狀態" — exactly the fix: chain_lock now serves as the selection during chain mode, so you don't have to re-click your attacker between hops and you can always see what's locked, where you can capture next, and how to bail out.
